title: TL/Markov-trace invariant pooling readout for rmatrix braid attention (design in integrable_attention_rmatrix.md section 5)
close_reason: Causal realization of the TL/Markov-trace readout: spectral multi-view probe sweeps (--braid-rmatrix-probes K) - per query, K extra monodromy sweeps at probe rapidities theta_k = u_i + delta_k (learned offsets, stable region), gated into the output with zero-init per-(head,probe) gates; bitwise-identical to the base sweep at init (asserted rtol=0/atol=0), gates receive gradients, KV parity holds. Theory note section 5 updated with the causality analysis (global pooled traces read the future; monodromy elements at fixed spectral points are the open-word analogue; the closed-word Markov trace's quadratic shadows are exactly probe-charges' observables). Goldens recaptured: zero trajectory drift on all 11 mechanisms, only the new config key changed.

title: GaugeBlock has no residual connection: zero-init deadlock freezes training (total grad = 0.0)
description:
DISCOVERED during 7b0.1 golden capture: the fixed-seed 50-step CPU loss trajectory for attention_type=gauge is CONSTANT at ln(128) for all 50 steps. Root cause chain: (1) GaugeBlock.forward (nanochat/gauge_block_torch.py:139) returns y_local with NO residual connection - Block.forward for gauge is 'return self.special_block(...)' so the residual was expected inside the special block, and it is absent; (2) GPT.init_weights zeroes sb.c_proj and lm_head per the canonical zero-init convention; (3) therefore the trunk output after block 1 is exactly 0, logits are exactly 0, and the gradient is mutually annihilated: dL/d(lm_head) = g @ x_final^T = 0 (x_final = 0) and dL/d(trunk) flows through lm_head.weight = 0. Empirically verified: total |grad| over ALL parameters after one backward = 0.0 exactly. python -m nanochat.train --attention-type gauge cannot learn AT ALL.

FIX DIRECTION (needs its own verification, NOT part of 7b0.1 which is behavior-preserving): give GaugeBlock the standard residual structure (x + gauge_op(norm(x)) or internal x + y_local), decide whether it also needs an MLP slot (currently the gauge block has NO MLP - 'replaces the whole block including MLP' was the A1 boundary decision, but without residual OR MLP it is neither a transformer block nor trainable). Interaction: the 7b0.1 golden fixture tests/fixtures/attention_goldens/gauge.json pins the current frozen trajectory - recapture it when this lands. Relation: 7b0.5 (gauge KV-cache + GQA) touches the same file; consider doing both together.
close_reason: Fixed: GaugeBlock now carries the canonical pre-norm residual skeleton internally plus a standard MLP slot (passed in via _build_ffn, so ffn_type incl. tropical applies to gauge; the 8gk.8 gauge*ffn rejection is removed in gpt.py+train.py and its test flipped). init_weights zeroes the special block's MLP projection -> gauge starts as identity. Verified total |grad| 0.0 -> 49.8, loss decreases over 50 steps; regression test test_nanochat_gauge_training_escapes_zero_init pins the deadlock. Gauge golden recaptured (old fixture pinned the frozen trajectory; new one alive, other 10 bitwise-unchanged).

title: feat(adjudicate): ci-v2 policy - floor validity gate, per-run clustering, Welch-t CI, budget cohorts
close_reason: ci-v2 adjudication policy landed: (1) floor validity gate - REFUTED requires baseline above the answer-prior floor (recorded answer_prior preferred, registered validity.baseline_floor fallback), else INCONCLUSIVE/floor_effect (the pilot1 no-power lesson); (2) one observation per TRAINED MODEL for evaltasks (ci-v1 pooled eval seeds as iid, n=9 pseudo-replication -> n=3 honest); (3) Welch-t CI with Satterthwaite df (t~2.78 at n=3, not z=1.96); (4) budget cohorts - largest cohort with both arms at min_seeds wins, so E1 supersedes pilot1 instead of budget_mismatch-blocking; (5) lineage dedupe so re-evals of the same checkpoint never double-count. 12 new/updated tests.

title: Artifacts index & manifest
description:
- Generate an index/manifest file summarizing available artifacts (baseline, bench, perf, vis, reports) with paths, timestamps, config hashes, and brief metric highlights.
- Update after each run or via a manual command; keep in artifacts/index.json(+md).
- Helps discover latest baselines/benchmarks for dashboards/regression checks.

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

EXPANDED SCOPE - mgr status (agent ergonomics): beyond the static index, add `mgr status [--json]` - one command answering "what exists and what is stale" for humans AND agents: latest baseline/bench/cert/scorecard/scaling artifacts with ages, run_ids, and config hashes; which mechanisms have CURRENT certificates (and which certs predate the latest code change to their mechanism file - staleness by mtime comparison); which registered hypotheses (hij.1) are adjudicable with existing artifacts vs BLOCKED; pending-regression summary vs the latest baseline. Rich panel output + --json schema for agent consumption. RATIONALE: this repo is developed largely by agent sessions; every session currently starts by spelunking artifacts/ - mgr status makes the project state discoverable in one call and compounds across every future session. Implementation: extend cli.py IN PLACE; reuse the index/manifest data this bead already defines (status = live view, index = durable record; one data layer, two presentations).
TESTS FOR MGR STATUS (plan-space pass 2): fixture-tree test - a synthetic artifacts/ directory with planted conditions (a stale cert older than its mechanism file's mtime, a fresh bench run, an adjudicable hypothesis with qualifying artifacts, a BLOCKED one without) drives mgr status and asserts each condition is reported correctly in both rich and --json modes; --json schema versioned and validated; empty-tree behavior (fresh clone) renders helpful guidance rather than a stack trace.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest generator produces index.json (and optional markdown) listing artifacts with metadata (run id/commit, config hash, metrics highlights, paths); stored under artifacts/; sample run executed.
close_reason: mgr status [--json] [--write-index]: latest artifact per kind with ages; per-mechanism certificate freshness by mtime vs mechanism source (live smoke immediately flagged tropical+ultrametric certs STALE - source changed since capture); engine-today view (would-rule vs refused with reason histogram, wrapping the real verdict engine); evidence-pool counts; schema mgr.status.v1; --write-index persists artifacts/index.json + index.md as the durable manifest. mgr report (yhy) covers the metric-highlights half

title: Torch ball-tree ultrametric attention path (E3 follow-on): port the exact O(K)-per-query LCP-shell algorithm to nanochat serving+training
notes: Code complete (committed): balltree mode (exact O(K T log T) sort/segmented-cumsum path, training/prefill/decode, any device, bitwise decode parity), mgr bench-ultrametric versioned artifact (mgr.bench.ultrametric_paths.v1), engine bench ingestion, both speedup hypotheses promoted to operationalized. REMAINING before close: generate >= 3 idle-box bench artifacts + adjudicate (waiting for the 71wp campaign to release the CPU - timings under loadavg 110 are dishonest).
close_reason: Code (prior commits) + evidence complete: hyp-balltree-valued-attention-speedup SUPPORTED with effect 39.35x (CI95 [34.95, 43.74], n=3 clean idle-box bench artifacts) vs the registered 4.0x - the exact O(K T log T) ball-tree forward path delivers an order of magnitude beyond the preregistered claim at T=4096. hyp-ultrametric-trie-decode-speedup REFUTED at 0.705x (CI [0.64, 0.77]): the python-loop trie decode is ~30% slower than the vectorized kernel at T=4096 - the O(K)-per-token asymptotic does not survive the current implementation's constant factors at nanochat-scale contexts; honest implementation negative, properly retired. Follow-on if decode speed matters: vectorize the trie query or use the balltree path for decode.

title: Add validation loss evaluation to nanochat training
description: Add simple validation support to nanochat.train: (1) --val-fraction flag to hold out X% of data for validation, (2) Periodic validation CE evaluation every N steps, (3) Log both train_ce and val_ce to summary.json. Keep it simple - no separate perplexity/bpb metrics needed initially. This enables fair A/B comparisons by measuring generalization, not just train loss.
close_reason: Closed against reality (was a stale in_progress claim from January): periodic validation CE evaluation EXISTS in train.py (--val-interval N, --val-batches K; val CE evaluated every N steps on the val split; train_ce_final + val_losses + val_ce_final recorded in summary.json) - landed in c6951ae but untested; now pinned by test_validation_loss_evaluated_and_recorded. DESIGN DEVIATION, deliberate: the bead asked for --val-fraction (hold out X% of data), but the dataloader's split design reserves the LAST parquet shard as the val split (dataloader.py: train = all but last shard, val = last shard) - a shard-level holdout. A fraction flag would cut across the shard/row-group streaming architecture for no measurable benefit at current scale; if sub-shard holdout is ever needed, file a new bead against dataloader.py. Unblocks z7r (A/B benchmarking can measure generalization).

title: EPIC: Production certificate harness - mathematical invariants as runtime checks
description:
WHY (background): The JAX demos validate their mathematical claims in-process (orthogonality checks in matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.py, 1-Lipschitz margin certificates in tropical demo, bit-exact reversibility ledger in reversible demo, mass conservation in simplicial demo, YBE checks in braid demo). But the PyTorch nanochat ports - the implementations that actually get trained and benchmarked - carry almost NONE of that validation. The theory-to-production bridge is the project's core promise (README "Dual Implementation Strategy"); today the production half of the bridge is unverified.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. `mgr certify`: a CLI command checking each mechanism's DEFINING mathematical invariant directly against the production torch modules, with explicit dtype-aware tolerances, rich output, and JSON artifacts.
2. Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for the algebraic kernels (quaternion/octonion products, tropical semiring, LCP ultrametricity, rotation orthogonality).
3. CI integration + regression-gate wiring so a certificate flipping green-to-red between snapshots fails the gate.
4. Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes (bf16/fp32), extreme scales, and adversarial patterns (ties, -inf masking, zero-norm quaternions).

STRATEGIC ROLE: (a) makes the project's claims falsifiable at production scale; (b) acts as the safety net for the EP-A refactor - certificates must pass before and after every migration step; (c) per-release certificate artifacts make the repo credible to outside researchers in a way READMEs cannot.

DELIBERATE ORDERING: this epic's B1 lands BEFORE EP-A's A1 refactor. Building the verification layer first means the refactor is performed under instrumentation instead of on faith.
SCOPE ADDITIONS (idea-wizard): 5. Certificate telemetry (5ki.5) - the designated-subset invariant checks run DURING training as a live health signal with a registered leading-indicator hypothesis (drift precedes divergence); converts this epic's certificates from static gates into early-warning instrumentation. 6. Differential JAX-torch kernel parity (5ki.6) - law tests (B2) prove each side obeys the axioms; the differential suite proves both sides compute the SAME function, closing the convention-divergence hole in the theory-to-production bridge.

title: mgr certify: per-mechanism mathematical invariant checks for nanochat
description:
WHY: the JAX demos verify their own math; the torch ports that actually get trained do not. This command IS the theory->production bridge, and it deliberately lands BEFORE the A1 refactor as its safety net.

WHAT: one CLI command (extend cli.py IN PLACE) that instantiates each mechanism at a small config and verifies its defining invariants with explicit dtype-aware tolerances. Rich table (green/yellow/red, measured value vs tolerance) + JSON artifact under artifacts/certs/nanochat/<run_id>/ (same layout conventions as bench-fixed-flops). Exit nonzero on any red.

THE INVARIANT LIST (this is the spec):
- ALL mechanisms - CAUSALITY: gradient of output[t] w.r.t. input[t+k] is exactly 0 (autograd jacobian on tiny shapes, T<=8; the single most important shared certificate).
- standard: softmax row-stochasticity; RoPE norm preservation per 2D pair.
- tropical: 1-Lipschitz in sup-norm (perturb input by eps, output change <= eps); gauge-fix invariance (tropical_gauge_fix shift does not change argmax routes or outputs); reported gamma margins match brute-force runner-up gaps.
- quaternion: rotor-gate norm preservation (unit-q application preserves value norms within tol); qmul associativity and |q1q2|=|q1||q2| on random unit quaternions.
- octonion: norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y|; alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y; AND a non-associativity witness - assert the associator [x,y,z] is nonzero for generic triples (guards against silently implementing an associative shortcut that would invalidate the framework's premise).
- reversible: forward->inverse round-trip error ||x - inv(fwd(x))|| <= tol per block (fp32); custom-autograd gradient correctness vs naive autograd on small shapes in fp64.
- gauge: rotation matrices orthogonal (R^T R ~ I); GAUGE INVARIANCE - attention output invariant under a global phase shift of input angles (this is the mechanism's actual claim; test it directly).
- ultrametric: strong triangle inequality structure for hard digits - sim(x,z) >= min(sim(x,y), sim(y,z)); trie-vs-kernel decode parity (already tested in test_demos.py - invoke/reference, do not duplicate).
- braid: YBE law holds when braid_crossing_law=ybe (surface the existing static check's result into the cert artifact); payload multiset invariance in discrete mode.
- simplicial: row-stochastic attention => mass conservation through the y1/y2 paths within tol.
- surreal: SurrealLayer decomposition - direction norm == 1; scaling equivariance (scale input by c, output scales as the linear map predicts).

CLI: mgr certify [--mechanism X] [--device cpu|cuda] [--dtype fp32|bf16] [--seed N] [--artifacts-dir D] [--run-id R].

ACCEPTANCE:
- Clean green run on CPU fp32 for all mechanisms in < 2 minutes.
- Failure path proven: a unit test monkeypatches a mechanism to violate one invariant and asserts certify reports red + nonzero exit.
- JSON schema documented (consumed by B3 CI gate and G2 verdict engine).

PITFALLS: tolerances must be dtype-aware (bf16 needs looser bounds - B4 owns the systematic dtype sweep); keep shapes tiny so the suite stays fast enough to run after every A1 migration step.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- VERBOSE MODE: --verbose prints per-check measured value, tolerance, margin-to-tolerance, and duration; run header panel logs seed/device/dtype/config so any red is reproducible from the log alone.
- JSON: include per-check duration_ms and measured values (not just pass/fail) - B3's drift detection and G2's evidence parsing need the numbers.
- LOGGING ON FAILURE: a red check logs the exact tensor stats (shape, dtype, seed) and a one-line repro command (mgr certify --mechanism X --seed N).
- E2E: this command is stage 4 of the rz8.8 pipeline script (fast subset) - keep the fast subset under 2 min CPU permanently; add a runtime regression assertion to its own tests.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM: certify is the designated home for the new theory-derived certificate families as their beads land - keep each in the fast subset where possible: route-stability margin certificates at finite beta (8gk.1: gamma > log(n)/beta implies route fixed); YBE residual + conserved-charge drift certificates (u55.3: rmatrix mode drift < 1e-5, heuristic modes measurably nonzero - certify the SEPARATION); symplecticity J^T Omega J = Omega and energy-drift boundedness (u55.5); triality invariance of the trilinear form (u55.4); Gromov-product degeneration checks (8gk.6: c -> 0 Euclidean and c -> -inf ultrametric endpoints). The JSON schema's per-check measured-value fields already accommodate these; add a family tag so reports group classical vs theory-derived certificates.
close_reason: Implemented mgr certify in cli.py: 41 invariant checks across all 11 mechanisms (shared causality certificate via autograd + per-mechanism: tropical 1-Lipschitz/gauge-shift/margin-vs-bruteforce, quaternion+octonion algebra laws incl. non-associativity separation witness, reversible roundtrip+custom-autograd grad parity, gauge rotation orthogonality/additivity, ultrametric exact strong-triangle on hard digits, braid YBE+restricted-law separation+payload invariance, simplicial mass conservation, fractal router simplex, surreal scale/direction laws). Rich table + repro commands on failure, JSON+md artifacts (schema_version 1, per-check measured/tolerance/duration_ms/family for B3/G2 consumers), --mechanism/--device/--dtype fp32|bf16/--seed/--verbose flags, exit 1 on red. Full suite 41/41 green in 2.0s (budget: 2min). Tests: full-suite green, monkeypatched-violation fails, CLI exit codes+artifacts, gauge regression. NOTE: deferred per close-out finding — the full gauge global-phase invariance certificate requires an architecturally equivariant to_angles map (documented in bead A5/7b0.5 territory); current gauge certs cover orthogonality/inverse/additivity which are the cumsum-transport correctness core. Suite found 1 real pre-existing bug on first run (hn5, fixed).

title: Property-based tests (Hypothesis) for algebraic kernels
description:
WHY: the B1 harness checks invariants on sampled instances; property-based testing checks the underlying algebraic LAWS over a generated input space and finds the corner cases humans do not write. The algebra implementations (qmul/qconj in quaternion_attention_torch.py, omul/oconj in octonion_attention_torch.py, tropical max-plus ops, ultrametric LCP digit logic, Givens/Cayley rotations) encode textbook axioms that Hypothesis can attack directly.

WHAT: add hypothesis to the [dev] extra in pyproject.toml; add tests/test_algebraic_properties.py (a new TEST file is consistent with the existing tests/ layout and AGENTS.md - genuinely new functionality). Laws to encode:
- Tropical semiring: associativity + commutativity of max (exact on floats - use exact equality), distributivity of + over max, identity element -inf behavior, idempotence max(a,a)=a.
- Quaternions: associativity (with magnitude-scaled fp tolerance), norm multiplicativity, conjugation anti-homomorphism (q1q2)* = q2* q1*.
- Octonions: alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y, Moufang identity, norm multiplicativity, and EXISTENCE of non-associativity (find/assert associator != 0 for generated generic triples).
- Ultrametric LCP on hard digit sequences: d(x,z) <= max(d(x,y), d(y,z)) - the strong triangle inequality, exact on integer digits.
- Rotations from angles (gauge Givens path): orthogonality R^T R = I, det(R) = +1.
- RoPE: per-2D-pair norm preservation.

STRATEGIES: bound float magnitudes (e.g. [-1e3, 1e3]) and scale tolerances with magnitude where laws are only approximately true in fp; use exact equality where the law is exact (tropical max, integer LCP).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Suite runs in CI in < 60s using a registered "ci" Hypothesis profile (max_examples tuned); a "thorough" profile for local deep runs.
- Mutation check performed during development: deliberately break one law (e.g. flip a sign in omul) and confirm Hypothesis catches it; document which seed/example in the close notes.
- Wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml test job (it is just pytest - no workflow change should be needed; verify).

NOTE: independent of A1 - these laws hold before and after the refactor, which is exactly what makes them useful during it.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (role change): raised to P1 and wired as a blocker of the A1 AttentionCore refactor (7b0.1). Rationale: during mechanism-by-mechanism migration, instance-level certificates (B1) catch behavioral drift, but the algebra-LAW suite here catches a different class of refactor bug (e.g. an einsum reordering that silently breaks octonion alternativity or tropical distributivity on edge inputs). Both nets must be green after every migration step. Keep the ci profile fast (<60s) so running it per-migration-step stays cheap.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM (polish round 2) - B2 is the designated home for the theory program's PURE-MATH identity tests (module-free laws; B1 certify owns instantiated-mechanism checks; the boundary: if it needs a trained module it is certify, if it is a mathematical identity it is here). The law catalog grows with each landing theory bead - MAINTENANCE CONTRACT: every theory bead's 'numerical theorem checks' land here as part of that bead's own acceptance:
- 8gk.1: LSE semiring axioms at random beta; |LSE_beta - max| <= log(n)/beta (exact inequality - any violation is a bug); mass-concentration bound w_max >= 1 - n e^(-beta gamma).
- 8gk.2: valuation dictionary identities in exact integer arithmetic (v(xy) = v(x)+v(y); v(x+y) >= min with constructed equality/strictness cases; LCP = v_p(x-y)).
- 8gk.5: weighted-ultrametric inequality on random address triples; Moran-dimension Monte Carlo (tolerance-bounded).
- 8gk.6: Gromov product == LCP on constructed trees (exact integers); the hyperbolic decomposition identity -d(q,k) = 2(q|k) - |q| - |k| (fp identity).
- u55.1: BCH identities to weight 4 vs matrix log/exp in fp64; nilpotency A^k = 0 (exact, structural).
- u55.3: YBE residuals for the trigonometric family; [T(u),T(v)] ~ 0 on small chains (fp64) WITH the heuristic-law failure case asserted (separation proves the test has teeth).
- u55.4: triality invariance of the trilinear form; representation-tying on basis elements (exact).
- u55.5: J^T Omega J = Omega for kick maps (fp64); exact kick-inverse round-trip.
- lab.1: Gumbel asymptotics (max of N Gaussians vs sqrt(2 ln N), CI-bounded).
- lab.3: CNF ordinal comparison + the addition-order regression case (exact).
Keep the ci Hypothesis profile under 60s by marking the expensive cases (Monte Carlo, fp64 chains) as the 'thorough' profile; every law test logs its strategy seed on failure for reproduction.
EOF
close_reason: Implemented tests/test_algebraic_properties.py: 18 Hypothesis property tests over the algebraic kernels. EXACT laws (exact equality): tropical max associativity/commutativity/idempotence/identity/-inf-annihilation, distributivity of + over max (exact via monotonicity of fp addition), integer LCP strong-triangle/symmetry, quaternion conj involution. SCALED-TOLERANCE laws (fp64, magnitude-scaled atol): quaternion associativity/norm-multiplicativity/conj-antihomomorphism/q-qbar=norm^2, octonion norm-multiplicativity/alternativity/middle-Moufang/o-obar=norm^2, Givens rotation norm+det+additivity (the cumsum-transport law), RoPE pairwise norm preservation. Non-associativity EXISTENCE via deterministic basis witness (e1e2)e4 != e1(e2e4). Profiles: ci (default, 18 tests in ~2.4s) and thorough (300 examples, ~11s) via HYPOTHESIS_PROFILE. hypothesis>=6.100.0 added to [dev]. MUTATION CHECKS (documented matrix): sign-flip mutation of qmul component -> caught by associativity, mathematically INVISIBLE to norm laws (sign flips preserve norm - the laws are complementary by design); magnitude mutation (x1.01) -> caught by both norm laws, invisible to associativity (scales both sides equally). BONUS FINDING: the thorough profile falsified my own original exact-equality version of the score-shift-covariance test with a 1-ulp example (fp addition is not associative) - fixed to ulp-tolerance with the falsification kept as a comment; demonstrates the profile has teeth. This suite + certify (5ki.1) are the two safety nets gating the A1 refactor, which is now READY.

title: Certificates in CI + regression-gate wiring
description:
WHY: certificates only protect the codebase if they run automatically. Two integration points: continuous (every PR) and longitudinal (regression between artifact snapshots).

WHAT:
1. CI: add a fast certificate job to .github/workflows/ci.yml - CPU, fp32, small shapes, deterministic seeds, < 2 min wall-clock (pattern: existing test job sets JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu). Runs `mgr certify` for all mechanisms; red certificate fails the job; keep it a SEPARATE job so a cert failure is immediately distinguishable from a unit-test failure in the PR UI.
2. Regression gate: wire the B1 certificate JSON into the existing `mgr regressions` machinery (cli.py:2279+) so a certificate that flips green->red (or a measured invariant value that degrades past threshold, e.g. reversible round-trip error growing 10x while still under tolerance) between a baseline and candidate snapshot is reported as a regression, honoring --fail-on-regression for CI use.
3. Document the thresholds and the "tightening-only" policy: tolerances may be tightened over time but loosening requires an explicit justification note in the bead/commit.

ACCEPTANCE:
- CI shows the new job; runs green on main.
- A fixture-based test proves the gate trips: synthetic baseline (green) + candidate (red) certificate JSONs -> regressions exits nonzero with a readable report row.
- Certificate values (not just pass/fail) appear in the regression report so slow drift is visible before it crosses tolerance.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (the harness and its JSON schema) and existing bead model_guided_research-ac9 (auto regression gate - coordinate schema and gate plumbing with it rather than building a parallel gate).

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

TOLERANCE PROVENANCE (added): when lab.1's concentration-class table lands, certificate tolerances should cite their concentration class instead of being hand-set: CLT-class invariants get sqrt(N)-scaled tolerance bands, EVT/Gumbel-class get log(N)-scaled bands, isometry-class (quaternion/octonion/rotation) get width-free bands. Until lab.1 lands, hand-set tolerances carry a provenance comment (# TODO(lab.1): derive from concentration class). This converts the "tightening-only" tolerance policy from convention into principle - a tolerance is justified by the mathematics of its fluctuation class, and loosening one requires showing the class was misidentified.

title: Numerical robustness fuzzing across dtypes and adversarial inputs
description:
WHY: training uses bf16 mixed precision, but unit tests exercise fp32. The exotic ops have dtype-specific failure modes that fp32 tests never see: cumsum gauge angles drifting in bf16, exp(s) surreal scales overflowing, max-plus arithmetic interacting with -inf masking (NaN from -inf + -inf patterns), p-adic digit rounding near .5 boundaries, qnormalize dividing by ~0 norms. The closed bead model_guided_research-0fw added NaN/Inf watchpoints, but there has been no systematic stress campaign.

WHAT: extend the B1 certify harness (same file/command, fuzz subcommand or --fuzz flag) with a sweep per mechanism:
- dtype in {fp32, bf16} (fp16 optional stretch),
- input scale in {1e-3, 1, 1e3},
- sequence length in {1, 2, 255, 256} (boundary cases around block size),
- adversarial patterns: all-equal inputs (softmax/argmax ties), saturated negative scores (-inf propagation through masking), zero-norm quaternion/octonion inputs (normalization division), alternating extreme +/- values (cancellation).
Record per cell: NaN/Inf occurrence in outputs AND gradients, max abs output, gradient finiteness. Output: mechanism x condition matrix with pass/warn/fail, rich table + JSON artifact.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Full fuzz report artifact committed for the current code state.
- Every FAIL cell either gets a root-cause fix (separate commit referencing this bead) or a filed discovered-from bug bead - finding tracked bugs IS the success condition, not an all-green table.
- Explicit regression coverage for: tie-handling, -inf score rows (a token attending to nothing), zero-norm normalization.

POLICY: do NOT fix failures by silently clamping in the fuzz harness or the mechanism - file beads and fix root causes with documented rationale.

DEPENDS ON: B1 (harness infrastructure).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PROGRESS LOGGING: rich progress bar over the cell matrix (mechanism x dtype x scale x length x pattern); each completed cell logs pass/warn/fail inline.
- FAILURE REPRODUCIBILITY: every FAIL cell logs the generating recipe (mechanism, dtype, scale, T, pattern name, seed) sufficient to reconstruct the failing input WITHOUT rerunning the sweep - the discovered-from bug beads must quote this recipe verbatim.
- REGRESSION CAPTURE: each fixed FAIL becomes a permanent pytest regression case (tiny, deterministic) so fixed bugs stay fixed; the fuzz report links cell -> regression test name once captured.
close_reason: Implemented mgr fuzz in cli.py: numerical robustness sweep, dtype {fp32,bf16} x scale {1e-3,1,1e3} x boundary lengths {1,2,63,64} x adversarial patterns {randn, all_equal (maximal ties), alternating (cancellation), zeros (zero-norm normalization paths)} = 880 cells across all 11 mechanisms, recording NaN/Inf counts in OUTPUTS AND GRADIENTS plus max magnitudes, per-cell repro recipes, rich progress bar + per-mechanism matrix table, JSON/md artifacts (schema_version 1, kind=fuzz) under artifacts/certs/fuzz/<run_id>, --fail-on-warn for stricter gating. BASELINE RESULT (22s CPU): 856 pass / 24 warn / 0 fail / 0 error - no NaN/Inf anywhere incl. zero-inputs, total ties, bf16, T=1. Harness detection power proven via NaN-injection test (monkeypatched block -> FAIL with counts + recipe), so zero fails is a finding, not a blind spot. THE 24 WARNS are one signature: reversible at scale=1e3 amplifies to ~1e6 (volume-preserving additive coupling has no normalization and cannot contract magnitudes) - mathematically expected, and it is empirical motivation for the symplectic/coercivity work in u55.5 (which predicts exactly this failure-to-contract and fixes it with conserved energy + confinement). No discovered-from bug beads needed (zero fails). Permanent pytest regressions added for the bead-named cases: ties (all_equal), zero-norm (zeros), T=1 boundary, across all 11 mechanisms, plus harness well-formedness and NaN-injection tests.

title: Certificate telemetry: invariant drift as a live training-health signal (with a registered leading-indicator hypothesis)
description:
WHY: B1 certifies SNAPSHOTS (a model at init or post-hoc); but invariants DRIFT during training - gauge rotations decay from orthogonality under optimizer noise, quaternion rotor norms wander, reversible round-trip error grows in bf16, tropical gamma margins collapse - and that drift is plausibly a LEADING indicator of instability that the loss curve only reveals after the fact. Nothing in this repo (or, to our knowledge, anywhere) treats mathematical-invariant drift as a first-class training-health channel. The NaN watchpoints (closed bead 0fw) catch the explosion; this bead catches the fuse burning.

WHAT:
1. --certify-interval N in train.py (IN PLACE): every N steps run a designated CHEAP subset of B1 invariant checks against the LIVE model (tiny probe batch, no_grad, fp32 islands). Designated checks chosen for cost, one per mechanism where meaningful: orthogonality residual (gauge), rotor norm deviation (quaternion/octonion), forward-inverse round-trip error (reversible), gamma-margin distribution summary (tropical), row-stochasticity/mass residual (simplicial/standard), Lorentz constraint residual (hyperbolic, when F6 lands), charge drift (integrable mode, when u55.3 lands). MEASURED VALUES (not pass/fail) appended to metrics.jsonl under a certs.* namespace - coordinate the schema_version bump with rz8.2 (which must land first).
2. OVERHEAD BUDGET: < 2% step time at the default interval on the CPU smoke config - measured and recorded in close notes; auto-warning if a configuration exceeds it; checks are sampled, never blocking the hot path.
3. DASHBOARD: nyp gains an invariant-drift panel (sparklines per designated invariant with warn-band highlighting) - note added to nyp; coordinate, do not duplicate.
4. THE HYPOTHESIS (register in G1 BEFORE the study): invariant drift PREDICTS divergence. Protocol: an induced-instability run population (LR ramped past the stable point; the u55.5 depth ladder's expected-failure no-norm configs are the natural population) + matched stable controls; detection rule: a designated invariant departs its trailing baseline band; measure LEAD TIME (steps between first detection and loss divergence, defined as NaN or 2x best-loss) and the detection-vs-false-alarm tradeoff (ROC over the run population). Ship the ROC + lead-time table as the study artifact.
5. ACTIONABLE MODE (stretch, default off): --certify-action warn|halt - halt-on-drift converts early warning into a budget saver for sweeps (an E1 rung that would diverge dies in minutes instead of hours); if halt triggers during any E1 smoke, record the compute saved.

TESTS & LOGGING: interval plumbing test (certs.* fields appear in metrics.jsonl at exactly the configured steps, absent otherwise); overhead measurement test (asserts the budget on the smoke config); drift-detection fixture test (synthetically degrade a live model mid-run - inject noise into rotation parameters - assert the telemetry curve moves and the warn fires); probe-batch determinism (seeded); KeyboardInterrupt flush (inherits rz8.2's try/finally guarantee - verify the certs fields survive it); the instability study artifact with seeds, ROC, lead-time table, and an honest negative if lead time is ~0 (an invariant that only breaks WHEN the loss breaks is still a finding - it kills the leading-indicator hypothesis for that mechanism).

DEPS: 5ki.1 (the checks - closed, satisfied), rz8.2 (the metrics stream - hard); parent 5ki epic. RELATION: nyp (panel), u55.5 (instability population), E1/w94.1 (halt-mode payoff), G1 (the leading-indicator hypothesis), 0fw (the lagging-indicator predecessor).
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass 2): E2E INTEGRATION (rz8.8 maintenance contract) - the Scenario 1 training stages enable --certify-interval at a small N and the scenario asserts certs.* fields are present in the produced metrics.jsonl (and survive the SIGKILL/resume cycle of Scenario 2 - telemetry must not break checkpoint resume, and resumed runs must continue the certs stream without gaps or duplicate steps; assert both). This is the composition test no unit test covers.

title: Differential JAX-torch kernel parity suite (same math, two implementations, seeded agreement)
description:
WHY: every framework exists twice - a JAX demo and a torch production module - and the shared algebraic kernels (quaternion/octonion multiplication tables, LCP digit logic, tropical max-plus ops, Givens/Cayley rotation generators) are INDEPENDENTLY implemented on each side. B2 (5ki.2) checks each side obeys the LAWS; nothing checks the two sides compute the SAME FUNCTION. A silent divergence (a sign convention, a different blade ordering, a transposed table) invalidates the project's core bridge claim - "the demo validates the theory, the torch port runs the benchmark" - while every law-based test stays green (both sides can satisfy the axioms while being different algebras up to convention). D3/rz8.3 already does seeded JAX<->torch agreement for HOSS Lanczos; this bead generalizes that pattern to all shared kernels.

WHAT: tests/test_cross_framework_parity.py (new TEST file - consistent with the tests/ layout and the 5ki.2 precedent):
- For each shared kernel pair, seeded random inputs generated ONCE in NumPy (the neutral source), evaluated through both implementations, agreement asserted at dtype-appropriate tolerance: EXACT for integer/digit logic (ultrametric LCP digit extraction) and for max/+ ops on identical floats (tropical, including -inf identity handling); atol <= 1e-6 fp32 for algebra products (qmul/omul vs the JAX demo construction); orthogonality-equivalent comparison for rotation generation (same angles -> same matrices both sides).
- Kernel list (initial): quaternion product + conjugation; octonion product (Cayley-Dickson blocks - blade order must match or the test documents the canonical permutation); tropical max-plus score/aggregate primitives; ultrametric digit extraction + LCP depth; gauge rotation generation from angles; surreal magnitude/direction decomposition.
- Where module APIs hide the kernel, extract a pure function WITHIN the existing module (in-place per AGENTS.md - no forks, no new code files) so the mathematical core is importable by the test.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Suite green in CI (< 30s, CPU, both JAX and torch already in the test environment);
- MUTATION CHECK during development: flip one sign in one multiplication table on one side - the suite catches it; record which mutation/seed in close notes (a parity suite that cannot fail proves nothing);
- any REAL divergence found gets a discovered-from bug bead and a documented resolution stating WHICH side was wrong and WHY (convention vs bug) - finding divergences IS the success condition, mirroring B4's policy. Do not paper over a divergence by loosening tolerance.

TESTS & LOGGING: this bead IS tests; additionally: tolerance table documented per kernel in the test module docstring; seeds logged in assertion messages so any failure is immediately reproducible; rich-formatted parametrized ids (kernel x dtype) for readable CI output.

DEPS: parent 5ki epic; no hard blockers (5ki.2 closed - its Hypothesis strategies for input generation are reusable here). RELATION: rz8.3 (the HOSS cross-check precedent; that bead keeps its own deeper Lanczos comparison), 3bx (the docs-vs-code audit this mechanizes at the kernel level).
close_reason: Implemented in full. tests/test_cross_framework_parity.py: 18 tests, all green - NumPy as the neutral seeded input source pushed through both frameworks. TIER A (direct two-implementation parity): quaternion qmul/qconj/qnormalize incl. batched shapes (fp32 atol 1e-6, fp64 atol 1e-12 under scoped jax.enable_x64 so the demos' fp32 default is untouched); octonion omul/oconj vs demo octonion_multiply/_conjugate (Cayley-Dickson parenthesization agreement) plus a quaternion-subalgebra cross-check that pins blade ordering between the two kernels; tropical - EXACT equality (max/+ are exact ops): new pure function tropical_inner extracted IN PLACE in tropical_attention_torch.py (behavior-preserving: 5/5 tropical certify checks incl. Lipschitz + margin pass, nanochat/test_tropical.py 20 green, demo tropical tests green) vs demo tmm, plus tropical_add/_multiply and -inf identity/propagation parity; gauge - GaugeBlock._apply_rotations (stateless, called unbound) vs demo apply_givens_stage/even_odd_pairs, forward + inverse (conventions verified identical). TIER B (shared-oracle, where the two sides expose different APIs over the same math): torch _PackedPrefixTrie alpha-weighted read vs brute-force LCP-weighted oracle (atol 1e-5); demo HeadTrie.deepest_occupied vs oracle argmax-LCP (exact int); single-key cross-agreement test linking both. DOCUMENTED EXCLUSIONS (in module docstring, per the bead's honesty policy): surreal (no shared kernel - torch decomposes activations, demo does transseries dominance), ultrametric soft/kernel mode (torch-only), _digits_hard_int (no demo twin). MUTATION CHECK (bead acceptance): flipping the az*bx sign in torch qmul -> test_qmul_parity FAILS; flipping the Cayley-Dickson 'ac - db' to 'ac + db' in omul -> both omul parity tests FAIL; both reverted, suite green (seed 20260610). DIVERGENCES FOUND: none - all shared kernels agree across frameworks, so no discovered-from bug beads were needed (the bridge claim holds for the testable kernel set). Tolerance table documented in module docstring. Gates: ruff clean, mypy clean on touched module, 42 tests green across the two new suites.

title: CMA-ES Phase 1 (nanochat bio/exotic params, top-10)
description:
- Define and run a 10D CMA-ES pilot on the most sensitive nanochat/bio-inspired knobs (e.g., tau_rrp, lambda_loge analogue, synaptic gains, structural costs) or closest equivalents in this repo; map to config files; run small-budget evaluations.
