title: EPIC GEO: Data geometry as predictor - profile the corpus, predict the winning mechanism
description:
THE IDEA: every theory epic predicts WHICH mechanism wins WHERE - but always conditioned on hand-asserted data properties ("hierarchical data", "wide dynamic range", "order-sensitive dependencies"). Those properties are MEASURABLE, model-free, before any training: delta-hyperbolicity (Gromov four-point condition) measures tree-likeness; ultrametricity indices (Rammal-Toulouse-Virasoro style) measure nested-cluster structure; log-magnitude spread measures dominance/dynamic-range structure; likelihood deltas under controlled segment transpositions measure order-sensitivity (the data-side shadow of non-commutativity). THE META-HYPOTHESIS - the strongest falsifiable claim this project can make: measured data geometry PREDICTS the mechanism ranking on the scorecard. If true, mechanism selection becomes a measurement rather than a bake-off - "profile your corpus, read off the architecture" - a genuinely new capability (architecture choice by geometry measurement, zero training). If false, the refutation is itself publishable (geometry is not destiny) and the scorecard remains descriptive rather than predictive.

WHY THIS COMPLETES THE PROGRAM: the C battery (vdc) answers "does mechanism M win on task T?"; the E epic answers "does the advantage scale?"; THIS epic answers "could we have KNOWN without running?" - the difference between a benchmark and a predictive science. It also closes an interpretive loop nobody else can close: 8gk.6's learnable per-head curvature reads geometry from the MODEL side; the profiler reads it from the DATA side; per-corpus agreement between the two is a consistency check with no analogue in the literature (registered as its own prediction in GEO.2).

LEAPFROG THESIS: the field selects architectures by leaderboard folklore and ablation roulette. A calibrated, model-free geometry profile with a preregistered, adjudicated prediction map converts "which inductive bias?" from rhetoric into measurement - and this project is uniquely positioned to build it because it already owns (a) eleven mechanisms spanning distinct geometries, (b) a task battery with controllable structure, and (c) the registry/adjudication machinery to keep the claim honest.

CHILDREN: GEO.1 mgr profile-data (estimators + synthetic calibration); GEO.2 the preregistered geometry->mechanism prediction map + adjudication; GEO.3 dose-response study (geometry dials vs advantage curves, with the profiler validating the dials and vice versa).

EVIDENCE DISCIPLINE: every claim preregisters in the G1 registry before any scorecard correlation is computed; estimator calibration on planted geometries is a hard gate before any real-corpus number is reported.
CHILD ID MAP (fresh-eyes pass): GEO.1 = 77l.1, GEO.2 = 77l.2, GEO.3 = 77l.3 (the epic was written before the children had ids; this map makes the labels resolvable without guessing).
CHILD ADDITION (idea-wizard round 2): GEO.4 = 77l.4 geometry-informed initialization - the epic's payoff graduates from prediction to DESIGN: measured geometry warm-starts the mechanisms' structural parameters (fractal radii via 8gk.5's optimal allocation, curvature via 8gk.6's convention, digit depth and beta_0 from the profile), with the warm-start advantage, parameter-travel, and bad-prior-safety predictions preregistered.

title: mgr profile-data: model-free data-geometry estimators with planted-geometry calibration
description:
WHY: see EPIC GEO rationale. The profiler is the load-bearing instrument: model-free estimators of the geometric properties the theory epics condition on. An estimator with unknown bias is a vibe - so CALIBRATION ON PLANTED GEOMETRIES IS THE CORE of this bead, not an afterthought.

WHAT: `mgr profile-data --data DIR | --task X [--mode tokens|activations] [--sample N] [--seed S] [--json]` computing, on sequences sampled from a corpus:
1. DELTA-HYPERBOLICITY: Gromov four-point delta over sampled quadruples of sequence representations, reported normalized (delta/diameter) with bootstrap CI - tree-likeness (predicts hyperbolic/ultrametric/fractal family).
2. ULTRAMETRICITY INDEX: fraction of sampled triangles violating the strong triangle inequality d(x,z) <= max(d(x,y), d(y,z)) plus a Rammal-style cophenetic correlation against the single-linkage dendrogram - nested-cluster structure.
3. DOMINANCE / DYNAMIC RANGE: distribution of log-magnitude spreads (decades spanned within sequences; heavy-tail exponent estimate) - predicts surreal.
4. ORDER-SENSITIVITY: NLL delta between original and k-adjacent-transposed streams under a cheap reference scorer (n-gram model or a tiny frozen standard checkpoint; both modes documented) - the data-side shadow of non-commutativity (predicts braid/integrable/gauge).
5. HIERARCHY DEPTH SPECTRUM: depth statistics of single-linkage trees over sampled representations (complements 1-2 with an explicit depth readout).

REPRESENTATION MODES (report both, agreement = robustness signal): tokens mode (co-occurrence/embedding-free distances where definable) and activations mode (frozen tiny reference model's hidden states). Absolute values are representation-relative; the registered claims are about ORDERINGS across corpora - say this in the output and docs.

CALIBRATION (hard gate): every estimator validated on synthetic data with KNOWN planted geometry before any real-corpus number is believed: balanced p-ary trees (delta ~ 0, ultrametricity high), uniform random point clouds in R^d (delta high, ultrametricity low), hyperbolic point clouds at known curvature, dial sweeps from the vdc.1 generators (once dials land). Each estimator must recover the planted ORDERING across its calibration ladder; these are seeded unit tests, CI-fast subset included.

OUTPUT: profile.json (versioned schema - consumed by GEO.2 adjudication) + a rich radar/table render per corpus; committed artifacts: profiles for the full C1 battery + a FineWeb-Edu sample (the baseline corpus everyone implicitly compares against).

PITFALLS: quadruple/triangle subsampling variance (bootstrap CIs mandatory, sample sizes documented); tokenizer artifacts in tokens mode (document; activations mode is the control); cost (profile of a corpus must run in minutes on CPU - subsample aggressively, it is an estimator not a census).

TESTS & LOGGING: calibration suite as deterministic seeded tests (planted-geometry ordering recovery asserted per estimator); bootstrap CI machinery tested on fixtures with known variance; schema validation + version field; malformed-input handling (tiny corpora -> wide CIs + loud warning, never a silent confident number); rich console output with per-estimator panels.

DEPS: parent GEO epic. No hard blockers (tree/point-cloud calibration data generated inline; coordinate with vdc.1 generators for the dial-sweep calibration tier rather than duplicating - that tier activates when vdc.1's dials land).
REFINEMENTS (plan-space pass):
- FILE PLACEMENT (AGENTS.md compliance, decided up front): the CLI command extends cli.py IN PLACE; estimator math goes into utils.py if it stays compact, else ONE new top-level module (geometry estimators are genuinely new functionality with no existing home - meets the high bar; do NOT scatter estimators across files, and do NOT put them under nanochat/ since they are framework-agnostic).
- TOKENS-MODE DEFAULT (so implementation needs no further design decision): sequence-to-sequence distance defaults to normalized edit distance over token ids for short sequences and Jaccard over token n-gram sets for long ones (threshold documented); both are embedding-free and tokenizer-stable. Activations mode uses cosine distance over mean-pooled hidden states of the frozen reference model. Other metrics may be added behind flags; the registered claims (77l.2) name which mode+metric they use.- E2E INTEGRATION (rz8.8 maintenance contract): when both exist, e2e Scenario 1 gains an OPTIONAL profile-data stage run against the scenario's generated diagnostic task (asserts profile.json validates against schema and the expected metric keys are present, with BEGIN/END rich panels and elapsed time per the scenario logging requirements); skipped-with-warning if the profiler has not landed.
close_reason: Implemented in full. geometry_profile.py (ONE new top-level module per the bead's file-placement refinement): all five estimators - (1) four-point delta-hyperbolicity, quadruple-diameter-normalized, bootstrap CI; (2) ultrametricity: per-triple relative violation (sorted d1<=d2<=d3, ultrametric iff d2==d3) + violating fraction + cophenetic correlation vs single-linkage (subdominant ultrametric) via Kruskal/union-find; (3) dynamic range: numeric-literal decades per doc + Hill tail exponent, NaN-not-fabricated when corpus has no numerals; (4) order sensitivity: relative NLL delta under k adjacent transpositions scored by add-one bigram model; (5) hierarchy depth spectrum: dendrogram depth stats normalized by log2(n). BOTH representation modes per the refinement: tokens (normalized edit distance <= 64 tokens, Jaccard trigram above) and activations (frozen SEEDED random-init tiny GPT, mean-pooled cosine; lazy torch import); both deterministic (byte-identical profiles at fixed seed, tested). CALIBRATION HARD GATE (seeded unit tests): planted tree exactly ultrametric with delta < 1e-12 and cophenetic > 0.999; ladder ordering tree(0.0000) < hyperbolic(0.038) < euclidean(0.069) recovered with separation margins; euclidean ultrametricity violation fraction > 0.9; hierarchy-depth ordering. CLI: mgr profile-data in cli.py IN PLACE (--data/--task/--mode/--sample/--points/--seed/--out/--json), rich tables + warning panels + interpretation note (ORDERINGS not absolutes); --task errors actionably pointing at vdc.1 until gen-tasks lands. profile.json schema_version=1 + validate_profile_schema. DISCOVERED PATHOLOGY, HANDLED: near-disjoint documents give a near-uniform Jaccard metric = DEGENERATE star-tree geometry (delta~0 reads falsely as hierarchical) - added distance-concentration diagnostic (CV < 0.05) with loud warning + distance_diagnostics schema field + regression test; this matters for 77l.2's ordering claims. ARTIFACT: artifacts/profiles/markdown_documentation/profile.json committed (concentration warning correctly firing on prose). TESTS: tests/test_geometry_profile.py, 19 tests green (calibration ladder, CI sanity, distance semantics incl. mode switch, order-sensitivity direction structured>shuffled, dynamic-range extraction, concentration warning, schema+determinism both modes, CLI roundtrip + 3 actionable-failure paths). Gates: ruff clean, mypy clean on the new module, 63 tests green across all session suites. Runtime: 0.2s on a 128-doc corpus (bead budget: minutes). DEFERRED per bead text: dial-sweep calibration tier activates when vdc.1 dials land; C1-battery + FineWeb profiles when those corpora exist in-env; e2e optional stage when rz8.8 lands. UNBLOCKS: 77l.4 fully; 77l.2 now waits only on hij.1; 77l.3 on vdc.1/vdc.2.

title: The geometry-to-mechanism prediction map: preregistered meta-hypotheses + mgr geometry-report
description:
WHY: the profiler (GEO.1) without registered predictions is a dashboard; with them it is a scientific instrument. This bead turns the theory epics' conditional claims into a quantitative PREDICTION MAP, preregistered in the G1 registry BEFORE any profile-vs-scorecard correlation is computed (git history is the timestamp, same discipline as vdc.3).

WHAT:
1. THE MAP: for each (profiler metric, mechanism family) pair with a theoretical basis, a registry entry of the form: "corpora/tasks ranked by metric m induce the predicted ranking of mechanism advantage (vs standard, equal FLOPs)". Concretely (>= 8 entries):
   - ultrametricity index / delta-hyperbolicity -> {ultrametric, fractal, hyperbolic} advantage (Pillar I: the valuation tree pays where the data is tree-like);
   - order-sensitivity -> {braid/integrable, gauge} advantage (Pillar II: non-commutative mixing pays where order carries information);
   - dynamic-range spread -> surreal advantage;
   - hierarchy depth spectrum -> depth at which ultrametric beats standard grows with profiled depth;
   - LOW everything (flat geometry, e.g. shuffled text) -> standard attention wins or ties everywhere (the null-control prediction - as important as the positive ones);
   COMPARATOR: rank correlation (Spearman or Kendall - pick one, document, version with the statistical policy) between metric-ranking and advantage-ranking across >= 5 corpora/tasks, with bootstrap CI; SUPPORTED if CI clears the registered threshold.
2. MODEL-SIDE CONSISTENCY CHECK (the novel cross-check): where 8gk.6's learnable-curvature heads have been trained per-corpus, register: per-corpus mean learned |c| rank-correlates with profiled delta-hyperbolicity (threshold registered). Data-side and model-side geometry must agree or the story has a hole - either outcome is informative, say so in the entry.
3. ADJUDICATION: by the G2 engine over (profile.json x C2/C4 artifacts) - the prediction entries name exact metric paths into both schemas; plus `mgr geometry-report`: the predicted-vs-observed rank matrix rendered (rich + markdown artifact), honest BLOCKED rows where qualifying scorecard runs do not yet exist.

ACCEPTANCE: >= 8 map entries + the null-control + the consistency check validating against the G1 schema (mgr hypotheses validate green); committed BEFORE the correlated scorecard runs; geometry-report renders against whatever evidence exists (a mostly-BLOCKED first report is the honest expected state and gets committed as such).

TESTS & LOGGING: registry entries schema-validated in CI; geometry-report fixture test (synthetic profiles + synthetic scorecard JSONs driving one entry to each verdict state); rank-correlation machinery unit-tested against hand-computed cases; deterministic bootstrap seeds.

DEPS: GEO.1 (profiler + profile.json schema), hij.1 (G1 registry + CLI); parent GEO epic. Soft: vdc.4 (scorecard evidence source - do not hard-block; entries sit open until evidence exists), 8gk.6 (consistency-check evidence).
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass 2): THE CORPUS SET, concretely - rank correlations need >= 5 points; the set is already in-house: the C1 battery tasks (8 synthetic, each a corpus with known intended geometry), the vdc.1 task-9 real-data corpus, and a FineWeb-Edu sample (the flat-baseline control) - >= 10 points without any new data work. Dial settings from 77l.3 add interpolated points for free. Name the exact corpus list in each registry entry so adjudication is mechanical.
CLARIFICATION (fresh-eyes pass 3; prevents an apparent contradiction with vdc.1 task 10): the null-control entry here ("flat geometry -> standard wins or ties") and the placebo expectation in vdc.1 task 10 ("structure-free -> all mechanisms TIE") are NOT in tension - they are two points on the SAME structure dial that task 10 exposes. At the fully-random end nothing is learnable, so all mechanisms tie at the entropy floor (the placebo expectation); at flat-but-learnable points (e.g. shuffled text with intact unigram/local statistics) there is generic structure that standard attention's unbiased machinery can exploit while exotic biases pay no rent, so standard-wins-or-ties is the expectation. The registry entries should name their dial position explicitly: the null-control prediction registers at the flat-but-learnable point, the tie prediction at the fully-random end - one dial, two registered expectations, and the smooth transition between them is itself a free dose-response observation for 77l.3.
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE FROM 77l.1 (discovered during profiler implementation): profiles carry a distance_diagnostics.concentrated flag (pairwise-distance coefficient of variation < 0.05). A concentrated profile means the representation could not discriminate documents (e.g. Jaccard saturation on near-disjoint prose) and its delta/ultrametricity values are STAR-TREE DEGENERATE - low values that do NOT indicate hierarchy. The registered ordering claims here MUST either exclude concentrated profiles from the corpus ranking or register the exclusion rule explicitly; an ordering computed over concentrated profiles is meaningless and would generate false SUPPORTED/REFUTED verdicts.

title: Dose-response study: mechanism advantage as a function of geometry dials
description:
WHY: binary "M beats standard on task T" claims are weak falsification - a threshold delta at one operating point. MONOTONE DOSE-RESPONSE claims are far sharper: "ultrametric advantage INCREASES monotonically with tree imbalance/depth, and vanishes at the flat end of the dial" predicts a CURVE, not a bit, and when refuted it localizes WHERE the theory breaks. This is the standard of evidence pharmacology demands and ML benchmarking almost never provides.

WHAT: using vdc.1's geometry/difficulty dials (a revision to vdc.1 adds them; this bead is its consumer):
1. TRIPLES: 3-4 (mechanism, task, dial) triples chosen for theoretical sharpness: (ultrametric, hierarchical retrieval, tree depth/imbalance dial); (tropical, robustness probe, perturbation-magnitude dial); (braid or integrable mode, word problems, non-solvable-fraction dial - when u55.3 lands; Dyck nesting-depth dial until then); (surreal, wide-range arithmetic, magnitude-decades dial).
2. PROTOCOL: per triple, train at fixed FLOPs across >= 5 dial settings x >= 3 seeds (mechanism + standard baseline at every setting); advantage = metric(mechanism) - metric(standard) per setting; plot advantage-vs-dial with CIs.
3. ADJUDICATION POLICY (registered with the predictions, BEFORE running): monotonicity assessed by isotonic-regression fit improvement over the flat fit (or Page trend test - pick one, document, version); each triple's prediction enters G1 with the dial range and direction.
4. PROFILE CROSS-CHECK (closes the loop GEO.1 calibration opened): run mgr profile-data on each dial setting's generated data - the corresponding profiler metric must move monotonically with the dial. This validates dial and profiler against each other; a dial the profiler cannot see, or a profiler metric the dial does not move, is a finding either way.
5. HEADLINE NUMBER: for at least one triple the dial must span advantage-on to advantage-off; report the crossover point ("ultrametric starts paying at measured ultrametricity >= X") - the first quantitative selection threshold of its kind.

ACCEPTANCE: curves artifact (matplotlib + JSON per C2 schema conventions) committed; registered monotone predictions adjudicated (G2-compatible verdicts); the profile-vs-dial cross-check table included; crossover point reported for >= 1 triple or its absence honestly stated.

TESTS & LOGGING: dial-monotonicity of the GENERATORS asserted as seeded tests (generator-level, cheap - belongs with vdc.1 but verified consumed here); isotonic/trend machinery unit-tested on synthetic curves (planted monotone + planted flat + planted non-monotone, each correctly classified); per-setting configs and seeds recorded in summary.json; rich progress panels per triple; all curves regenerable from artifacts alone.

DEPS: GEO.1 (profiler for the cross-check), vdc.1 (dials - hard dependency: the revision must land first), vdc.2 (eval harness); parent GEO epic. RELATION: vdc.4 (scorecard absorbs the dose-response rows), u55.3 (word-problem dial), G1/G2 (registration + verdicts).
REFINEMENTS (plan-space pass 2):
- BUDGET DISCIPLINE: the dose-response study is about CURVES, not scale - per-setting training uses the smallest fixed-FLOPs budget at which the task is learnable by the strongest mechanism (establish via a 3-point pilot per triple, document the chosen budget in the manifest). 3-4 triples x >= 5 settings x >= 3 seeds x 2 models = 90-120 small runs; at smoke budgets this is hours, not days.
- ORCHESTRATION: reuse the w94.1 pattern - bench-fixed-flops subprocess sequencing + a sweep-level manifest.json (pending/running/done/failed per cell) so the study RESUMES after interruption instead of restarting; artifacts under artifacts/dose_response/<triple>/<dial>/<seed>/ following house conventions.

title: Geometry-informed initialization: warm-start structural parameters from the data profile (--init-from-profile)
description:
WHY: GEO.1/GEO.2 make the profiler PREDICT which mechanism wins; the same measurements can BUILD the winner better. Several mechanisms carry interpretable structural parameters whose optimal values the theory beads literally derive from data geometry - and today every one of them is initialized by convention: fractal per-branch radii (8gk.5 derives the optimal allocation from branch probability mass - currently a theory deliverable with NO practical consumer), hyperbolic per-head curvature c (8gk.6/mnn.4 - learnable, conventionally init near 1), ultrametric digit depth K and alpha/beta temperatures (hierarchy depth spectrum says what K the data needs), initial Maslov beta (the profiled margin distribution vs the log(m)/beta route-stability threshold says where annealing can START without losing routes). Geometry-informed warm-starting converts GEO from a prediction service into a DESIGN service: measure the corpus, then instantiate the matched architecture - no other framework selection story in the field closes this loop.

PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS (G1, before running):
(a) geometry-init matches or beats default-init in final quality at equal budget, with the LARGEST gap early in training (warm-start advantage decays as learning catches up - the curve SHAPE is the signature, not just the endpoint);
(b) learned structural params travel LESS in parameter space from geometry-init than from default-init (distance-traveled metric - evidence the init was near the optimum rather than lucky);
(c) SAFETY: on geometry-MISMATCHED corpora (deliberately wrong profile) the warm start does not hurt beyond noise after full budget (bad priors must wash out, or the feature is a footgun and we say so).

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THE MAPPING TABLE (the design core): profiler metric -> mechanism parameter -> formula, each row citing its theory bead: branch-mass distribution -> fractal radii r_i (8gk.5 optimal-allocation/Moran formula); delta-hyperbolicity -> per-head curvature c init (8gk.6 rescaling convention); hierarchy depth spectrum -> ultrametric digit depth K + temperature inits; margin distribution of random-init scores -> beta_0 for the 8gk.1 anneal (start where coverage is already nonvacuous). Document rows whose formula is not yet derived as explicitly blocked-on-theory rather than improvising.
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --init-from-profile PATH in train.py IN PLACE: reads profile.json (77l.1 schema), computes the mapped params, applies them at model construction; summary.json records {param: (default_value, profile_value, source_metric, formula_ref)} - full provenance, so any run is auditable.
3. EXPERIMENTS: C1 hierarchical retrieval (the geometry-rich case) + one flat control corpus, mechanisms {ultrametric, fractal, hyperbolic-when-landed}, init in {default, geometry, mismatched}, >= 3 seeds, fixed FLOPs; training curves with CIs; predictions (a)-(c) adjudicated.
4. REGISTRY: the three predictions with exact metric paths.

TESTS & LOGGING: mapping unit tests (synthetic profile.json with hand-computed expected parameter values - exact fixtures per formula row); no-profile path byte-identical to current defaults (the flag must be a pure opt-in; goldens before/after); malformed/mismatched-schema profile -> actionable error naming the missing metric; provenance block present in summary.json and validated by schema test; the mismatched-profile harness IS the safety test (c); curves artifact regenerable from metrics.jsonl alone; rich panel at startup showing the applied mapping table for the run.

DEPS: 77l.1 (hard - profile.json is the input contract); parent 77l epic. RELATION: 8gk.5 (radius formula - this is its first practical consumer), 8gk.6/mnn.4 (curvature convention), 8gk.1 (beta semantics), mnn.6 (hyperbolic consumer when landed), GEO.2 (prediction map says WHICH mechanism; this bead tunes HOW), G1.
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass): THE BETA_0 ROW IS NOT PURE-PROFILE - unlike radii/curvature/digit-depth (read directly from profile.json), the beta_0 mapping needs the margin distribution of the RANDOM-INIT model's scores on profiled data - a model+data interaction. Implement it as a cheap startup probe (one forward pass over a small probe batch at init, no_grad, margins recorded) rather than pretending it lives in profile.json; the mapping table marks each row's input source as profile-only vs startup-probe, and the provenance block in summary.json records the probe batch seed. Tests: probe determinism (seeded); probe cost bounded (< 1% of a smoke run, measured).

title: EPIC: Unified attention core, hybrid architectures & performance
description:
WHY (background): The 11 attention mechanisms in nanochat (dispatch in gpt.py Block.__init__, ~gpt.py:326-363; implementations in nanochat/*_torch.py) each independently re-implement the same scaffolding: QKV projection, GQA head repetition (repeat_kv_heads), RoPE, causal masking (model_utils.causal_attn_mask), KV-cache insertion, and the three decode paths (train Tq==Tk / single-token Tq==1 / chunked Tq>1) that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically. This ~10x duplication means every cross-cutting fix is an 11-file change, every new framework costs a full re-implementation, and the single most promising research direction named in the README ("Hybrid Architectures": different attention types per layer, ensembles, adaptive routing) is structurally blocked.

WHAT (scope of this epic, see child beads):
1. Factor shared scaffolding into one place while preserving each mechanism's math EXACTLY (the EP-B certificate harness is the safety net and intentionally lands first).
2. Unlock per-layer mechanism schedules and a learned Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM) router - a genuinely novel architecture and the project's best shot at a headline result.
3. Close known production gaps: gauge block raises NotImplementedError on KV-cache (gauge_block_torch.py ~line 81); octonion attention runs an O(T^2) per-query Python loop (octonion_attention_torch.py ~line 159).
4. Open the path to custom Triton kernels (tropical max-plus first).

STRATEGIC VALUE: turns the repo from "11 parallel experiments" into "a composable algebra of attention mechanisms". Composability is what makes frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F) cheap to add, the standalone API (EP-H) presentable, and hybrids/scorecards (EP-C) possible.

CONSTRAINTS (AGENTS.md): revise files in place (gpt.py, model_utils.py, existing *_torch.py); NO V2/improved/unified file forks; an incredibly high bar for any new code file. All console output rich-styled.

ORDERING: B1 (certificate harness) must be green BEFORE the A1 refactor begins, and re-run green after each mechanism migration.
SCOPE ADDITION (idea-wizard): 5. Attention algebra (7b0.8) - the score/aggregate factorization the A1 refactor produces is also a scientific instrument: recombining score(X) with aggregate(Y) across mechanisms decomposes each mechanism's advantage into its routing half and its mixing half. This is a second research payoff of A1 beyond hybrids/MoM (and a reason the protocol design in A1 should keep the two stages cleanly separable with declared affinity semantics).

title: Refactor: extract shared AttentionCore scaffolding across all 11 mechanisms
description:
WHY: Every mechanism file (tropical_attention_torch.py, ultrametric_attention_torch.py, braid_attention_torch.py, fractal_attention_torch.py, quaternion_attention_torch.py, octonion_attention_torch.py, simplicial_attention_torch.py, surreal_torch.py) duplicates the scaffolding that CausalSelfAttention (gpt.py:185-305) implements canonically: QKV projection, GQA repeat_kv_heads, RoPE application, causal_attn_mask handling, KV-cache insert, and the three decode paths (Tq==Tk train / Tq==1 decode / Tq>1 chunked decode). Historically a fix in one copy does not propagate to the other ten. This duplication blocks per-layer schedules (A2), MoM routing (A3), cheap addition of frameworks #12/#13 (EP-F), and a presentable standalone API (H1).

WHAT: Introduce a single scaffold (revise gpt.py and/or model_utils.py IN PLACE per AGENTS.md - no new file unless genuinely unavoidable) where a mechanism contributes only its distinguishing operations: score(q,k)->affinities and aggregate(affinities,v)->y, plus optional pre/post hooks for mechanisms that need them (gauge transport, braid permutation accumulation, ultrametric digit projections to_digits_q/to_digits_k). Mechanism modules keep their files but shrink to the math.

HOW (migration discipline):
1. Design the protocol off the union of current signatures; document it with docstrings in code.
2. Migrate standard attention first with a golden test proving zero behavior change.
3. Migrate one mechanism at a time; after EACH migration run `mgr certify` (B1) and the tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests. Never migrate two mechanisms in one commit.
4. Preserve ALL config flags (tropical_*, ultrametric_*, braid_*) and buffer-stat logging (e.g. tropical_gamma_head_mean/min) byte-for-byte in summary outputs.

BOUNDARY DECISIONS (document in code): reversible (halves channels, wraps attention as F, requires even n_head) and gauge (replaces the whole block incl. MLP) are BLOCK-level specializations - do not force them into the core protocol; define and document where the scaffold's responsibility ends.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All existing tests pass; B1 certificate suite green before AND after with identical or tighter tolerances.
- Fixed-seed 50-step CPU training run per mechanism: loss trajectory identical to pre-refactor (bitwise where dtype allows, else atol<=1e-6) - capture pre-refactor goldens FIRST.
- Net LOC reduction in mechanism files; no public flag or CLI change.

PITFALLS: torch.compile interactions (keep dispatch static per layer, no data-dependent Python branching in hot path); do not break the WeakKeyDictionary trie cache in ultrametric; keep buffer registration names stable (external tooling reads them).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (migration discipline + logging):
- The per-migration verification gate is now THREE nets, all green after EACH mechanism migration: (1) mgr certify (B1/5ki.1), (2) the Hypothesis algebra-law suite (B2/5ki.2 - now a wired dependency), (3) tests/test_demos.py KV-cache parity tests.
- GOLDEN-CAPTURE LOGGING: when capturing pre-refactor goldens (fixed-seed 50-step loss trajectories per mechanism), store them as committed fixture files with a manifest (mechanism, seed, config hash, torch version) and log the capture conditions - a golden that cannot be re-derived is not a golden.
- MIGRATION LOG: maintain a short per-mechanism migration checklist in the PR/commit messages (mechanism, nets run, golden match result, LOC before/after) so the 11-step campaign is auditable afterward.

=== EXECUTION LOG (2026-06-10, RubyKite) ===

SCAFFOLD: AttentionCore in nanochat/model_utils.py (in place, no new code file). Protocol: forward() owns QKV proj / RoPE+QK-norm / transpose / KV-insert with uniform pre-insert pos0 capture / optional GQA repeat (gqa_via_repeat) / reassembly+c_proj+finalize. Override points coarse-to-fine: attend() | score()+aggregate() around the shared conditional-mask+softmax default | finalize(). linear_cls hook for surreal. sdpa_causal_attend shared helper (standard non-flex + surreal).

MIGRATION LOG (each its own commit; gate = goldens 11/11 BITWISE + certify 43/43 + algebra/parity/demos 104 passed):
1. standard 4bb103a: attend() override (flex + entropy + SDPA three-path via helper; chunked mask now causal_attn_mask, bitwise-identical tensor). gpt.py 778->724.
2. tropical eae7688: attend()+finalize() (the post-c_proj gauge center is why finalize exists). 323->302.
3. ultrametric 40efc63: attend() keeps trie-ingest-on-every-cached-forward + Tq==1 read short-circuit; trie decode exercised directly. 400->376.
4. simplicial 8f0bc02: pure score()+aggregate(); scaffold pos0 replaces hand-captured t0. 88->47.
5. surreal cb46813: linear_cls=SurrealLayer, gqa_via_repeat=False, attend=sdpa helper. 116->84.
6. quaternion 24af8b1: score()=flat dot (= scalar part of q*conj(k)); aggregate()=rotor-gate associativity factorization. 185->95.
7. octonion 65d2b7e: aggregate() keeps per-query loop with explicit (Q*conj(K))*V parenthesization; 7b0.6's target now isolated in one method. 188->105.
8. braid 832ff76: score()=s_q+s_k^T exposed for 7b0.8; attend() override (sigmoid/threshold weights, schedule recording reads masked scores, /sqrt(Tk)). 221->193.
9. fractal 4167908: score() ONLY - default attend+aggregate are verbatim what the old forward did. 109->55.

TOTALS: mechanism files 1630->1257 (-373), gpt.py -54, model_utils.py +144 (net -283). State-dict keys, buffer names (tropical_gamma_*, attn_entropy_head_mean), config flags, CLI: all byte-stable. Boundary documented in AttentionCore docstring: reversible+gauge stay block-level (A1).

GOLDENS: tests/test_attention_core_goldens.py + tests/fixtures/attention_goldens/*.json (committed 4887517, BEFORE any refactor). Single-thread CPU, seed 20260610, 50 AdamW steps, host+torch-pinned with skip-and-recapture semantics.

DISCOVERED (the harness paid for itself before the refactor started):
- FIXED 9363c1f: GPT.init_weights crashed for surreal (SurrealLayer has no .weight; exp(s)*normalize(v) cannot represent zero stably -> guard + keep construction init). train --attention-type surreal was broken since surreal landed.
- FILED model_guided_research-1fr6 (bug, P1): GaugeBlock has NO residual connection -> zero-init mutual deadlock, total |grad| = 0.0 exactly, gauge training frozen at ln(V) forever. Fix changes numerics so it is NOT in this behavior-preserving campaign; gauge.json golden pins the frozen trajectory and must be recaptured when 1fr6 lands.

FINAL VALIDATION: full suite 249 passed/1 skipped (CI config), mgr theorems validate --deep 0 errors/0 warnings, flex verifier clean, ruff clean, mypy: zero NEW errors (3 pre-existing in touched files confirmed at pre-refactor commit).
close_reason: Completed: AttentionCore scaffold extracted in place (model_utils.py); all 9 in-scope mechanisms migrated one-commit-each with bitwise golden parity (11/11 mechanisms, 50-step fixed-seed CPU trajectories), certify 43/43 before+after, algebra/parity/demos nets green per migration, full suite 249 passed, theorems validate --deep clean. Net mechanism-file LOC -373. Reversible+gauge documented as block-level boundary. Discovered+fixed surreal init_weights crash; discovered+filed gauge residual deadlock (1fr6).

title: Per-layer attention schedules (heterogeneous mechanism stacks)
description:
WHY: README "Future Directions -> Hybrid Architectures". Theory assigns different comparative advantages per mechanism (gauge: stability; ultrametric: hierarchy; tropical: robustness; standard: general). Layers plausibly want different inductive biases (e.g. gauge early for stable feature extraction, ultrametric mid for hierarchical routing, standard late for generic mixing). Today GPTConfig.attention_type is one global string - heterogeneous stacks are impossible without code edits.

WHAT: Allow GPTConfig.attention_type to accept a per-layer list. CLI: --attention-schedule "gauge,ultrametric,standard,standard" (length must equal n_layer, or be a shorter repeating pattern - define and document the expansion rule). Validate per-mechanism constraints PER LAYER at config time with actionable error messages: head_dim%4 (quaternion), head_dim%8 (octonion), n_embd even (gauge/reversible), n_head even (reversible).

KV-CACHE: engine.KVCache must support per-layer geometry differences; simplicial y1 cache lanes only for simplicial layers (precedent: engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache); schedule validation REJECTS gauge in cached-generation paths until A5 lands (clear error naming the bead).

ARTIFACTS: summary.json records the full resolved schedule; bench-fixed-flops accepts schedule strings as variants; estimate_flops() (gpt.py:610) currently assumes homogeneous layers - extend to sum per-layer estimates.

OPTIMIZER: verify Muon's matrix-parameter grouping (setup_optimizers, gpt.py:623-664) remains correct for heterogeneous blocks (different blocks expose different parameter shapes).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Mixed-schedule training runs on CPU smoke config; generation works for cache-supporting mixes.
- Tests: config validation errors (wrong length, invalid mechanism, constraint violation per layer), per-layer dispatch correctness (each layer instantiates the intended class), KV parity test for a mixed stack (full forward vs incremental decode).
- run.md/summary.json show the schedule; regressions tooling treats schedule string as the variant key.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (shared scaffolding makes per-layer instantiation uniform).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- STARTUP LOGGING: at model build, log the fully-resolved per-layer schedule as a rich table (layer index, mechanism, per-mechanism constraint check result) so a misconfigured schedule is visible before any compute is spent.
- E2E TEST: tiny CPU training run with a mixed schedule asserting (a) summary.json records the resolved schedule, (b) each Block instantiated the intended class (introspect model.blocks), (c) metrics.jsonl present and parseable.
- NEGATIVE TESTS with message-quality assertions: wrong schedule length, unknown mechanism name, per-layer constraint violation (e.g. octonion with head_dim%8!=0 at layer 2) - each error must name the offending LAYER INDEX and the fix.
- KV parity test for a mixed stack must cover both single-token and chunked decode paths.

title: Mixture-of-Mechanisms (MoM): learned routing across attention mechanisms
description:
WHY: the most ambitious architectural idea available to this project: instead of choosing a mathematical structure per run (or per layer, A2), let the model LEARN which structure to apply. This converts the 11-way A/B question into differentiable architecture search, and the learned routing weights are publishable evidence by themselves ("which math does a transformer prefer, per depth and per token type?").

WHAT: a MoM block that runs K member mechanisms in parallel and blends outputs y = sum_i g_i(x) * y_i with a learned gate. Start with the cheap, KV-cache-compatible subset: standard, tropical, ultrametric(kernel), quaternion, fractal. Three routing modes, in implementation order:
1. per-layer scalar gates (softmax over K learned logits),
2. per-token gating: gate = softmax(Linear(norm(x))) applied tokenwise,
3. straight-through top-1 hard routing to recover compute (optional stretch).
CLI: --attention-type mom --mom-mechanisms standard,tropical,... --mom-routing layer|token|top1 --mom-balance-coef <float> (MoE-style load-balancing aux loss to prevent router collapse).

DIAGNOSTICS: log routing entropy and per-mechanism utilization as registered buffers (pattern: tropical_gamma_* in tropical_attention_torch.py) and emit them into the D2 metrics stream.

KV-CACHE: each member keeps its own cache lane => memory cost ~K x; document honestly and default-cap K<=4.

HONEST ACCOUNTING: compute cost is ~K x per layer. Fixed-FLOPs comparisons must use measured FLOPs/wall-clock, not the homogeneous analytic estimate (relates existing bead model_guided_research-bks "FLOPs vs measured TFLOPs validation").

ACCEPTANCE:
- Trains stably on CPU smoke config; routing weights visible in metrics JSONL.
- Unit tests: gate gradient flow (router weights move from init on seeded data); non-collapse with balance loss (utilization entropy above threshold after N steps); KV parity in layer-gating mode; config validation (unknown member, K too large).
- A4 covers the benchmark; this bead only needs a smoke-scale sanity comparison vs standard.

DEPENDS ON: A2 (per-layer machinery and heterogeneous KV-cache are prerequisites).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- E2E SMOKE: short CPU training with --attention-type mom asserting router stats (per-member utilization, routing entropy) appear in metrics.jsonl (D2) at every log_interval and are non-degenerate by run end.
- LIVE LOGGING: per-layer member utilization logged at log_interval (compact rich line); COLLAPSE WARNING: if utilization entropy stays below threshold for K consecutive intervals, emit a loud rich warning suggesting --mom-balance-coef increase - silent router collapse is this architecture's primary failure mode.
- UNIT TESTS: gate gradient flow; balance-loss effect (entropy with vs without aux loss on seeded data); straight-through estimator correctness for top1 mode (forward uses argmax, backward uses soft grads); KV parity in layer-gating mode; cache memory accounting matches K-lane expectation.

title: Hybrid-vs-monolith benchmark at fixed FLOPs
description:
WHY: claims about hybrid stacks are worthless without the same fixed-budget, multi-seed, CI-bearing harness used for monoliths. This bead is the empirical payoff of A2/A3.

WHAT: extend `mgr bench-fixed-flops` (cli.py:1142+) to accept schedule strings and MoM variants, then run a curated comparison:
(a) all-standard baseline;
(b) three hand-designed schedules, each with its theoretical motivation documented inline in the report:
    - "stability->hierarchy->generic": gauge, ultrametric, standard, standard
    - "robust front-end": tropical, tropical, standard, standard
    - "geometry mid-stack": standard, quaternion, quaternion, standard
(c) MoM-layer with K=3 (standard/tropical/ultrametric) if A3 has landed;
>=3 seeds each; report mean +/- CI for final loss, tokens/s, peak memory.

OUTPUT: suite report.md + summary.json under artifacts/bench/hybrids/<run_id>/ using the existing artifact layout; schema compatible with the `mgr regressions` command so hybrid runs participate in regression gating. Statistical comparison reuses the Mann-Whitney U / effect-size machinery already present in cli.py (per-head-metrics command).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Report ranks variants with effect sizes and CIs; one explicit verdict line per theory motivation (supported/refuted at this scale).
- Runs reproducible from a single documented command; seeds and configs recorded in summary.json.

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-z7r (granular A/B feature benchmarking) shares harness patterns - coordinate plumbing, do not duplicate; this bead specifically answers the hybrid question. MoM FLOPs accounting caveat from A3 applies (use measured wall-clock alongside analytic budget).

DEPENDS ON: A2 (schedules). A3 optional - run (c) only if available; do not block on it.

title: Gauge block: KV-cache + GQA support (remove NotImplementedError)
description:
WHY: gauge_block_torch.py raises NotImplementedError when a kv_cache is passed (~line 81) and requires n_kv_head==n_head. It is the ONLY mechanism excluded from generation, which blocks `mgr sample` full coverage (D5), gauge participation in cached hybrid schedules (A2), and any future serving story. tests/test_demos.py currently asserts the rejection - that test flips to a parity test when this lands.

TECHNICAL CORE: the obstacle is the cumulative gauge field - global angles are a cumsum of per-token local angles, so token t's frame depends on all previous tokens. SOLUTION: cache the running cumulative angle (a per-stream (B, d/2) fp32 tensor) in the KVCache as a per-layer extra-state lane, following the simplicial y1 precedent (engine.py ensure_simplicial_y1_cache / insert_simplicial_y1). At decode step t: global_angle_t = cached_cumsum + local_angle(x_t); transport x_t into the global frame; attend against cached keys ALREADY STORED IN THE GLOBAL FRAME (transport is applied per-token at insert time, so cached keys remain valid - write the invariance argument as a code comment, it is the correctness crux).

ALSO: lift the n_kv_head==n_head restriction by applying repeat_kv_heads after transport, matching the standard path.

NUMERICS: angle accumulation in bf16 drifts over long sequences - keep the cumsum buffer fp32 regardless of autocast, and document this.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity test mirroring the tests/test_demos.py KV pattern: full-sequence forward vs token-by-token incremental decode, and vs chunked decode, atol per dtype.
- Long-decode drift test: 2k-step decode, max deviation from full-forward bounded and recorded.
- A2's schedule validation updated to allow gauge in cached paths; D5 sampling includes gauge.
- B1 gauge certificates (orthogonality, gauge invariance) pass in cached mode too.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (land after the refactor so cache plumbing is written once against the final scaffold; gauge stays a block-level specialization per A1's boundary decision).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- DEBUG LOGGING: optional flag (e.g. --gauge-cache-debug / env) logging per-decode-step cache state: position, fp32 cumsum norm, drift estimate vs full-forward reference on the first N steps - makes drift bugs diagnosable from logs alone.
- E2E: once landed, gauge must join the mgr sample determinism scenario (rz8.8 Scenario 3) and the mixed-schedule generation test in A2; update the test that currently ASSERTS NotImplementedError to assert parity instead (do not leave a stale expected-failure).
- UNIT TESTS: cumsum-lane save/restore through D1 checkpointing (a resumed generation must continue the gauge field exactly); GQA path (n_kv_head < n_head) parity after the restriction is lifted.
close_reason: Completed: KVCache gains a per-layer fp32 running-cumsum angle lane (ensure_gauge_angle_cache; reset() zeroing since the lane accumulates; prefill() batch expansion). K/V cached already in the fixed global frame - frame-invariance argument written as the module docstring. c_attn split into c_q/c_k/c_v: GQA via SDPA enable_gqa; NotImplementedError + n_kv_head==n_head restriction removed. Gauge joins both parametrized KV suites; new tests: token-by-token parity, 2048-step drift bound, cache-reset semantics. New certify check gauge.kv_decode_matches_full_forward (44/44 fp32+bf16). NOT done here (blocked on unlanded beads, noted): cumsum-lane save/restore through D1 checkpointing (rz8.1 not landed), mgr sample determinism scenario (rz8.8 not landed), A2 schedule validation (7b0.2 not landed).

title: Octonion attention: vectorize the per-query Python loop
description:
WHY: octonion_attention_torch.py (~line 159) loops over Tq in Python computing (Q * K_conj) * V per query to avoid materializing a (B,H,Tq,Tk,N,8) tensor. This makes octonion training orders of magnitude slower than peers and poisons every fixed-FLOPs/wall-clock comparison it appears in (budget spent in Python overhead, not math). It also makes octonion infeasible for the EP-E scaling ladder.

WHAT: replace the loop with CHUNKED vectorization: process Tq in tiles of size c, computing the non-associative product with batched einsum over the tile. Choose c automatically from a memory budget: B*H*c*Tk*N*8*dtype_size <= budget (expose --octonion-tile-budget or derive from available memory; document default). CRITICAL: octonion multiplication is NON-ASSOCIATIVE - the exact parenthesization ((Q*K_conj)*V) must be preserved; any "optimization" that reorders the product changes the mathematics.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Outputs match the current loop implementation on seeded inputs to atol<=1e-6 fp32 (target bitwise where reduction order is unchanged) across shapes: Tq in {1, 7, 256}, GQA on/off, B in {1, 4}.
- >=5x wall-clock improvement on CPU at T=256 (record before/after numbers in the close notes; store microbench JSON under artifacts/perf/ following the pattern of existing bead model_guided_research-2cg per-op microbench).
- Property test added to the B2 suite asserting alternativity x(xy)=(xx)y and norm multiplicativity |xy|=|x||y| hold identically for the vectorized path (guards against silent reordering).
- Decode path (Tq==1) remains fast - no regression.

PITFALLS: omul builds octonions from 2x2 quaternion blocks via Cayley-Dickson (o1*o2 = (ac - d_conj*b, d*a + b*c_conj)) - keep that structure; einsum over blades must reproduce the same multiplication table (verify against a brute-force table test).

DEPENDS ON: A1 (vectorize once, against the final scaffold interface).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- INIT LOGGING: log the auto-chosen tile size and the estimated peak intermediate memory once at module init (debug level) - the tile heuristic must be auditable from logs.
- E2E MICROBENCH SCRIPT: before/after wall-clock + peak-mem JSON artifact under artifacts/perf/ including env info (device, dtype, torch version); rich comparison table printed; this artifact is the bead's evidence of the >=5x claim.
- UNIT TESTS: tile-boundary correctness (Tq not divisible by tile size; Tq smaller than one tile; Tq==1 decode), brute-force multiplication-table equivalence test for the einsum path, OOM-guard behavior when the budget forces tile=1 (must degrade to current per-query behavior, not crash).

title: Tropical max-plus Triton kernel (first custom kernel)
description:
WHY: README Open Question #4: "Hardware: custom kernels for exotic operations?" Tropical attention is the best first target: max-plus GEMM (tropical_max_plus_attention, tropical_attention_torch.py:19-85) is structurally identical to matmul with (+,*) replaced by (max,+), so a Triton tile kernel is a direct transcription of the classic matmul tiling - and tropical is among the most promising mechanisms (1-Lipschitz robustness certificates). The current implementation is memory-bound through broadcast intermediates.

WHAT:
1. Forward Triton kernel: score(q,k) = max_d(q_d + k_d) fused with causal masking, then y_d = max_k(score + v_kd).
2. Custom backward: the subgradient routes through argmax indices - store argmax_d (scores) and argmax_k (aggregation) per output during forward (this mirrors exactly how route extraction works in the JAX demo tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.py; the kernel byproduct gives us per-sample discrete routes at production scale for free - note this for interpretability follow-ups).
3. Integration behind --tropical-kernel triton|reference with automatic fallback when Triton/CUDA is unavailable (CPU CI must pass untouched).
4. Margin statistics (tropical_gamma_*) computed as a kernel byproduct (runner-up tracking) so the diagnostics don't disappear in fast mode.

TIES POLICY: multiple argmax => deterministic first-index selection in BOTH kernel and reference; assert agreement in tests (otherwise gradients differ silently).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Parity vs reference: max/+ are exact ops, so same-dtype outputs must match bitwise on tie-free inputs; tie-handling test passes; gradcheck (fp64 reference path) on small shapes.
- Speedup >=3x at T>=1024 on GPU, recorded in artifacts/perf/.
- B1 tropical certificates (1-Lipschitz, gauge-fix invariance, margin consistency) pass with the kernel enabled.

DEPENDS ON: A1 (stable integration surface) and existing bead model_guided_research-c6h (Triton hotspot benchmarking plan - confirms tropical is the top-value target and sets measurement methodology BEFORE we invest in kernel work).
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- PATH LOGGING: log the active kernel path (triton|reference) and the REASON (flag, no CUDA, no triton import) exactly once at model build - benchmark results must never be ambiguous about which path ran; record the path in summary.json.
- CI: GPU-optional e2e training smoke (--tropical-kernel triton) gated on torch.cuda.is_available + triton import; CPU CI runs the reference path untouched. Gradcheck and tie-policy tests run on CPU against the reference implementation regardless.
- UNIT TESTS: forward bitwise parity on tie-free inputs; deterministic first-index tie test (constructed ties); backward parity vs reference autograd; margin-stat byproduct equals reference computation.

THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM (see 8gk.1): the kernel should support FINITE-BETA mode (log-sum-exp semiring aggregation, numerically-stable streaming LSE per tile - same tiling, different reduction op) so dequantization-annealing runs (beta schedules from 8gk.1 / lab.3) get kernel speed throughout training, not only at the tropical endpoint. The beta=inf path must remain bitwise-identical to the max-plus path; finite-beta parity tests against the reference LSE implementation with magnitude-scaled tolerances. If finite-beta complicates v1, ship max-plus first and file the finite-beta extension as a discovered-from bead - do not silently drop it.

title: Attention algebra: score/aggregate recombination matrix - which half of attention carries the inductive bias?
description:
WHY: the A1 refactor factors every mechanism into score(q,k)->affinities and aggregate(affinities,v)->y. That factorization is not just plumbing - it is a SCIENTIFIC INSTRUMENT nobody has built: compose score(X) with aggregate(Y) across mechanisms and the resulting composite matrix DECOMPOSES each mechanism's advantage into its routing half and its mixing half. Questions that become answerable: is tropical's robustness in the max-plus scores or the max-plus aggregation? Does ultrametric routing + softmax aggregation retain the hierarchy win (if yes, the trie's value is ADDRESSING, not mixing - which directly informs the A7 Triton kernel and the 8gk.2 ball-tree design)? Is rotor-gated aggregation (quaternion) useful under standard scores? DISTINCT FROM: A2/7b0.2 (per-layer schedules = composition ACROSS layers) and A3/7b0.3 (MoM = parallel blending of WHOLE mechanisms); this bead composes INSIDE one head.

WHAT:
1. AFFINITY-SPACE CONTRACT (the design core): not all pairs type-check - ultrametric scores are match probabilities, tropical affinities live in max-plus, softmax aggregation expects logits. Extend the A1 protocol so each score() declares its affinity semantics (logits | probabilities | max-plus) and each aggregate() declares what it consumes. ADAPTERS between spaces are exactly the log/exp Maslov correspondence from 8gk.1 - the adapter IS the semiring homomorphism, and an adapter is only honest where that homomorphism is exact (state this in code comments; it is the pleasing part). Tier-1 composites need no adapter (run the full matrix); tier-2 are adapter-mediated (run the theoretically motivated subset, adapter documented per pair); gauge and reversible are out of scope (block-level per A1's boundary decision - say so in validation errors).
2. COMPOSITE CERTIFICATE TABLE (short derivation note, house style): which (score, aggregate) pairs preserve which B1 certificates - causality (all pairs, by construction), 1-Lipschitz (needs BOTH stages nonexpansive - derive which combinations qualify), mass conservation (row-stochastic aggregation only). The table is itself a contribution: certificate composition rules for attention.
3. CLI: --attention-score X --attention-aggregate Y (mutually exclusive with --attention-type, validated with actionable errors); summary.json records the pair as the variant key; bench-fixed-flops accepts pair variants.
4. THE SWEEP: tier-1 matrix + motivated tier-2 pairs at fixed FLOPs, >= 3 seeds, on 2-3 C1 tasks where parent mechanisms differ most (hierarchical retrieval, Dyck, robustness probe) + LM smoke; deliverable: a matrix heatmap (advantage vs standard) with the pure-mechanism diagonal marked.
5. PREREGISTER (G1, before the sweep): (a) ultrametric-score x standard-aggregate retains >= 70% of pure ultrametric's hierarchical-retrieval advantage (the advantage lives in routing); (b) standard-score x tropical-aggregate retains < 30% of tropical's robustness advantage (the certificate needs max-plus scoring); (c) at least one OFF-DIAGONAL composite beats both parents somewhere (the recombination-novelty bet - register as an open question with adjudication criteria if a directional bet is not justified).

TESTS & LOGGING: contract validation tests (incompatible pair -> actionable error naming the affinity spaces; adapter pairs construct correctly); KV-cache parity for 3 representative composites (house full-vs-incremental pattern); certify causality across the entire tier-1 matrix (parameterized test - cheap, tiny shapes); composite certificate table cross-checked numerically (every pair claimed 1-Lipschitz gets the perturbation test; a violation falsifies the derivation and fails loudly); variant-key determinism; heatmap artifact with seeds + CIs.

DEPS: 7b0.1 (the protocol IS the instrument - hard); parent 7b0 epic. RELATION: 8gk.1 (adapters = Maslov homotopy; coordinate), vdc.1 (tasks), vdc.4 (best composites join the scorecard), 7b0.7 (kernel work informed by where routing value concentrates), G1 (preregistration).
REFINEMENTS (plan-space pass 2):
- STARTING MECHANISM SET: the composable set is determined by A1's final protocol-conformance table; start from the A3 cheap KV-compatible subset precedent {standard, tropical, ultrametric(kernel), quaternion, fractal} and extend as conformance allows (braid's permutation accumulation and simplicial's y1 path use pre/post hooks - whether their score/aggregate halves are separable enough to recombine is itself documented as part of the conformance table). Note tier-1 (no-adapter) pairs are the SAME-affinity-space pairs, so the full matrix is mostly tier-2 - the heatmap renders tier per cell so readers see which results are adapter-mediated.
- E2E/BENCH SMOKE: one composite variant (ultrametric-score x standard-aggregate) added to the rz8.8 Scenario 4 bench-fixed-flops variant list once both land - proves the --attention-score/--attention-aggregate plumbing composes with the harness and regression gate end-to-end, with the scenario's stage-level rich logging.
