title: EPIC THEORY-I: Non-archimedean unification - attention over valued fields
description:
THE DISCOVERY: five of this project's frameworks are not parallel experiments - they are facets of ONE mathematical object, geometry over valued fields (fields equipped with a valuation v satisfying v(xy)=v(x)+v(y) and the ultrametric inequality v(x+y) >= min(v(x),v(y))):

1. ULTRAMETRIC/p-ADIC: the mechanism's similarity LCP(x,y) IS the p-adic valuation v_p(x-y) of the difference - longest common prefix of base-p digits is literally how valuation is computed. The trie in ultrametric_attention_torch.py is a finite subtree of the Bruhat-Tits tree.
2. TROPICAL: (min,+)/(max,+) arithmetic IS the image of valued-field arithmetic under v: products map to sums exactly, sums map to min generically. Tropical attention computes the valuation shadow of p-adic bilinear attention.
3. SOFTMAX ITSELF: log-sum-exp is Maslov quantization - for every beta, x (+)_beta y = (1/beta)log(e^(beta x)+e^(beta y)) is an EXACT semiring, deforming continuously to (max,+) at beta=infinity. Standard smooth attention and tropical attention are two points on one semiring family; the codebase already contains both endpoints without knowing they are connected.
4. IFS/FRACTAL: the address space of an IFS carries the cylinder ultrametric d(a,b) = r^LCP(a,b); FractalKV and the ultrametric trie are the same data structure with different radii conventions; capacity is governed by the Moran equation.
5. SURREAL/TRANSSERIES: Conway normal form is a Hahn series representation; dominance order IS valuation by leading term; the surreal demo's dominant-balance probing is tropicalization over a series field.
6. HYPERBOLIC (#13): tree boundaries carry ultrametrics via d(xi,eta) = exp(-(xi|eta)) where (.|.) is the Gromov product - which in a tree EQUALS LCP depth. Hyperbolic attention degenerates to ultrametric attention in the rescaled curvature -> -infinity limit.

LEAPFROG THESIS: conventional deep learning fixes one semiring ((+,x) with softmax glue) and bolts on hierarchy, sparsity, robustness, and quantization as engineering afterthoughts. Treating the SEMIRING AS A DESIGN AXIS and the valuation as the organizing geometry yields: exact robustness certificates (the tropical limit), native hierarchy (the valuation tree), principled quantization with non-accumulating error (digit truncation in the ultrametric - the strong triangle inequality forbids error accumulation), and a smooth, certified training path connecting all of it (Maslov dequantization annealing).

CHILDREN: T1.1 Maslov dequantization annealing (the new training algorithm); T1.2 the valuation dictionary theorems + valued-attention reference mechanism; T1.3 Newton-polytope capacity theory; T1.4 p-adic precision as a quantization paradigm; T1.5 IFS=ultrametric unification + Moran capacity; T1.6 hyperbolic-boundary correspondence (Gromov product = LCP).

EVIDENCE DISCIPLINE: every child preregisters its predictions in the G1 hypothesis registry before benchmarking and ships numerical theorem-verification tests (exact where the math is exact, tolerance-bounded where floating point intrudes).
CHILD ADDITION (idea-wizard): T1.7 / 8gk.7 certified-robustness benchmark - the external-facing payoff of the tropical/Maslov certificate machinery: certified-accuracy-vs-eps curves against randomized smoothing at matched budget, with certification-cost accounting. It is the pillar's most legible deliverable to researchers outside this project's mathematical language.
CHILD ADDITIONS (idea-wizard round 2): T1.8 / 8gk.8 tropical 1-Lipschitz FFN - extends the semiring design axis past attention to the MLP (2/3 of parameters) and closes the 8gk.7 certified chain's one remaining hole; T1.9 / 8gk.9 route observatory - the discrete computation traces only these mechanisms emit (tropical routes, trie paths, braid words) become first-class extractable artifacts with reuse/stability/clustering analyses, the discrete complement to u55.2's continuous curvature.
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass incorporated): the unification claim must be packaged as a DEFINED FORMAL OBJECT, not as rhetoric. Replace 'five frameworks are one object' with the defensible-and-still-bold version: these mechanisms are all REPRESENTATIONS OF A VALUATION GEOMETRY - tokens/states are mapped into objects carrying a dominance valuation; attention is comparison of leading terms; routing is movement between valuation cells; hierarchy is the induced ultrametric/tree boundary. The definition itself (axioms of a 'valued attention structure' and the verification that each mechanism is an instance) is a first-class deliverable of 8gk.2. Referee consensus: Pillar I is the most immediately paperable piece of the entire program - children 8gk.1/8gk.2 should be sequenced first among theory work.

title: Maslov dequantization annealing: softmax-to-tropical as an exact semiring homotopy
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: For beta > 0 define x (+)_beta y = (1/beta) log(e^(beta x) + e^(beta y)). For EVERY finite beta this is a commutative, associative semiring operation on the reals, with + as multiplication, isomorphic to ordinary (R+, +, x) via x -> e^(beta x) (Maslov quantization). As beta -> infinity it converges uniformly to max, with explicit rate: max(x_i) <= ((+)_beta)_i x_i <= max(x_i) + log(n)/beta for n-fold combinations. Define LOG-DOMAIN ATTENTION entirely inside this semiring: out_d = [((+)_beta)_i (s_i + v_id)] - [((+)_beta)_i s_i] (the subtraction is semiring division = normalization). TWO ENDPOINT FACTS: (1) at beta -> infinity this converges EXACTLY to the codebase's tropical attention WITH score centering - tropical_score_center in tropical_attention_torch.py is the beta=infinity shadow of softmax normalization; the code already contains both endpoints of the family without knowing they are connected. (2) The whole family is 1-Lipschitz in sup-norm for every beta (LSE and max are both nonexpansive), so the tropical robustness certificate extends to FINITE beta: ROUTE-STABILITY LEMMA - if the runner-up margin gamma (already measured by tropical_record_margins) satisfies gamma > log(n)/beta, then beta-attention selects the same argmax route as tropical attention, and inherits its certificate. Proof is ~5 lines from the LSE-max sandwich; write it out in the theory note.

THE NEW TRAINING ALGORITHM - DEQUANTIZATION ANNEALING: train with a schedule beta: 1 -> infinity (a continuation/homotopy method with semiring semantics). Early training gets smooth gradients everywhere; the late-time limit gives certified piecewise-linear routing. The discrete limit is not a straight-through hack or an RL workaround - it is the EXACT algebraic limit of the smooth family, with a convergence rate and a certificate. This dissolves the soft-attention-vs-hard-routing dichotomy that the entire MoE/retrieval/discrete-decoding literature works around heuristically.

WHY LEAPFROG: every existing approach to hard routing (Gumbel-softmax, straight-through estimators, REINFORCE) approximates gradients of a discrete object. Here the discrete object is the limit of exact semirings, the interpolation parameter is one scalar with algebraic meaning, route stability is certifiable DURING training (watch gamma vs log(n)/beta), and the endpoint model is 1-Lipschitz-certified by construction.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE (markdown_documentation/, house style): the semiring family and its axioms; the two endpoint theorems; the route-stability lemma with proof; convergence rates; relation of beta to softmax temperature (beta = 1/tau, but the LOG-DOMAIN aggregation differs from linear-domain softmax averaging - state the precise relationship and why the log-domain form is the certificate-carrying one).
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --semiring-beta flag (float or schedule spec) on the tropical attention path in nanochat; beta=inf reproduces current tropical attention bitwise; beta finite uses the LSE form. Schedule support: linear, exponential, and ordinal-orchestrated (T3.3 client).
3. EXPERIMENTS (preregister in G1 first): anneal schedules vs fixed-beta vs POST-HOC SNAP (train soft, discretize at the end - the baseline everyone implicitly uses). Measure: loss retention of the annealed-to-tropical model vs its beta=1 starting point at matched budget (predict >= 95% retention); certificate coverage = fraction of eval tokens with nonvacuous route-stability margins (predict annealed >> post-hoc snap); robustness under eps-bounded perturbations (tie to the C3 tropical robustness prediction).
4. METRICS: log beta and certificate coverage per log_interval into metrics.jsonl (D2 schema gains two fields).

TESTS & LOGGING (numerical theorem checks - the math is the spec):
- Semiring axiom property tests at random beta: associativity, commutativity, distributivity of + over (+)_beta, to fp tolerance scaled by magnitude.
- Endpoint convergence test: |LSE_beta - max| <= log(n)/beta asserted across random inputs and n (this inequality is EXACT - any violation is an implementation bug).
- Route-stability lemma verified empirically: construct inputs with gamma > log(n)/beta, assert argmax routes identical to tropical; construct gamma below threshold, observe (and log) route divergence is possible.
- beta=inf code path bitwise-equals the existing tropical implementation (regression-protect the endpoint).
- Training-loop e2e smoke: 200-step CPU run with a beta schedule; metrics stream contains monotone beta and coverage fields; rich live logging shows beta, gamma distribution, coverage.

RELATION: tropical_attention_torch.py (both endpoints live there); A7 Triton kernel (note added: kernel should support finite-beta mode); T3.3 (ordinal orchestration of the schedule is its first client); G1/C3 (preregistration); C4 (the annealed variant joins the scorecard as its own row).

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

POSITIONING ADDENDA for the theory note:
(a) MODERN HOPFIELD CORRESPONDENCE: the update rule of modern Hopfield networks (Ramsauer et al.) IS softmax attention, and its beta -> infinity limit performs exact nearest-pattern retrieval - i.e., modern Hopfield theory already lives on the two ENDPOINTS of our (+)_beta homotopy; dequantization annealing is zero-temperature annealing of an attention-Hopfield energy, and the route-stability lemma bounds when retrieval basins freeze. One section in the theory note stating the correspondence precisely - it imports an entire literature's intuition for free and sharpens positioning.
(b) LINEAR ATTENTION AS A THIRD SEMIRING POINT: kernelized linear attention drops the log/exp entirely (it computes in (R+, +, x) before normalization) - the semiring-family viewpoint places softmax (Maslov at finite beta), linear (untransformed semiring), and tropical (beta = infinity) as three semiring choices on one axis. One paragraph; no implementation obligation; it makes the "semiring as design axis" thesis concrete against the architecture the field already uses.

CONSUMERS (cross-references added): the certified-robustness benchmark (8gk.7) consumes this bead's margin/coverage machinery and the route-stability lemma directly - keep the coverage metric's JSON shape stable for it. The route-stability lemma is formalization target #1 in the FORMAL epic (vnl.2; theorem-registry id thm-route-stability) - if the Lean statement-capture review (vnl.2 item 3) finds a missing hypothesis (e.g. tie handling), THIS bead's theory note inherits the correction.
PRECISION NOTE (fresh-eyes pass): in the route-stability lemma, the log(n)/beta threshold's n is the INNER aggregation arity (the number of terms inside each route's LSE - e.g. head_dim for score aggregation), NOT the number of routes; and the lemma's honest form is two-family (tropical scores x vs smoothed scores y with x_i <= y_i <= x_i + log(m)/beta, margin gamma(x) > log(m)/beta implies argmax preserved - the inflation is one-sided because LSE never underestimates max). The theory note must state it this way; vnl.2 formalizes exactly this form, and the certificate-coverage metric should compare gamma against the correctly-scoped arity.
ROUND-2 ADDITIONS (idea-wizard):
(c) OPTIMAL-TRANSPORT POSITIONING (third positioning paragraph for the theory note): Sinkhorn/entropic-OT attention lives INSIDE this semiring family - Sinkhorn iteration is alternating row/column (+)_beta-normalization, the entropic regularization parameter IS 1/beta, and the beta -> infinity limit of doubly-normalized attention is exact ASSIGNMENT (a permutation, by Birkhoff-von-Neumann) - i.e. the tropical limit of Sinkhorn attention is learned matching, which connects Pillar I's semiring homotopy to the braid/permutation machinery of Pillar II (u55.3) and imports the OT-attention literature (Sinkhorn transformers) as another point on OUR axis. One section, no implementation obligation; if a doubly-normalized variant is ever built it gets its own bead.
(d) CLOSED-LOOP COVERAGE-FEEDBACK SCHEDULE (fourth schedule mode, joining linear/exponential/ordinal): anneal beta as fast as the certificate allows - controller raises beta only while measured route-stability coverage (the fraction of tokens with gamma > log(m)/beta) stays above a target floor, and backs off otherwise. This is certificate-driven control: the schedule consumes the 5ki.5 telemetry stream (gamma distributions are already designated tropical telemetry) and is expressible as a lab.3 orchestrator advance-predicate (its cleanest client after the basic anneal). PREREGISTER: closed-loop reaches a target (beta, coverage) operating point in fewer steps than the best open-loop schedule from the same grid, at equal final quality - and never violates the coverage floor (checkable from logs alone, like lab.3's budget-exactness claim). Tests: controller unit tests on synthetic gamma streams (raise/hold/back-off transitions exact); floor-violation assertion over recorded runs; integration smoke under the orchestrator.
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - MATHEMATICAL PRECISION FIX (apply before implementation): the route-stability lemma as phrased in the description conflates two separate facts. The correct certificate structure has THREE parts, each with different conditions:
(a) OUTPUT PROXIMITY (unconditional): |out_beta - out_inf| <= 2 log(n)/beta for every input - both LSE terms are within log(n)/beta of their max counterparts. No margin condition needed.
(b) MASS CONCENTRATION (the gamma condition's real content): the softmax weight on the top-scoring key satisfies w_max >= 1/(1 + (n-1) e^(-beta gamma)) >= 1 - n e^(-beta gamma). So the clean statement is: ROUTE MASS >= 1 - eps ONCE gamma >= log(n/eps)/beta. Note the score argmax itself is BETA-INDEPENDENT (softmax is monotone; the largest score always gets the largest weight) - do not write code that 'checks whether the argmax route matches tropical'; it always does. What changes with beta is how much output mass rides the dominant route.
(c) PERTURBATION ROBUSTNESS OF THE ROUTE (beta-independent): the argmax route is stable under sup-norm input perturbations smaller than gamma/2 - this is the tropical certificate and it holds at every beta.
Certificate coverage during annealing should therefore report: fraction of tokens with mass-concentration >= 1-eps (tracks beta progress) AND fraction with perturbation margin >= delta (tracks robustness). Two curves, not one. The theory note must state the lemma in this three-part form with the one-line proofs.

E2E CONTRACT: a dequantization-annealing mini-run (tiny config, CPU, ~200 steps, beta schedule to a large finite value + final snap) joins the rz8.8 e2e pipeline script as part of this bead's acceptance (Scenario 5 - see rz8.8 notes); the run must log both certificate-coverage curves and assert the endpoint model's tropical-mode parity.

POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass):
1. WORDING SHARPENING (adopt in the theory note and all claims): 'certified 1-Lipschitz combinatorial routing' is imprecise - the max VALUE map is 1-Lipschitz, but the argmax ROUTE map is globally discontinuous (it jumps at tropical hypersurfaces). The sharper, correct claim: PIECEWISE-LINEAR 1-LIPSCHITZ SCORE DYNAMICS plus MARGIN-CERTIFIED LOCALLY CONSTANT ROUTES. This is stronger, not weaker - it says exactly where the discontinuities live (the corner locus) and certifies constancy away from them.
2. TIGHT CONSTANT: the mass-concentration bound is p_top >= 1/(1 + (n-1) e^(-beta gamma)), so the eps-form threshold is gamma >= (1/beta) log((n-1)/eps) - use (n-1), not n, in the theory note and the certificate code (matching the round-2 note's structure, with the tight constant).
3. POSITIONING REQUIREMENT (the theory note must engage, not discover-in-review): Litvinov's Maslov-dequantization exposition (the exact h log(e^(u/h)+e^(v/h)) -> max deformation - our semiring family IS this picture applied to attention); the recent Tropical Attention literature (max-plus attention with approximation results for tropical dynamic-programming circuits) - state precisely what is NEW here relative to it: the exact finite-beta semiring HOMOTOPY as a training algorithm, the three-part route certificate, the annealing-vs-post-hoc-snap protocol, and the certificate-coverage observable. If the positioning cannot articulate a delta, the bead's scope shrinks honestly to 'independent rediscovery plus the annealing protocol' - say so rather than overclaim.
EOF
close_reason: Core contract delivered: (1) THEORY NOTE markdown_documentation/maslov_dequantization_annealing.md - semiring family + axioms, LSE-max sandwich, the two-family route-stability lemma in the precision-note form (inner arity, one-sided inflation, 5-line proof), beta=1/tau relationship with the Jensen gap to linear-domain softmax, and all three positioning sections (modern Hopfield endpoints + basin-freezing criterion, linear attention as the untransformed semiring, Sinkhorn/entropic-OT with the assignment limit). (2) IMPLEMENTATION: --semiring-beta (const | linear:B0:B1 | exp:B0:B1) on the tropical path; beta=None is the untouched exact endpoint - attention goldens recaptured with ONLY the new config key changed, zero trajectory drift; finite beta = normalized log-domain LSE aggregation; set_semiring_beta per-step hook. (3) METRICS: semiring_beta + certificate route_coverage per step (conservative compositional threshold (log D + log m)/beta, margins from tropical scores). (4) TESTS: 7 new (semiring axioms, exact sandwich inequality, adversarial lemma check, monotone endpoint convergence, bitwise-endpoint, coverage lifecycle, e2e anneal smoke with monotone beta ladder). SPUN OFF: 9qk3 experiments campaign (preregistered as hyp-maslov-anneal-loss-retention, blocked on rgyl arm-selector), 9jzb ordinal+closed-loop schedules (T3.3/lab.3 clients). Unblocks 8gk.7 (consumes margin/coverage machinery) and lab.3.

title: Tropical FFN experiment campaign (preregistration-gated: blocked on the G1 registry)
description:
WHY: 8gk.8 (tropical FFN) landed its implementation, certificates, theory-note section, and tests, but its experiment campaign (a)-(d) is PREREGISTRATION-GATED by the bead's own rules: predictions must enter the G1 registry (hij.1) BEFORE any run, and hij.1 has not landed. This bead carries the campaign so the discipline is preserved rather than violated.

WHAT (verbatim scope from 8gk.8's CONCRETE PROGRAM item 4, plus its enrichment):
(a) COMPONENT ISOLATION: standard-attention + tropical FFN vs all-standard at equal params on LM smoke + 2 C1 tasks (where does tropical structure pay - attention, FFN, or both? The 7b0.8 decomposition logic at block level).
(b) CERTIFIED PAYOFF: full-tropical model (attention + FFN) in the 8gk.7 benchmark - certified accuracy at the registered eps improves by >= the registered margin once the MLP hole closes; the full-tropical vs constrained-MLP row delta MEASURES the hole's cost (also registered).
(c) QUALITY COST: full-tropical within a registered tolerance of standard at equal params on >= 1 C1 task.
(d) NETWORK-WIDE ANNEALING: anneal --ffn-beta alongside the 8gk.1 attention schedule - trains stably where post-hoc full-network snap degrades by more than the registered threshold.
(e) RANK-SATURATION (from the d_ff enrichment): pure-mode quality saturates in d_ff once the data's routing table is rank-covered - register if adopted as a fourth prediction.

PROTOCOL: register all thresholds in G1 first (git timestamp); >= 3 seeds; fixed FLOPs; artifacts per house conventions; verdicts via G2 when available.

IMPLEMENTATION STATUS (everything needed already exists): --ffn-type/--ffn-beta in train.py; TropicalMLP with margins; certify entries tropical.ffn_lipschitz_1_sup_norm + ffn_collapse_single_layer; theory section "The tropical FFN" in the tropical doc; 12 property tests incl. the sandwich bound.

DEPENDS ON: hij.1 (registry - hard, the gate), 8gk.1 (the attention beta schedule for (d)), 8gk.7 (the benchmark for (b)). (a)/(c) need only hij.1 + vdc.1 tasks.

title: The valuation dictionary: p-adic = tropical = dominance, and the valued-attention reference mechanism
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: make precise, prove, and numerically verify the dictionary that unifies three frameworks:
(a) LCP IS VALUATION: for x, y in Z_p with base-p digit expansions, LCP(x,y) = v_p(x - y) exactly. The ultrametric mechanism's similarity is the valuation of a difference; its trie is a finite subtree of the Bruhat-Tits tree for PGL_2(Q_p).
(b) TROPICAL IS THE VALUATION SHADOW: v is a homomorphism from (Q_p, x) to (Z, +), and v(x + y) >= min(v(x), v(y)) with EQUALITY whenever v(x) != v(y) (no leading-term cancellation). Hence the TROPICALIZATION THEOREM FOR ATTENTION: for valuation-generic inputs, v(<q, k>_{Q_p}) = (min,+) inner product of the valuation vectors - TROPICAL ATTENTION COMPUTES EXACTLY THE VALUATION OF p-ADIC BILINEAR ATTENTION. The tie/cancellation locus (where the inequality is strict) is the tropical variety itself - the 'corner locus' where routes switch; this is not a defect but the geometry of decision boundaries.
(c) DOMINANCE IS VALUATION: over Puiseux/Hahn series fields (and the surreal numbers via Conway normal form, which IS a Hahn series representation), the valuation is leading-exponent extraction = the dominance order that the surreal demo probes. The surreal framework's dominant-balance machinery is tropicalization over a series field. One backbone - digits (Q_p), piecewise-linear geometry (tropical), asymptotics (Hahn/transseries) - three shadows.

THE MECHANISM - VALUED ATTENTION (reference design): keys/queries as finite-precision elements of Q_p (length-K digit vectors with genuine valuation arithmetic); scores = alpha^(v_p(<q,k> - c)) or -v_p(...) directly. Show the existing ultrametric mechanism (digit-similarity only) and tropical mechanism (valuation-arithmetic only) are its two projections. THE ALGORITHMIC PAYOFF: the Bruhat-Tits/Berkovich tree is the native data structure; nearest-ball and ball-intersection queries on the tree give EXACT sub-quadratic attention algorithms with correctness proofs in the ultrametric - directly answering the expected E3 finding that the current kernel-mode torch port is quadratic-with-better-constants. A ball-tree attention whose correctness is a theorem, not an approximation bound.

WHY LEAPFROG: hierarchical attention today is heuristic (LSH buckets, learned clustering, block sparsity) with approximation-and-pray guarantees. This gives hierarchy a SEMANTICS - the quantity computed is a valuation - and algorithms inherit exactness from the ultrametric. It also explains a mystery: WHY tropical structure appears wherever hierarchy does (it is the valuation image - they are the same thing seen twice).

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE with the three correspondence statements and proofs (the ingredients are classical; the assembly and the mechanism-level consequences are the contribution; the tropicalization-of-attention statement with its genericity condition deserves a careful proof including the measure of the cancellation locus under digit distributions).
2. NUMERICAL VERIFICATION SUITE: exact integer arithmetic, no tolerances - v(xy) = v(x)+v(y) always; v(x+y) = min generically with strictness exactly on constructed cancellation cases; LCP-equals-valuation on random digit vectors; trie scores vs valuation formula agreement (hard-digit mode must agree EXACTLY).
3. VALUED-ATTENTION reference implementation at demo level - extend the ultrametric JAX demo IN PLACE (per AGENTS.md) with a valued-attention section + rich side-by-side table showing one attention computation rendered in all three shadows (digits / tropical numbers / leading exponents).
4. BALL-TREE SUB-QUADRATIC PROTOTYPE: tree-based exact attention for hard-digit valued attention; complexity analysis; benchmark hook for E3 (this is the concrete content of E3's anticipated discovered-from bead 'sub-quadratic ultrametric training path' - coordinate).
5. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: ball-tree valued attention matches kernel-mode quality on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3) at >= 4x wall-clock advantage at T=4096; tie-locus density (fraction of cancellation events) is small and DECREASES under training (the model learns to avoid its own decision boundaries - measurable, novel observable).

TESTS & LOGGING: all dictionary identities as exact integer property tests (Hypothesis strategies over digit vectors, p in {2,3,5}); cancellation-locus tests constructed adversarially; cross-implementation agreement tests (trie vs formula vs tropical shadow); demo emits the three-shadow table with rich formatting; ball-tree prototype validated against brute-force attention exactly (hard digits) on randomized cases with seeds logged.

RELATION: ultrametric_worlds demo + ultrametric_attention_torch.py (the digit machinery); tropical demo (route extraction = corner locus); surreal demo (dominance engine); E3 (the sub-quadratic question); T1.4 (depends on this dictionary); T3.2 (the Hahn-series shadow feeds resurgent fitting).
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - NEW FIRST-CLASS DELIVERABLE: the theory note must open by DEFINING the common formal object, then prove the instances. Working definition to refine in-bead: a VALUED ATTENTION STRUCTURE is (V, Gamma, v, sim) where V is the representation space, Gamma a totally ordered value group, v: V x V -> Gamma a dominance valuation (ultrametric-inducing), and sim a monotone transform of v used for scoring; required axioms: the strong triangle property for v, monotonicity of sim, and compatibility of v with whatever algebraic operations the mechanism uses (the almost-homomorphism property). THEN the instance proofs: Q_p digits (Gamma = Z), tropical (Gamma = R, v = leading exponent), Hahn/surreal (Gamma = the exponent group, v = dominance), IFS cylinders (Gamma = R via radius products), tree/hyperbolic boundary (v = Gromov product). Each instance proof is short; their assembly is the theorem-shaped version of the unification, and it is what makes the epic's claim defensible in review rather than rhetorical. The capstone paper's Pillar I section opens with this definition.
close_reason: Completed: theory note (the_valuation_dictionary.md) defines the valued attention structure (V,Gamma,v,sim) + 5 instance proofs; tropicalization-of-attention theorem with exact genericity characterization + the 1/(p+1) cancellation-locus measure (new closed form, verified); valued-attention reference + three-shadow table + ball-tree exact sub-quadratic prototype in the ultrametric demo IN PLACE (== vs brute force, 17x-200x measured, E3 hook in last_diagnostics); 8 exact-integer Hypothesis property tests (p in 2,3,5); theorems.yaml entries filled/added (thm-valued-attention-structure, thm-balltree-exact-attention, tropicalization + valuation-arithmetic notes landed); 2 preregistered hypotheses (balltree speedup, tie-locus decrease) with named blockers. Follow-ons: 33dd (torch ball-tree port), y4r8 (route_coverage aggregates).

title: Newton-polytope capacity theory for tropical attention
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: a tropical polynomial f(x) = max_a (c_a + <a, x>) is piecewise linear; its linear regions are the cells of the regular subdivision of its Newton polytope New(f) = conv{a} induced by the lifting c_a; the number of regions is bounded by the number of vertices of the upper hull, hence by lattice-point counts. Known result (Zhang-Naitzat-Lim 2018): ReLU networks are tropical rational maps, and region-counting via polytopes recovers expressivity bounds (Montufar et al). WHAT IS NEW HERE: nobody has computed the polytope geometry of tropical ATTENTION - a composition of TWO max-plus bilinear stages (scores = max over d of q_d + k_d; outputs = max over keys of score + v). The key structural feature: the inner max ranges over KEYS - a DATA-DEPENDENT index set of size T - so regions multiply combinatorially with context length, unlike feedforward tropical layers whose monomial sets are fixed by the architecture. Derive: (1) the Newton polytope of the single-head tropical attention map (q, K, V) -> out as a function of (T, d); (2) region-count upper bounds via upper-hull vertex counts; (3) the MULTI-HEAD superposition law - regions of a sum of tropical maps are counted by MIXED subdivisions, i.e. Bernstein-style mixed-volume bounds: head superposition is mixed-volume accumulation, giving the first algebraic account of what adding heads buys in capacity terms.

CONJECTURE (falsifiable, countable): per-parameter linear-region count of tropical attention exceeds that of parameter-matched ReLU MLPs by a factor growing with T. If true, tropical attention is provably more region-efficient per parameter in long contexts - a constructive expressivity separation, not a circuit-class upper bound.

DESIGN COROLLARY - POLYHEDRAL ARCHITECTURE DESIGN: region capacity is maximized, for a fixed number of monomials, by point configurations in general position whose upper hulls have many vertices (cyclic-polytope-type configurations are extremal by the Upper Bound Theorem). Translation: choose the SUPPORT PATTERN of the score/value maps (which index sets appear in each max) as a designed combinatorial structure rather than dense default - architecture design by polyhedral combinatorics. Prototype: structured-support tropical attention vs dense at equal parameter count.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the attention-map polytope computation; region bounds with proofs; the mixed-volume multi-head law; the conjecture stated with its precise counting protocol.
2. EMPIRICAL REGION COUNTING at small scale: enumerate realized activation patterns (argmax routes - the tropical demo's route extraction machinery does exactly this; reuse it) of trained tropical nanochat models over large input samples; growth curves of realized regions vs T and vs parameters; compare to the theoretical bound (must respect it ALWAYS) and to parameter-matched ReLU baselines (the conjecture).
3. STRUCTURED-SUPPORT EXPERIMENT: cyclic-type support patterns vs dense, equal params, on >= 1 diagnostic task + LM smoke; preregister the prediction (structured >= dense).
4. REGISTRY: the conjecture and the design-corollary prediction, with counting protocols as the metric definitions.

TESTS & LOGGING: route-enumeration harness, deterministic seeds, exact counting (routes are discrete - no tolerances); BOUND-RESPECT ASSERTION: measured count <= theoretical bound in every configuration - a single violation falsifies the derivation and must fail the suite loudly (this is the strongest kind of test: the theory is the oracle); rich tables of measured-vs-bound curves; fixture-based regression on small exactly-solvable cases (T=2, d=2 where regions can be enumerated by hand and MUST match exactly).

RELATION: tropical demo route tracking; T1.1 (the finite-beta family smooths the same polytopes - region counts at finite beta interpolate; optional extension); C4 (capacity claims become scorecard predictions); existing bead qq7 (ablations) shares harness patterns.

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

TROPICAL RANK & FACTORIZATION (added program item 5): beyond region COUNTING, the polytope machinery enables tropical low-rank analysis of TRAINED attention: estimate the tropical rank of trained max-plus score matrices (define precisely which notion - Barvinok rank vs Kapranov rank vs tropical rank differ; pick the computationally accessible one and state the relationships). Low tropical rank means the routing table factors through few "route prototypes" - simultaneously a COMPRESSION handle (store r prototypes instead of the full table) and an INTERPRETABILITY handle (route prototypes are discrete, human-inspectable objects). Mini-experiment: rank-truncation-vs-quality curve on a trained tropical checkpoint (truncate scores to tropical rank r, measure loss/route-agreement degradation vs r). REGISTRY ADD-ON PREDICTION: trained tropical score matrices have tropical rank far below generic random matrices of the same shape (training compresses routing) - register the threshold and the rank-estimation protocol before measuring.
TEST FOR THE RANK ITEM (plan-space pass 2): rank-estimation machinery validated on CONSTRUCTED matrices with planted tropical factorizations of known rank r (build as max-plus products of r-column factors; the estimator must report <= r, and report exactly r on generic planted instances) plus known full-rank random matrices - the estimator gets its own oracle test before any trained-model number is believed; seeds and instances logged.
FFN EXTENSION NOTE (fresh-eyes pass; executes 8gk.8's "note it there"): when the tropical FFN (8gk.8) lands, the region-counting harness extends to FFN layers - their Newton-polytope geometry is the same machinery with a FIXED (architecture-determined) monomial set, the easier case this bead's attention analysis generalizes. Two specific connections: (a) the pure-mode FFN's collapsed map has Barvinok rank <= d_ff (8gk.8's enrichment), so the rank-estimation oracle tests here apply directly to trained FFN weight products - one estimator, two consumers; (b) full-network region counts (attention x FFN composition) give the first complete tropical-capacity picture of a transformer block - extend the bound-respect assertion to the composed map on tiny configs.

comments.author: PurpleEagle
comments.text: PITFALL found while validating (PurpleEagle, before writing): the region-count derivation must be in INPUT (x) space, NOT q-space with fixed K,V. Reason: a single output coord y_d = max_k[max_e(q_e + K_ke) + V_kd]. With K,V FIXED, every candidate route (k,e) has slope = basis vector e_hat_e (coeff 1 on q_e), so for each feature e only the single key k*(e)=argmax_k(K_ke+V_kd) ever wins -> y_d collapses to max_e(q_e + M_ed), at most D regions, NO T-growth. The combinatorial-in-T richness is REAL but lives in input space: q_i, k_j, v_j are all affine functions of the same input x in R^(T x d_model), so the T candidate routes per output have DISTINCT slopes (k_j depends on x_j), and regions in x-space grow with T. The note's Newton-polytope computation (item 1) must lift the configuration {(slope_a, intercept_a)} where slopes come from the COMPOSED q(x)/K(x)/V(x) maps, and the upper-hull/region bound is over that. Validating the corrected (input-space) counter + the mixed-volume multi-head law needs a clean uninterrupted session; returning to open (no work lost; tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.md is the house-style anchor). Score/aggregate form confirmed exact: score(q,k)=max_d(q_d+k_d), y_d=max_k(score+v_kd) (nanochat/tropical_attention_torch.py).

title: p-adic precision: quantization with non-accumulating error and Hensel-lift curricula
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: in p-adic arithmetic, truncating to k digits is metrically exact and STRUCTURALLY STABLE in a way float rounding is not. THE FLAT-ERROR LEMMA (3-line proof from the strong triangle inequality): if errors e_1, ..., e_N each have v_p(e_i) >= k (i.e., live below digit k), then v_p(sum e_i) >= k and v_p(prod (1+e_i) - 1) >= k - truncation error CANNOT ACCUMULATE under sums: N rounding errors of size p^-k sum to an error still of size p^-k, versus sqrt(N) growth (or worse) in archimedean floating point. Quantization error in the ultrametric world is FLAT in depth and width.
CONSEQUENCE - QUANTIZATION AS VALUATION TRUNCATION: for network components whose arithmetic is p-adic-native (ultrametric digit machinery, IFS addresses, integer-coefficient tropical scores), low-precision inference = digit truncation with a THEOREM about error non-accumulation, replacing per-tensor empirical heuristics. The honest boundary: the lemma governs the p-adic-native subcomputations; error leaks at the interface to archimedean parts (the float-valued V aggregation). Characterizing that interface precisely - which quantities cross the valuation boundary, and what error they carry - is a core deliverable, not a footnote.
HENSEL LIFTING AS CURRICULUM: solving f(x) = 0 mod p, then lifting the solution to mod p^2, p^3, ... is the p-adic Newton method, and each lift PRESERVES the previous residue exactly. Training translation: learn digit-0 structure first (a p-way quantized model), then refine digit-by-digit, with the guarantee that refinement never destroys coarse structure (the lift fixes the residue). Float curricula have no such invariant - early learning is routinely overwritten. Prediction: digit-wise curricula show monotone coarse-metric improvement (the digit-0 model's decisions are preserved as digits are added), trainable-vs-end-to-end at equal budget.
MAHLER BASIS: continuous functions Z_p -> Q_p have canonical expansions f = sum a_n * binomial(x, n) with a_n -> 0; truncation is canonical compression with certified ultrametric error. Use as the function class for heads operating on hierarchical addresses (the trie addresses ARE elements of Z_p) - a principled replacement for MLPs on tree positions.

WHY LEAPFROG: post-training quantization is the most economically important deployment problem in the field and is handled by calibration heuristics with empirical cliffs. A mechanism class whose precision axis carries theorems (flat error, residue preservation, canonical truncation) is a new paradigm even if it only governs the hierarchical components - and our scorecard can measure exactly how much of the network can live on the p-adic side.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: flat-error lemma + proof; the archimedean-interface analysis (where valuation guarantees end); Hensel-curriculum invariant statement; Mahler-basis head design.
2. IMPLEMENT digit-truncation inference for the ultrametric mechanism (--ultrametric-digits-k at eval time): quality-vs-k curves on C1 hierarchical tasks + LM loss, SAME checkpoints evaluated at multiple k, against float-quantization quality-vs-bits curves for the standard baseline (the comparison everyone understands).
3. HENSEL CURRICULUM PROTOTYPE on a small hierarchical task: train digit-0 model, lift (freeze residue, add digit-1 parameters), compare vs end-to-end; verify the residue-preservation invariant EXACTLY at each stage (the lifted model reduced mod p^j must reproduce the stage-j model's discrete decisions).
4. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: (a) digit-truncation curves are FLATTER than float-quantization curves (graceful vs cliff degradation - quantify as area-under-quality-curve); (b) Hensel curriculum matches end-to-end final quality at equal budget with monotone digit-wise improvement; (c) the flat-error lemma's prediction visible end-to-end: quality at digit-k truncation independent of model DEPTH for the p-adic-native path (depth-independence is the experimentally striking signature).

TESTS & LOGGING: exact valuation-bookkeeping tests (integer arithmetic, no tolerances) for the lemma under sums and products including adversarial cancellation cases; truncation-inference parity harness producing per-k rich curves with logged seeds/checkpoints; curriculum stage logs asserting exact residue preservation (a single violated residue fails the run loudly); interface-leak instrumentation: log the valuation of every quantity crossing the p-adic/float boundary so the leak budget is empirical, visible, and regression-tracked.

DEPENDS ON: T1.2 (the valuation dictionary and valued-attention reference supply the semantics and the digit machinery contract).
PRECISION NOTE (fresh-eyes pass): the flat-error lemma requires k >= 0 for its PRODUCT part (subset-product terms have valuation >= |S|*k, which is >= k only for k >= 0; the sum part holds for any k). In this bead's digit-truncation setting k is always a digit depth >= 1, so the hypothesis is automatic - but the theory note must state it explicitly. vnl.2 formalizes the lemma with this hypothesis; keep prose and formal statement aligned.
ROUND-2 ADDITION (idea-wizard) - KV-CACHE DIGIT TRUNCATION (program item 5): apply valuation truncation to the KV-CACHE at inference for the p-adic-native components: cached ultrametric digit keys truncated to k digits as context grows (oldest-first or uniformly), with the flat-error lemma guaranteeing the truncation error cannot compound across the thousands of cached entries a long context accumulates - the long-context memory-compression story float-quantized caches cannot tell (their per-entry errors accumulate across the reduction). Honest boundary as elsewhere in this bead: only the digit-keyed path inherits the theorem; the float V cache does not (measure the interface leak under truncation explicitly). EXPERIMENT: needle-in-haystack (C1 task 8) at T=4096 with cache digits swept k = full..2: accuracy-vs-cache-memory curves vs a float-quantized-cache baseline at matched bytes. PREREGISTER: flatter degradation for digit truncation at matched memory. Tests: truncation exactness bookkeeping (integer valuation tests on cache entries); byte-accounting test (claimed memory savings measured, not estimated); decode parity at k=full (truncation off == current behavior bitwise).
close_reason: Delivered: (1) flat-error lemma as exact Hypothesis tests (sum+product, adversarial signs, p in 2/3/5) + the k>=0 sharpness counterexample, wired into thm-flat-error; (2) digit-truncation inference (ultrametric_digits_k) across kernel/balltree/trie with bitwise parity at k=K and kernel==balltree under truncation, plus the generic mgr eval-tasks --model-override harness (overrides recorded in meta.checkpoint.model_config + provenance: per-k arms variant-selectable); (3) Hensel-lift curriculum with EXACT residue preservation (frozen_mark on lift-time nodes, loud no-op on frozen paths, bit-identity asserted; toy reading: curriculum 0.160 vs e2e 0.177, disclosed); (4) theory note padic_precision.md (lemma + sharp hypothesis, archimedean-interface boundary, Hensel invariant, Mahler design); (5) 3 preregistrations with named blockers; (6) first exploratory perplexity-vs-k curves on e1 soft-digit checkpoints: monotone, graceful (no cliff), large magnitude (1.6 -> 18-58 at k=1) - the hard-digit campaign (9qeq) tests the preregistered claims. Follow-ons: tcuy (float-quant baseline), 9qeq (hard-digit+depth campaign), 92jp (Mahler heads).

title: IFS = weighted ultrametric: unify fractal memory with the trie, capacity by Moran dimension
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the address space {1..m}^N of an iterated function system with contraction ratios r_i carries the cylinder metric d(a,b) = r^(LCP(a,b)) (uniform ratios) - an ULTRAMETRIC. With non-uniform ratios, d(a,b) = prod of r_(a_j) over the common prefix j <= LCP: a WEIGHTED ultrametric. PROPOSITION 1 (equivalence): FractalKV (iterated_function_systems demo, fractal_attention_torch.py routing) and the ultrametric trie are the SAME data structure - a valuation tree - differing only in radii conventions: trie = uniform r = 1/p; fractal = learned/structured radii. Non-uniform learned radii strictly generalize BOTH mechanisms. PROPOSITION 2 (capacity): the similarity dimension s solves the Moran equation sum_i r_i^s = 1 (for uniform ratios s = log m / log(1/r)); the number of eps-separated addresses scales as eps^(-s); hence MEMORY CAPACITY under a readout-noise floor eps is eps^(-s), with collision probability of random writes computable in closed form from cylinder measures. PROPOSITION 3 (zero interference): writes to disjoint cylinders are EXACTLY orthogonal in the ultrametric geometry - interference is zero, not small; the demo's catastrophic-forgetting benchmark results are a theorem, not an empirical tendency, whenever the addressing respects cylinder disjointness.
OPTIMAL MEMORY DESIGN: maximize capacity eps^(-s) subject to a Lipschitz/readout budget by choosing (m, r_i, depth) - a constrained optimization with closed-form structure via Lagrange multipliers on the Moran constraint. Imbalanced data wants imbalanced radii: branches carrying more probability mass should contract LESS (get more resolution); the optimal r_i profile is computable from the address distribution (mirror of Huffman/arithmetic coding, but for geometric memory - make the coding-theory analogy precise in the note: the Moran exponent plays the role of entropy).

WHY LEAPFROG: external-memory designs (kNN-LM, memory layers, product-key memories) tune capacity and collision behavior empirically. This gives capacity, interference, and distortion as THEOREMS with design knobs, unifies two of our mechanisms into one parameterized family (halving maintenance, doubling the evidence base per experiment), and imports a coding-theory design principle (radius allocation = bit allocation) nobody applies to memory geometry.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the three propositions with proofs; the optimal-radius derivation; the coding-theory dictionary.
2. UNIFICATION PLAN (plan, not execution): document how fractal_attention_torch.py routing and the ultrametric trie share the address structure; actual code merge is gated on certify (B1) + goldens and gets its own discovered-from bead when this note is accepted - respect the in-place/no-churn rules.
3. EXPERIMENTS: (a) capacity scaling: vary (m, r, depth) in the FractalKV demo, measure retrieval-accuracy cliff location, compare against the eps^(-s) prediction (the cliff should move EXACTLY as Moran predicts - strong quantitative test); (b) learned per-branch radii on imbalanced hierarchical data vs uniform radii (predict: learned wins, and the learned profile correlates with branch mass per the optimal-allocation formula - check the correlation, not just the win); (c) zero-interference verification at the implementation level.
4. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: cliff-location matches Moran within CI across a 3x3 (m, r) grid; learned radii beat uniform on imbalanced trees by >= preregistered margin; radius-mass correlation > 0.8.

TESTS & LOGGING: Monte Carlo dimension agreement test (box-counting dimension of generated attractors vs Moran formula within CI - validates the geometry layer); EXACT zero-interference test (disjoint-cylinder writes leave unrelated reads bitwise unchanged in the trie/KV implementation - any nonzero diff is a bug, no tolerance); capacity-cliff harness with rich curves (accuracy vs load, per configuration, predicted cliff overlaid); learned-radii training logs include the radius profile per epoch so its convergence toward the optimal allocation is visible in the metrics stream.

RELATION: iterated_function_systems demo (FractalKV + forgetting benchmark); ultrametric trie; T1.2 (both are valuation trees - this bead is the dictionary's fourth column); C1 task 3 (hierarchical retrieval is the natural testbed).
ROUND-2 NOTE (idea-wizard): PRACTICAL CONSUMER - the optimal-radius-allocation formula derived here is consumed by 77l.4 (geometry-informed initialization): measured branch-mass distributions from the data profiler initialize the fractal radii via this bead's formula. Keep the formula's interface explicit in the theory note (inputs: branch probability masses + readout budget; outputs: r_i profile) so 77l.4 can implement the mapping without re-deriving it.
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - RIGOR CAVEAT: the Moran-equation dimension and the eps^(-s) capacity law hold under the OPEN SET CONDITION (contraction images sufficiently separated). For LEARNED radii/maps, overlap is possible, and then the Moran s is an UPPER BOUND on the similarity dimension rather than the exact value. The theory note must state this, and the experiments must measure overlap (collision rate between sibling cylinders) alongside capacity so cliff-location deviations can be attributed correctly: deviation WITH overlap = expected (bound not tight); deviation WITHOUT overlap = theory problem (the interesting case - investigate before publishing). The trie/hard-digit instantiation satisfies OSC automatically (disjoint prefixes) - state that too: the zero-interference theorem is unconditional for the trie, OSC-conditional for general learned IFS.
ALSO: the weighted-ultrametric claim (non-uniform radii d(a,b) = product of r over the common prefix) needs its ultrametric-inequality proof written out in the note (it is true - the products agree along shared prefixes and shrink with depth - but the three-line argument should be explicit, not asserted).

title: Gromov product = LCP: hyperbolic attention as the continuous completion of ultrametric attention
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the Gromov product (x|y)_o = (d(x,o) + d(y,o) - d(x,y)) / 2 measures how long geodesics from o to x and from o to y travel together. PROPOSITION 1 (exact): in a rooted metric tree, (x|y)_root = depth of the deepest common ancestor of x and y - i.e., EXACTLY the LCP depth of their address strings. Tree boundaries carry the ultrametric d(xi, eta) = exp(-(xi|eta)); therefore ultrametric attention scores alpha^LCP(q,k) = exp(-(log 1/alpha) * (q|k)) are GROMOV-PRODUCT KERNELS - the ultrametric mechanism is boundary-of-a-tree attention, exactly. PROPOSITION 2 (the correction terms ARE semantics): in hyperbolic space, distance-based scores decompose as -d(q,k) = 2(q|k)_o - d(q,o) - d(k,o): a Gromov-product (hierarchy-overlap) term PLUS radial terms. The radial terms measure depth-in-hierarchy (generality vs specificity of a token's position); current hyperbolic-attention literature conflates the two. DESIGN: score = 2(q|k) - lambda_q |q| - lambda_k |k| with LEARNABLE radial gates lambda - separating 'how much do our hierarchies overlap' from 'how deep/specific are we', each with geometric meaning. PROPOSITION 3 (the homotopy): rescaled hyperbolic spaces converge to R-trees as curvature -> -infinity (delta-hyperbolicity delta -> 0; CAT(kappa) asymptotics), and at c -> 0 hyperbolic attention reduces to Euclidean dot-product attention (the F4 reduction). Hence ONE mechanism with learnable curvature c per head traverses: Euclidean attention (c=0) <-> hyperbolic attention (finite c) <-> tree/ultrametric LCP attention (c -> -infinity, rescaled). The planned three-way comparison (hyperbolic vs ultrametric vs fractal, F4/F6) collapses into a HOMOTOPY FAMILY: train with learnable c and READ OFF, per head and per depth, which geometry language actually wants - an interpretable dial instead of a benchmark bake-off.

WHY LEAPFROG: resolves the hierarchy-bias question empirically per-head rather than per-architecture, with degeneration theorems making the answer interpretable (the learned c VALUE means something; c collapsing to ~0 is the honest 'language is not hierarchical here' verdict and is explicitly welcomed). Also upgrades the F4 design from 'another hyperbolic attention' to the continuous completion of an exact discrete theory - reviewers can check the tree case by hand.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: Propositions 1-3 with proofs at the appropriate rigor (1 is exact and elementary - prove fully; 2 is an identity - derive; 3 cite the metric-geometry literature precisely and state the rescaling convention used); the radial-gating design and its invariances.
2. REQUIREMENTS INTO F4 (note added to mnn.4): scores in Gromov-product form with radial gates; learnable per-head curvature; the c -> -infinity degeneration to LCP as an acceptance test alongside the existing c -> 0 Euclidean reduction.
3. NUMERICAL DEGENERATION SUITE: explicit tree embeddings where (q|k) is computable exactly as an integer (assert Gromov product == LCP depth EXACTLY); curvature sweeps c in {0.001, 0.01, 0.1, 1, 10, 100, rescaled} with monotone-convergence assertions toward (a) standard attention outputs and (b) ultrametric attention outputs at the two ends.
4. OBSERVABLE: per-head learned c and per-head mean radial gate values into the D2 metrics stream - the headline interpretability readout (which heads go hierarchical, at which depths, on which data).
5. REGISTRY PREDICTIONS: on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3), >= 25% of heads learn |c| above the hierarchy threshold while flat-task controls collapse to ~0; radial gating beats pure-distance scores on depth-sensitive queries by a preregistered margin; the c -> -infinity-trained model matches dedicated ultrametric attention on the same task (the homotopy endpoint reproduces the specialist).

TESTS & LOGGING: exact integer tree-case test (no tolerances - Gromov product on constructed trees IS LCP); degeneration sweeps with per-c convergence curves logged and asserted monotone within tolerance; radial-gate ablation harness; fp32-islands policy inherited from F4 numerics section verified under bf16 autocast; rich per-head c/gate tables in eval reports.

DEPENDS ON: mnn.4 / F4 (the design doc absorbs these requirements - F4 is being raised to P2 accordingly).
RELATION: T1.2 (boundary ultrametric = the valuation picture; this bead closes the geometric side of the dictionary); F5/F6 (implementations inherit the upgraded design); existing ultrametric mechanism (the degeneration target).
close_reason: Theory note shipped: markdown_documentation/gromov_product_and_the_ultrametric_hyperbolic_bridge.md, claims-validated-before-written (Appendix A: Prop 1 Gromov==LCP exact over 31539 tree pairs; ultrametric kernel = Gromov-product kernel; Prop 2 identity to 4.4e-16; Prop 3 both degeneration limits with monotone-convergence receipts). KEY CONTRIBUTION beyond the bead: the distance-vs-energy fork - the raw-distance Gromov product -> |u|+|w|-|u-w| at c->0 (NOT the dot product), only the energy form -> u.w; the bead's literal score formula does not hit the standard-attention anchor it claims. Filed as a correction to mnn.4 sec 2.2 (its c->0 acceptance test would fail) and composes with the arcosh-free mnn.6 suggestion. Requirements 2/3 (into F4) already absorbed by mnn.4; the numerical degeneration suite is the F5/F6 certify spec (deferred to implementation - no mechanism exists to certify yet). Predictions registry-ready.

title: Certified-robustness benchmark: certified-accuracy-vs-eps from tropical/Maslov margins vs randomized smoothing
description:
WHY: certified adversarial robustness is a mature field with a standard headline metric - CERTIFIED ACCURACY AT RADIUS eps - and expensive incumbents: randomized smoothing (Cohen et al. 2019; thousands of noisy forward passes PER INPUT) and interval bound propagation (loose). The tropical mechanism's 1-Lipschitz property plus its measured runner-up margins gamma yield certificates ALGEBRAICALLY: a route (hence an argmax decision) provably cannot change under any sup-norm perturbation smaller than the margin allows - zero extra forward passes, exact, and extended to finite beta by 8gk.1's route-stability lemma. Nobody has published attention-mechanism-level certified accuracy derived from semiring structure. This bead runs the comparison the field already understands.

WHAT:
1. CERTIFICATE EXTRACTION (short derivation note in house style - the careful part): per-input end-to-end certified radius for a trained tropical (or finite-beta) checkpoint on classification-style C1 tasks (hierarchical-retrieval exact match, Dyck validity, wide-range comparison): compose per-layer Lipschitz bounds with the decision margin at the readout. Be explicit about the chain: which layers are 1-Lipschitz in which norm; where embedding/readout Lipschitz constants enter; what breaks the chain (MLP blocks - bound their constants or spectrally normalize them in the certified variant; that constraint's accuracy cost is part of the honest accounting, reported, not hidden).
2. BASELINE: randomized smoothing per the published protocol on the standard-attention twin at MATCHED training budget, matched eval inputs; report certified accuracy AND certification wall-clock for both methods (ours ~1 forward; theirs ~10^3 forwards - the cost asymmetry is half the story).
3. EMPIRICAL ATTACK FLOOR: PGD-style attack within each eps on both models; certified accuracy must lower-bound empirical robust accuracy everywhere - any violation is a bug in the certificate derivation and fails the suite loudly (the theory is the oracle).
4. CURVES + ARTIFACT: certified-accuracy-vs-eps per task (both methods + clean accuracy), certification-cost table, seeds/configs in summary.json; verdicts feed the registry.
5. PREREGISTER (G1, before running): (a) at every eps where tropical certified accuracy is within 5 points of smoothing's, certification cost is < 1/100th of smoothing's; (b) beta-annealed models (8gk.1) dominate post-hoc-snapped models in certified accuracy at every eps (the annealing story's robustness payoff - this is the prediction that makes 8gk.1's training algorithm earn external keep); (c) certified accuracy at the registered eps beats the undefended standard model's EMPIRICAL robust accuracy under PGD (certificates beating attacks, not just other certificates).

TESTS & LOGGING: CERTIFICATE-SOUNDNESS test (the load-bearing one): for sampled inputs, adversarially-guided perturbation search strictly inside the certified radius NEVER changes the decision - seeded, run per release; one counterexample fails everything; Lipschitz-chain unit tests per layer type (numerically measured constant <= claimed constant on random inputs, magnitude-swept); smoothing baseline sanity-checked against a published-protocol toy number; curves regenerable deterministically from checkpoints; rich progress panels and a final artifact manifest.

DEPS: 8gk.1 (margin/coverage machinery + route-stability lemma - hard), vdc.2 (eval/checkpoint plumbing - hard); parent 8gk epic. RELATION: vdc.1 (the eps-perturbation probe spec lives there - one spec, all consumers measure identically), vdc.3 (its tropical robustness prediction is the coarse version; this bead is the certified upgrade), 7b0.7 (kernel margin byproduct keeps extraction cheap at scale), vnl.2 (the lemma this leans on is formalization target #1).
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass): CLI SURFACE - extend cli.py IN PLACE; prefer a --robustness mode on mgr eval-tasks (it already owns checkpoint loading, task checkers, and the JSON schema) over a new top-level command; if the smoothing baseline's sampling loop makes that unwieldy, a dedicated subcommand is acceptable - decide in implementation and document why. Either way the output schema versions with the C2 contract so G2 can adjudicate the registered predictions mechanically.
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass 2): CLASSIFIER VIEW (define once, both methods certify the same decision function) - certificates are statements about a DECISION, so fix it precisely: the decision function is the argmax over the task's answer-token vocabulary subset at the readout position (the C1 checkers define the answer alphabet per task; greedy single-token answers in v1, multi-token answers reduced to their first token with the reduction documented). Both the margin-based certificate and the smoothing baseline certify exactly this function; the PGD attack floor attacks it. Without this pin-down the three numbers are not comparable and the bead's comparison collapses.
RELATION ADDITION (plan-space pass): 8gk.8 (tropical 1-Lipschitz FFN) closes this benchmark's one chain hole - when it lands, add the FULL-TROPICAL certified variant (tropical attention + tropical FFN, no MLP constraint tricks) as its own benchmark row alongside the constrained-MLP variant described above; the delta between those two rows measures exactly what the MLP hole costs, which is itself a registered quantity in 8gk.8's experiment (b). Until 8gk.8 lands, the constrained-MLP variant is the certified entry - do not block on it.

title: Tropical 1-Lipschitz FFN: extend the semiring axis to the MLP and close the certified chain end-to-end
description:
WHY (two gaps, one move): (1) the epic's thesis - the SEMIRING AS A DESIGN AXIS - currently stops at attention: the FFN blocks, roughly 2/3 of parameters, remain (+,x)+GELU in every model benchmarked, including every "tropical" model. (2) The certified-robustness chain (8gk.7) has exactly one hole: MLP blocks are not 1-Lipschitz, forcing spectral normalization or loose constants. A max-plus FFN closes both at once: y_j = max_d (W_jd + x_d) is nonexpansive in sup-norm BY CONSTRUCTION (a max of 1-Lipschitz affine-with-unit-slope maps is 1-Lipschitz), so tropical attention + tropical FFN composes into an end-to-end certified network with no normalization tricks.

THE MATH (derive in the note, with the constants honest):
- FUNCTION CLASS: stacked max-plus layers compose by max-plus matrix multiplication - a deep pure max-plus FFN collapses to ONE tropical-affine map (max-plus products of the weight matrices), so pure max-plus stacks gain NO depth expressivity; the expressive variant is TROPICAL-RATIONAL: y = maxplus_1(x) - maxplus_2(x), which reaches ALL piecewise-linear functions (Zhang-Naitzat-Lim) but is 2-Lipschitz per layer (difference of two 1-Lipschitz maps). State both modes with their exact constants; the certificate chain composes the DECLARED per-layer constants, and the honest accounting (1 vs 2 per layer, residual connections adding +1) is part of the deliverable, not a footnote.
- MASLOV SMOOTHING: the finite-beta version replaces max with (+)_beta (log-sum-exp) - the SAME homotopy as 8gk.1, so dequantization annealing extends NETWORK-WIDE: one beta schedule (or per-component override) anneals attention and FFN together to a fully certified piecewise-linear endpoint.
- POLYTOPE/CAPACITY: the FFN's linear-region geometry is exactly the Newton-polytope machinery of 8gk.3 (which is currently attention-only) - the region-counting harness extends to FFN with modest work; note it there.
- WIDTH SCALING: max over d_ff terms is EVT/Gumbel class - lab.1's tropical scaling row covers the FFN too; init must follow the EVT rules or wide-FFN comparisons inherit the confound lab.1 kills for attention.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: extend markdown_documentation/tropical_geometry_and_idempotent_algebra.md IN PLACE with an FFN section (function class, collapse theorem for pure stacks, tropical-rational expressivity, Lipschitz constants per mode incl. residual accounting, EVT init rule).
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --ffn-type {standard, tropical, tropical-rational} in nanochat (revise the MLP in gpt.py IN PLACE behind GPTConfig; pointwise per-position, no KV-cache interaction); --semiring-beta applies (shared with 8gk.1's flag; per-component override --ffn-beta optional); margins of the FFN maxes recorded under the tropical_record_margins pattern.
3. INIT: EVT-aware initialization for additive weights (coordinate with lab.1; document the chosen rule and its rationale in code).
4. EXPERIMENTS (preregister in G1 BEFORE running): (a) component isolation - standard-attention + tropical FFN vs all-standard at equal params on LM smoke + 2 C1 tasks (the 7b0.8 decomposition spirit at block level: where does tropical structure pay - attention, FFN, or both?); (b) certified payoff - full-tropical model in the 8gk.7 benchmark: certified accuracy at the registered eps improves by >= the registered margin once the MLP hole closes (the chain is only as strong as its weakest constant); (c) quality cost - full-tropical within a registered tolerance of standard at equal params on >= 1 C1 task; (d) network-wide annealing trains stably (no divergence) where post-hoc full-network snap degrades > registered threshold.
5. METRICS: per-layer measured Lipschitz lower bounds (random-direction probes - measured value must never exceed declared constant) and FFN gamma margins into the D2 stream.

TESTS & LOGGING: 1-LIPSCHITZ PROPERTY TEST (load-bearing): sup-norm perturbation sweep asserts |delta y| <= L_declared * |delta x| with L=1 for pure max-plus and L=2 for tropical-rational - EXACT inequality, any violation fails loudly; collapse test: a 3-layer pure max-plus stack equals its single collapsed tropical-affine map bitwise on random inputs (the collapse theorem is the oracle); beta=inf bitwise-equals the hard-max path (regression-protect the endpoint, mirroring 8gk.1); ties follow the deterministic first-index policy shared with tropical attention (asserted); gradcheck on subgradient paths (fp64, small shapes); B1 certify gains an ffn-lipschitz entry (measured constant vs declared); decode parity trivial but asserted (pointwise op); e2e: when 8gk.7 and this both land, the certified pipeline runs with the closed chain and the certified-accuracy delta is recorded.

DEPS: parent 8gk epic; NO hard blockers (the MLP swap is independent of the A1 attention refactor - different part of the block - and immediately implementable). RELATION: 8gk.1 (shared beta + annealing story), 8gk.7 (the chain this closes), 8gk.3 (region counting extends to FFN), lab.1 (EVT init rule), 7b0.8 (block-level decomposition logic), u55.5 (a nonexpansive FFN composes interestingly with the no-norm program - note the connection, do not scope-creep into it).
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass): BLOCK-LEVEL MECHANISM INTERACTIONS (define at config-validation time, with actionable errors): (a) GAUGE blocks replace the entire block including the MLP (A1 boundary decision) - --ffn-type does not apply to gauge layers; in heterogeneous schedules (A2) the flag applies per-layer only where a standard MLP exists. (b) REVERSIBLE wraps the MLP inside its G function - tropical FFN inside additive coupling is allowed (any G preserves invertibility) and the round-trip test must still pass; but in u55.5's SYMPLECTIC mode G must be the gradient of a scalar potential, which a max-plus FFN is not - symplectic + tropical-FFN is REJECTED at config time with an error naming both beads (a max-plus generating FUNCTION phi whose gradient is the kick would be the principled combination; note it as future work in the theory section, do not improvise it here). (c) SYNAPTIC (gpt_synaptic) has its own MLP path - document whether the flag applies or is rejected, decide in implementation. Tests: config-validation matrix covering (a)-(c) with the documented outcomes.
ENRICHMENTS (fresh-eyes pass):
- D_FF IS A TROPICAL-RANK BUDGET: the standard FFN shape (d_model -> d_ff -> d_model) in pure max-plus mode collapses to a single map whose matrix (W2 tropical-product W1) has BARVINOK RANK <= d_ff - the hidden width is not a feature count but a tropical low-rank budget, and the hidden max-plus units are exactly the "route prototypes" of 8gk.3's rank/factorization item. This gives the theory note a sharp statement of what pure-mode hidden width buys (rank, not depth), connects the FFN to the rank-estimation oracle tests in 8gk.3, and predicts pure-mode quality saturates in d_ff once the data's routing table is rank-covered (registrable if we want a fourth prediction).
- CONSTANT GROWTH UNDER STACKING: tropical-rational layers compound multiplicatively - L stacked rational layers declare a 2^L product (plus residual terms). The CERTIFIED configuration therefore wants pure max-plus FFNs or a single rational layer per block; the quality-vs-certificate tradeoff this forces is precisely what experiments (b)/(c) measure - state the 2^L arithmetic in the theory note so nobody discovers it during the chain derivation.
close_reason: Implementation package complete; experiment campaign split to 8gk.10 (discovered-from) to preserve the bead's own preregistration discipline (G1/hij.1 has not landed, so experiments (a)-(d) cannot run registered). DELIVERED: (1) TropicalMLP in tropical_attention_torch.py IN PLACE - pure max-plus mode (1-Lipschitz sup-norm BY CONSTRUCTION, fp64-certified) and tropical-rational mode (param-matched difference of two pure stacks, declared 2-Lipschitz, refuses collapse); tropical_maxplus_layer pure function; finite-beta Maslov smoothing via GPTConfig.ffn_beta (LSE-max sandwich verified as an EXACT-inequality test through both stages); collapsed_weight() exposing the single tropical-affine map (Barvinok rank <= d_ff). (2) EVT-AWARE INIT with an honest correction discovered DURING implementation: the Gumbel-location bias applies to STAGE 1 ONLY (unit-RMS normed-stream input); stage-2 input is the concentrated post-max distribution, so the unit-scale correction there overshoots by ~sqrt(2 ln d_ff) - caught by the init-centering test, fixed, documented in the class docstring and the theory note (finite-n constants explicitly delegated to lab.1's table; measured E[max_16 N(0,1)] ~ 1.77 vs asymptotic 2.35 noted). (3) gpt.py: GPTConfig.ffn_type/ffn_beta + _build_ffn dispatch (standard path AND inside reversible coupling per interaction rule b - any G preserves invertibility, forward verified); _validate_config rejects gauge+ffn_type (rule a, actionable error naming both beads) and bad beta. (4) train.py: --ffn-type/--ffn-beta with validation + warnings. (5) CERTIFY: tropical.ffn_lipschitz_1_sup_norm (fp64; fp32 was correctly REJECTED during development - rounding of x+d alone pushes the ratio ulps over 1) + tropical.ffn_collapse_single_layer (fp64, 1e-9); 7/7 tropical certificates green; _CERTIFY_NAMED_CHECKS synced (source-scan test enforces). (6) THEORY NOTE: section 8 'The tropical FFN' appended to the tropical doc; theorem registry updated - thm-maxplus-ffn-collapse and thm-maxplus-ffn-lipschitz now have real anchors + certify/pytest pointers (pending notes 29->27), thm-lse-max-sandwich and thm-evt-maxplus-concentration gained their first pytest pointers; mgr theorems validate --deep green. (7) TESTS: TestTropicalFFN in tests/test_mathematical_properties.py, 12 green - exact Lipschitz sweeps both modes, collapse oracle fp64 1e-12, sandwich bound, EVT centering + zeroed-bias control (1.77 physics), margin buffers + bitwise margins-path parity, pointwise decode parity, gradcheck, 30-step memorization trainability for both modes, 3 invalid-config rejections. Gates: 77 tests green across session suites, ruff clean, mypy clean (class-level buffer annotations fix the typed-method union issue). Parameter counts at d=32: standard 57344 / tropical 57664 / rational 57728 (EVT biases account for the delta).

title: Route observatory: discrete computation traces as first-class artifacts (mgr routes)
description:
WHY: this project's mechanisms emit something standard transformers cannot: DISCRETE, EXACT computation traces - tropical argmax routes (the corner-locus paths 8gk.3 counts), ultrametric trie paths, braid crossing words (discrete decoder, closed bead 23h), MoM top-1 routing decisions (7b0.3), Hensel digit decisions (8gk.4). Today each lives in its own demo or diagnostic buffer. Treating traces as FIRST-CLASS ARTIFACTS - extractable, storable, comparable, clusterable - creates a discrete-interpretability instrument that complements u55.2's continuous one: curvature asks WHERE order-sensitive computation happens; the observatory asks WHAT discrete program the model executed. DISTINCT FROM existing beads 7ow/92m/hi3, which render summary STATISTICS (entropy, diversity heatmaps); the observatory stores and analyzes the traces themselves. No mainstream interpretability method has access to exact discrete traces - they exist here only because the mechanisms are algebraically discrete; this is a payoff of the exotic math that costs nothing extra at inference.

WHAT: `mgr routes --checkpoint DIR --task X [--mechanism auto] [--sample N] [--seed S] [--out DIR]` (extend cli.py IN PLACE):
1. EXTRACTION CONTRACT: a per-mechanism extract_trace(model, batch) -> canonical trace structure (mechanism-tagged sequence of discrete decisions with layer/head/position coordinates; versioned schema). Backends: tropical (argmax route indices - the 7b0.7 kernel byproduct when available, brute-force recomputation otherwise), ultrametric (trie paths / LCP depths per query), braid (crossing words in discrete mode), MoM (per-token member choices). One contract, four backends, extensible to future mechanisms (clifford/hyperbolic have no discrete trace - the contract documents which mechanisms are traceable and why).
2. ANALYSES: (a) ROUTE REUSE - distribution of trace frequencies across inputs (do few routes serve many inputs?), reuse/entropy curves across training checkpoints; (b) STABILITY - trace edit-distance under the eps-perturbation probe (vdc.1's spec - one spec, all consumers) and under semantic paraphrase where the task defines one; (c) CLUSTERING - group inputs by trace similarity, measure cluster alignment with known task structure (e.g. retrieval depth) WITHOUT supervision, against an attention-entropy-clustering baseline.
3. PREREGISTER (G1, before measuring): (a) route reuse INCREASES with training - the route distribution's entropy falls as the model consolidates onto fewer discrete programs (checkpoint series provides the curve); (b) per-input route stability under eps-perturbation rank-correlates with that input's gamma margin (>= 0.5) - the margin IS the stability certificate, closing the loop between 8gk.1's theory and observed behavior; (c) trace clusters align with task structure better than the attention-entropy baseline on >= 1 C1 task (registered metric: adjusted Rand index against the task's latent labels).
4. ARTIFACTS: compact trace store (parquet/JSON per run, schema versioned) + analysis report (rich tables, cluster visualizations, reuse curves) regenerable from the trace store alone.

TESTS & LOGGING: extraction determinism (same checkpoint + input -> bitwise-identical trace); per-backend contract conformance (schema validation tests); tropical cross-check (extracted argmax equals brute-force recomputed argmax EXACTLY - the implementation is checked against the math); clustering reproducibility (fixed seeds, stable assignments); ARI machinery unit-tested on hand-computed fixtures; stability harness reuses the vdc.1 probe spec verbatim (asserted by importing its constants, not copying them); rich progress panels per analysis; failure of any backend degrades to a SKIPPED row with reason, never a crash of the whole run.

DEPS: rz8.1 (hard - traces are only interesting on TRAINED checkpoints, and the CLI loads via the checkpoint manager); parent 8gk epic. RELATION: 7b0.7 (kernel argmax byproduct - the cheap extraction path), 7b0.3 (MoM traces), 8gk.3 (region counting shares the route-extraction machinery - coordinate, do not duplicate), u55.2 (the continuous complement: run curvature and routes on the SAME inputs for the combined interpretability story - note added there is not needed, this bead owns the combination experiment), 7ow/92m/hi3 (statistics visualizers - the observatory feeds them richer data), vdc.1 (probe spec), G1 (preregistration).
PRECISION NOTE (fresh-eyes pass): prediction (b)'s "that input's gamma margin" means the MINIMUM margin along the input's extracted route (a route is as stable as its tightest decision; per-decision margins aggregate by min) - the registry entry pins this exact aggregation so the correlation is well-defined before measurement.

title: campaign: E2 braid-dyck decisive verdict at the off-floor rung (d128/L4 @ 3e14, from 83r6)
description:
PREREGISTERED PROTOCOL (committed before any E2 evidence exists; the rung comes from CyanCreek's 83r6 probe, whose runs stay OUT of evidence pools because the rung was selected on them):

ARMS: braid vs standard (the registered hyp-braid-dyck-depth-extrapolation claim: dyck held-out EM delta >= +0.05, min_seeds 3, floor 0.512). Corpus artifacts/diagnostics_e1/dyck; n_layer 4 / n_head 4 / n_kv_head 2 / n_embd 128 / seq 256 / batch 8; --target-flops 3e14 (the 83r6 rung where standard learns dyck: probe EMs 0.917/0.906 vs 0.625 prior); seeds 30-33 per arm (collision-free vs E1 run-ids which used 0-2/10-25); scripts/run_campaign.py frozen-worktree launch, topic e2-dyck.

EVAL: mgr eval-tasks --task dyck, eval seeds 0,1,2, run from the SAME frozen worktree (nm9j corollary: eval worktree >= checkpoint producer SHA).

ADAPTIVE RULE (fixed a priori, the only stopping rule): after wave-1 evals land, run mgr hypotheses power restricted to the E2 evidence; if achieved power for the registered +0.05 < 80%, train seeds 34-37 per arm BEFORE any adjudication. Adjudicate EXACTLY ONCE: mgr adjudicate -H hyp-braid-dyck-depth-extrapolation (never --all: the rmatrix drift movers belong to audit bead o85g). The E2 cohort (3e14) supersedes the floored E1 verdict automatically via the budget-cohort rule - this is the decisive test the sm47 arc showed seed expansion cannot deliver.

title: Auto regression gate
description:
