title: Capstone position paper: Valued fields, integrable composition, and the asymptotics of learning
description:
WHAT: the synthesis document - a flagship theory paper (markdown_documentation/ in-repo + arXiv-ready draft) assembling the three pillars into one coherent account of what this project discovered by taking esoteric mathematics seriously as ML design language:
PILLAR I (non-archimedean): hierarchy, sparsity, robustness certificates, and quantization are shadows of ONE structure - geometry over valued fields; softmax is Maslov quantization of the tropical skeleton; LCP is valuation; dominance is valuation; tree boundaries close the circle through hyperbolic geometry. The dequantization-annealing training algorithm and the flat-error quantization theorems as the practical fruits.
PILLAR II (Lie-theoretic): the residual stream is a connection; depth composes by BCH/Magnus (compressible when nilpotent); integrable mixing carries exactly conserved charges (protected memory, the word-problem benchmark); triality is the symmetry of attention's own trilinearity; symplectic coupling makes stability a theorem and normalization unnecessary.
PILLAR III (asymptotic): width scaling by concentration class (EVT-muP for max-plus - the field's comparisons are confounded without it); scaling breaks as resurgent nonperturbative sectors (predictable from below); training programs as well-founded ordinal descents (terminating by theorem).

THE DIFFERENTIATING FEATURE - EVIDENCE WITH RECEIPTS: every empirical claim in the paper cites its preregistered hypothesis id in the G1 registry and its adjudicated verdict from the G2 ledger, including the REFUTED ones - a theory paper whose experimental section is mechanically auditable from the repo. This format is itself a contribution (reviewers can re-run the adjudication).

STRUCTURE (draft outline - refine in-bead): 1. The thesis (math as design axis, not decoration); 2-4. The three pillars, each: dictionary/theorems -> mechanism consequences -> preregistered predictions -> ledger verdicts; 5. The cross-pillar syntheses (ordinal orchestration of Maslov annealing; Moran capacity feeding resurgent sector estimates; surreal magnitude-channel meeting symplectic no-norm); 6. Honest negative results and where the mathematics did NOT pay; 7. The research-loop methodology (G3) as reproducible practice.

POSITIONING SECTION (do this seriously): vs geometric deep learning (we add valuation/non-archimedean geometry, which GDL lacks entirely); vs tropical-NN literature (we add the semiring homotopy + attention polytopes); vs hyperbolic-NN literature (we add the Gromov=LCP exact dictionary); vs integrable-systems-in-ML scattered works (we add conserved-charge instrumentation + the word-problem protocol); vs muP (we add non-CLT classes); vs scaling-law fitting (we add the resurgent program).

ACCEPTANCE: complete draft with every cited verdict resolving to a real ledger entry; internal review pass for mathematical correctness (each pillar's key proofs checked independently - use fresh-eyes agent sessions per the repo's multi-agent practice); the repo's README gains the paper as the canonical statement of what this project is; submission-ready formatting.

DEPENDS ON: the pillar beads whose theory notes this synthesizes - 8gk.1 (Maslov), 8gk.2 (valuation dictionary), u55.1 (BCH/Magnus), u55.3 (integrable attention), lab.1 (NSA parameterization). Later results (scorecards, scaling verdicts) strengthen it but do not gate the draft - the paper ships with whatever the ledger holds and says so.

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

ADDENDA - three evidence threads added to the paper's scope (none gate the draft; each strengthens it if landed by draft time):
(a) MACHINE-CHECKED MATHEMATICS: cite the theorem registry (vnl.1) with per-theorem proof status; theorems marked lean-checked where vnl.2/vnl.3 landed. Machine-checked theorems + machine-adjudicated experiments is the complete "evidence with receipts" story - no ML theory paper currently offers both.
(b) THE GEOMETRY->MECHANISM PREDICTION MAP (GEO epic, 77l.2): if adjudicated by draft time, the predictive (not merely descriptive) scorecard is a cross-pillar synthesis: Pillar I supplies the geometric quantities, the profiler measures them, the map predicts the winners. Position as the project's answer to "when should anyone USE this?".
(c) CERTIFIED ROBUSTNESS (8gk.7): the certified-accuracy-vs-eps curves are Pillar I's most externally legible payoff - the one figure robustness researchers can evaluate without learning any of our language. Include the cost-asymmetry table.
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - FRAMING DIRECTIVE for the paper, adopted: do NOT sell '11 beautiful connections'. Package as THREE THEOREM-PRODUCING MACHINES: (I) valuation => routing certificates; (II) Lie/integrable composition => depth and memory invariants; (III) asymptotic regime separation => valid scaling comparisons. Open Pillar I with the formal valued-attention-structure definition (8gk.2 note). ORDER CLAIMS BY EVIDENTIAL STRENGTH, leading with the safest: (1) dequantization annealing with route certificates; (2) valuation geometry as the common abstraction; (3) p-adic/Hensel curricula; (4) BCH/commutator depth compression for constrained blocks + decay diagnostic for unconstrained; (5) the EVT scaling table. The high-risk claims (triality equivariance, YBE charge-decodable memory, prospective transseries break prediction) appear AFTER the safe core, each with its registered verdict and explicit risk framing; ordinal orchestration is presented as proof-discipline infrastructure, not as a core ML result. The wording disciplines from the round-3 notes propagate here: 'piecewise-linear 1-Lipschitz score dynamics + margin-certified locally constant routes' (not 'certified Lipschitz routing'); 'drift controlled by shadow Hamiltonian under coercivity' (not 'drift impossible'); the narrowed EVT confounding scope (lab.1 note). Required positioning section additions: Litvinov; Tropical Attention; Tensor Programs V; Baez Octonions; SympNets-class literature; R-matrix x ML works.

title: EPIC: Hypothesis ledger & operationalized AI-guided research loop
description:
WHY (background): the project's entire identity is "AI-guided research" - the genesis was GPT-5 Pro generating additional research directions and scoring its own proposals on a 0-1000 rubric (README sections "Genesis" and "The AI Self-Evaluation Framework"). Yet nothing in the repo operationalizes that loop: predictions live in README prose, verdicts live scattered across artifacts/ directories, and the cycle "AI proposes -> human+AI implement -> results update beliefs -> informed next proposals" has no machinery. Repos with benchmarks are common; a repo that PREREGISTERS its claims machine-readably and auto-adjudicates them against artifacts is novel research infrastructure - and it compounds, because every future experiment plugs into it.

WHAT (scope, see child beads):
1. G1: a versioned machine-readable hypothesis registry (statement, source/provenance incl. "model-proposed", prediction with exact metric path + comparator + threshold, status lifecycle open/supported/refuted/inconclusive/blocked, append-only verdict history). Seeded by encoding ALL existing claims from the README and the 11 framework docs (~25-30 entries).
2. G2: an automatic verdict engine (`mgr adjudicate`) that locates qualifying artifacts (summary.json, D2 metrics JSONL, C2 eval-tasks JSON, B1 certify JSON, E2 scaling fits), applies preregistered comparators with a CI-aware statistical policy, appends verdicts (never silently flips), and emits a ledger scoreboard report.
3. G3: the documented research-loop protocol (how AI-proposed hypotheses enter, get scored on the existing rubric, become beads via br with discovered-from links, get adjudicated) so future agents extend the registry rather than the README.

STRATEGIC VALUE: the most differentiating epic - it makes the project's meta-thesis (AI as research partner) executable rather than anecdotal, and it imposes preregistration discipline that makes every EP-C/EP-E result more credible.
CHILD ADDITION (idea-wizard round 2): G4 = hij.4 statistical integrity upgrade - power-gated verdicts (UNDERPOWERED qualifier; the engine tells you how many seeds you need) and ledger-level Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control ("N SUPPORTED, of which M survive FDR" as the mandatory headline). At 50+ registered hypotheses, per-comparison statistics stop protecting the project's credibility claim; this child is what keeps the receipts honest at scale.
close_reason: Epic complete - all four children closed: hij.1 (machine-readable hypothesis registry, 38 entries, append-only governance), hij.2 (verdict engine mgr adjudicate, policies ci-v1 through ci-v4 with floor gate / budget cohorts / lineage dedupe / variant selectors / power+FDR), hij.3 (docs/research_loop.md - the loop as an executable protocol with the braid-dyck worked example), hij.4 (power-gated verdicts + ledger-level FDR). The loop the README only narrated is now machinery: 8 supported / 3 refuted / 7 inconclusive verdicts adjudicated by the engine with receipts, registered-report discipline proven end to end (kgj1, 9qeq, 9qk3, 8h0e in flight)

title: Hypothesis registry + mgr hypotheses CLI (preregistration infrastructure)
description:
WHY: see EP-G epic rationale. The registry is the load-bearing artifact: claims live in prose today (README "Why It Matters" bullets, the Expected Performance table, the framework docs' predictions); none are machine-checkable, so none ever get formally confirmed or retired.

WHAT:
1. SCHEMA + STORAGE: a versioned registry file (hypotheses/registry.yaml - one new DATA file, not code proliferation; directory keeps future per-study files tidy). Entry schema:
   {id (stable slug), statement (one-sentence prose claim), mechanism(s), source {kind: human|model, provenance: e.g. "GPT-5 Pro initial self-scoring session, 2025" | "README v1 Expected Performance table"}, date_registered, prediction {metric_path (exact path into a named artifact schema: C2 eval JSON / D2 metrics / B1 certify JSON / E2 fits), comparator (>=, <=, delta-vs-baseline), baseline spec (mechanism + equal-FLOPs requirement), threshold/effect size, min_seeds, scale_caveats}, status (open|supported|refuted|inconclusive|blocked), evidence [artifact paths + run_ids], verdict_history [append-only {date, verdict, artifact refs, adjudicator: human|engine-version}]}.
2. SEEDING (the bulk of the work): encode ALL existing claims - the 11 frameworks' README "Why It Matters" bullets, the Expected Performance table, and the falsifiable-predictions sections of the framework docs (~25-30 entries). Each entry traceable to its prose source. Claims too vague to operationalize get status=open with prediction=null and a note "needs operationalization" - visible debt, not silent omission (C3 operationalizes the task-battery subset).
3. CLI: `mgr hypotheses list [--status S] [--mechanism M] / show ID / add (interactive or flags) / validate` - rich tables, --json mode for tooling. Validation: schema-check all entries, verify metric_path points at a registered artifact schema, reject duplicate ids.
4. GOVERNANCE: registry changes flow through normal git review; verdict_history is APPEND-ONLY (the validate command rejects history rewrites by comparing against the committed parent version when available).

IMPLEMENTATION NOTE: prefer stdlib dataclasses + pyyaml-free JSON if avoiding new deps matters - or YAML via a tiny vendored loader decision documented in-code; pydantic only if [server] extra is acceptable to require for the CLI path (decide in implementation, document why).

ACCEPTANCE: registry seeded with all existing claims; `mgr hypotheses validate` green and tested (malformed fixture rejected); README's speculative table REPLACED with a pointer to the registry + a generated summary table (the README stops accumulating untracked claims).
CROSS-LINK (from the FORMAL epic, fresh-eyes pass): (a) the schema gains an OPTIONAL theorem_refs field (list of theorem-registry ids from vnl.1's hypotheses/theorems.yaml) so an empirical hypothesis can cite the theorem that motivates it - one field, additive, no other coupling; (b) whichever of this bead and vnl.1 lands FIRST decides the serialization format and loader for BOTH registries (the two epistemic-state files must share one format/loader - two formats would be pointless maintenance surface); the second to land inherits the decision.
close_reason: Completed: hypotheses/registry.yaml seeded with 24 entries covering the README Expected Performance table, Why-It-Matters bullets, and the framework docs' falsifiable predictions - 15 operationalized against the schemas that landed this session (mgr.evaltasks.v1/mgr.telemetry.v1: metric_path + comparator + threshold_kind + equal-FLOPs baseline REQUIRED by validation + min_seeds + scale caveats), 9 with prediction:null + operationalization_note naming the exact blocker. Includes the registered NULL hypothesis (placebo) and the theory-program specificity controls. Format inherited from vnl.1 (YAML/PyYAML) per the cross-link rule; theorem_refs validated against theorems.yaml. CLI: mgr hypotheses list/show/validate/add - append-only governance enforced vs git HEAD (deletions/history rewrites/date edits rejected; statement morphing warns); add text-appends preserving comments with rollback on invalid. README speculative table REPLACED with registry pointer (acceptance). 17 tests; full suite 356 passed.

title: Automatic verdict engine (mgr adjudicate)
description:
WHY: a registry nobody adjudicates is a wish list. The engine closes the loop: artifacts in, verdicts out, deterministically and re-runnably - the mechanical half of "AI-guided research with receipts".

WHAT: `mgr adjudicate [--hypothesis ID | --all] [--artifacts DIR...] [--dry-run]`:
1. MATCHING: for each hypothesis, locate qualifying artifacts across the known schemas (summary.json, D2 metrics.jsonl, C2 eval-tasks JSON, B1 certify JSON, E2 scaling fits) by mechanism + metric_path + the prediction's qualification requirements (equal-FLOPs configs, min_seeds, matched data snapshot).
2. GATEKEEPING (garbage-in protection): if qualifying artifacts are missing or fail requirements (seed count too low, budgets mismatched), the verdict is BLOCKED with a machine-readable reason - the engine must REFUSE to adjudicate weakly rather than produce a soft verdict. This rule is the engine's integrity core; test it explicitly.
3. STATISTICAL POLICY: multi-seed mean + CI (bootstrap or t-based, documented); SUPPORTED if the CI clears the threshold in the predicted direction; REFUTED if it clears in the opposite direction; INCONCLUSIVE if the CI straddles. Policy version recorded in each verdict (so a future policy change cannot silently reinterpret history).
4. LEDGER SEMANTICS: append a verdict_history entry (date, verdict, evidence refs, engine version); NEVER mutate prior entries; status field updates to the latest verdict.
5. REPORT: the ledger scoreboard - markdown (and HTML reusing the regressions HTML/CSS machinery in cli.py): one row per hypothesis with status, latest evidence, and verdict history; sorted by status then mechanism. This is the public face of the project's epistemic state.
6. INTEGRATION: invoked as the final step by C4 (scorecard) and E2 (scaling report); also runnable standalone over any artifact tree.

ACCEPTANCE:
- End-to-end test with fixture artifacts driving one hypothesis to EACH verdict state (supported/refuted/inconclusive/blocked);
- determinism: same artifacts -> byte-identical verdicts (fixed bootstrap seed);
- append-only enforcement tested (attempted history rewrite detected by validate);
- first real adjudication run committed: the ledger report for whatever evidence exists at execution time (expect mostly BLOCKED - that snapshot of honest ignorance is itself valuable and should be committed).

DEPENDS ON: G1 (registry + schemas), D2 (metrics stream is the most common evidence source).
GATEKEEPING ADDITIONS (idea-wizard round 2): (a) TAINTED EVIDENCE - artifacts whose provenance block (rz8.2) shows git_dirty=true or missing provenance are refused for verdicts with a machine-readable BLOCKED reason ("evidence not attributable to a committed code state"); exploratory analyses may read them, verdicts may not. (b) UPGRADE PATH - power-gated verdicts and ledger-level FDR control land as a follow-on policy upgrade in hij.4 (kept separate so this engine ships with the simple CI policy first; hij.4's policy-version bump rules apply). Test additions: tainted-artifact fixture -> BLOCKED with the correct reason; clean-artifact twin -> adjudicates normally.
notes: GATEKEEPING ADDENDUM (from rz8.2, landed 2026-06-10): artifacts now carry a provenance block (metrics.jsonl header + summary.json 'provenance'): {git_sha, git_dirty, config_hash, data_snapshot_hash, tainted}. RULE for the verdict engine: tainted=true (dirty tree or no git at launch) means the artifact is REFUSED as adjudication evidence - emit verdict BLOCKED with reason 'tainted evidence (unattributable code state)'. Also enforce config_hash equality across the arms of an equal-FLOPs comparison (same resolved config except attention_type/scheduler under test).
close_reason: Completed: mgr adjudicate closes the receipts loop. Schema-indexed evidence matching (evaltasks/train; arm = change exactly your axis from the all-defaults baseline; FOR-ALL semantics for multi-mechanism entries, worst arm decides). Integrity core: REFUSAL with machine-readable BLOCKED reasons (no artifacts / insufficient seeds incl. >=2 CI floor / 5% budget tolerance / metric missing / TAINTED per rz8.2 provenance - eval-tasks summaries gained the provenance block here / not operationalized); refusals are report-only, never ledger writes. Policy ci-v1 stamped in every verdict: Welch-normal CI for absolute_delta, fixed-seed 10k bootstrap for ratio; supported/refuted/inconclusive by CI position vs the registered threshold. Ledger: text-surgery appends preserving comments, status update, full validation incl. append-only-vs-HEAD with atomic rollback. Report: console + report.md + verdicts.json. FIRST REAL RUN COMMITTED (2026-06-10-initial): 24/24 BLOCKED - the honest-ignorance baseline. Determinism: byte-identical verdicts.json (tested). HTML variant deferred (regressions machinery too coupled; rides with hij.4). 13 tests; full suite 373 passed.

title: AI-research-loop protocol documentation + worked example
description:
WHY: the meta-loop (AI proposes -> scores -> human+AI implement -> adjudicate -> beliefs update -> better proposals) is the project's actual thesis (README Genesis + AI Self-Evaluation Framework), but it currently exists only as origin-story prose. Documenting it as an executable protocol means future sessions and agents EXTEND the registry instead of accreting untracked claims in the README - and makes the loop reproducible by anyone.

WHAT: docs/research_loop.md (new doc file in the existing docs/ directory) covering:
1. THE LOOP, step by step: proposal (human- or model-originated) -> scoring on the existing 0-1000 rubric (theoretical novelty / practical feasibility / potential impact / mathematical rigor / implementation clarity - the README's dimensions, captured here as a REUSABLE PROMPT TEMPLATE with the exact wording used for AI scoring sessions) -> registration in the G1 registry with falsification criteria -> bead creation via br with discovered-from links (hypothesis id <-> bead id cross-referencing convention defined here) -> implementation -> G2 adjudication -> registry update -> next-cycle proposals informed by the ledger state.
2. CONVENTIONS: how hypothesis ids map to bead ids and git branches; where scoring transcripts get archived (provenance for model-proposed entries); when a REFUTED hypothesis spawns a revised successor vs gets retired.
3. WORKED EXAMPLE: one hypothesis walked through the complete cycle with real artifacts. Use whatever has actually completed by execution time - the tropical 1-Lipschitz certificate (B1) is the safe default (claim -> registry entry -> certify artifact -> adjudication -> SUPPORTED verdict in the ledger); a C4 scorecard row is the richer option if available.
4. AGENTS.md gains a short pointer section (a few lines, not duplication) so every future agent session discovers the protocol.

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete; the worked example uses REAL committed artifacts (no hypothetical paths); the scoring prompt template is copy-paste runnable; AGENTS.md pointer added.

DEPENDS ON: G1 (registry must exist; the worked example needs it). G2 strengthens the example but a manual adjudication is acceptable if G2 has not landed - note which was used.
close_reason: docs/research_loop.md: the loop as an executable protocol (propose, score with the verbatim README rubric as a reusable prompt template, register pre-evidence, bead, frozen-worktree campaign, ci-v4 adjudication, beliefs update); conventions table (id mapping, scoring-transcript archiving under artifacts/proposals/, refuted-to-successor rules with the kgj1 precedent); worked example = the full braid-dyck arc (pilot1 floor lesson, E1 supported-but-floored, sm47 seed-expansion failure quantified by the power instrument, 83r6 rung, 8h0e preregistration) + 9qk3 compact second; AGENTS.md pointer section added

title: Statistical integrity upgrade: power-gated verdicts + ledger-level FDR control (extends mgr adjudicate)
description:
WHY: the registry is heading past 50 preregistered hypotheses (hij.1 seeds ~25-30; vdc.3 adds ~15; GEO.2, C3 additions, and the theory beads add more). At that scale, two statistical failure modes threaten the project's CENTRAL credibility claim - "evidence with receipts":
(1) UNDERPOWERED ADJUDICATION: min_seeds=3 is convention, not power analysis. A SUPPORTED verdict from a test with 20% power to detect the registered effect is noise laundered as evidence - and a REFUTED verdict from the same test is worse (the effect may be real and undetectable at n=3).
(2) MULTIPLE COMPARISONS: with 50+ hypotheses adjudicated at alpha=0.05, 2-3 false SUPPORTED verdicts are EXPECTED under the global null. Per-hypothesis CIs do not address the family. The closed bead 7sl added Holm-Bonferroni WITHIN single benchmark reports; nothing governs the LEDGER - which is the unit reviewers will actually read.

WHAT (extends the G2 engine after hij.2 lands; all changes policy-versioned per hij.2's rules so prior verdicts are never silently reinterpreted):
1. POWER ANALYSIS: `mgr hypotheses power [--hypothesis ID | --all]`: from observed variance in existing qualifying artifacts (or a designated pilot-run set named in the registry entry), compute (a) achieved power for the registered effect size at the current seed count and (b) the seed count needed for 80% power. Rendered into the ledger per hypothesis ("3 seeds, 34% power - needs 8").
2. GATEKEEPING UPGRADE: the G2 engine gains the verdict qualifier UNDERPOWERED: a CI-clears-threshold result obtained at power below the configured floor (default 50%) is reported as SUPPORTED-UNDERPOWERED / REFUTED-UNDERPOWERED - a visible asterisk, never a clean verdict; BLOCKED reasons gain "insufficient seeds for registered power" so the fix is actionable (the engine tells you how many seeds to add).
3. LEDGER-LEVEL FDR: the scoreboard computes Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted q-values across ALL adjudicated hypotheses (the family = the whole ledger, recomputed at each adjudication run, FDR level and procedure recorded in the policy version). HEADLINE FORMAT (mandatory): "N SUPPORTED, of which M survive FDR at q=0.10" - both numbers always shown; the gap between them IS the honesty, and hiding it would be the exact sin the registry exists to prevent.
4. SCOPE NOTE: directional preregistration already buys protection (one-sided tests, no garden-of-forking-paths); say so in the policy doc - FDR is the backstop for the family size, not a substitute for preregistration.
5. COORDINATION: vdc.3 gains a note (lands with this bead): min_seeds in NEW registry entries should be power-derived once pilot variance exists; existing entries grandfathered at their conventional value until re-registered (append-only history preserved).

TESTS & LOGGING: power computation validated against closed-form two-sample normal cases (hand-computed fixtures, exact within numerical tolerance); BH procedure unit-tested against the canonical textbook example (known p-value vector -> known q-values, exact); UNDERPOWERED qualifier fixture test (synthetic high-variance artifacts -> qualifier appears; adequate-power artifacts -> clean verdict); determinism (fixed bootstrap seeds); ledger renders raw and FDR-adjusted columns side by side (snapshot-reviewed); policy-version bump test (verdict history from the previous policy version is preserved untouched and labeled - append-only semantics survive the upgrade).

DEPS: hij.2 (hard - this extends the verdict engine and its policy machinery); parent hij epic. RELATION: hij.1 (registry fields), vdc.3 (min_seeds policy note), vdc.4 + 77l.2 (the largest verdict families - primary beneficiaries), 7sl closed (the per-report precedent this generalizes), cbm (the paper reports the FDR-surviving count as its headline evidence figure).
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass): P-VALUE DERIVATION FOR FDR - Benjamini-Hochberg operates on p-values, which the engine's CI-based policy does not directly produce; define the derivation explicitly in the policy doc: per-hypothesis p-value from the same bootstrap machinery that builds the CIs (proportion of bootstrap resamples on the wrong side of the registered threshold, one-sided per the registered direction; deterministic with the fixed bootstrap seed; minimum resolvable p bounded by resample count - state it, and use enough resamples that q=0.10 decisions are not resolution-limited). The BH unit test then covers the full path: synthetic artifacts -> bootstrap p-values -> q-values -> headline counts, against hand-computed expectations.
REFINEMENT (plan-space pass): FDR FAMILY ACROSS POLICY VERSIONS - the BH family at each adjudication run consists of each hypothesis's LATEST verdict regardless of which policy version produced it (verdict history stays append-only and untouched); the scoreboard FLAGS any family mixing policy versions (a one-line notice naming the versions) so a reader knows the q-values aggregate heterogeneous statistical machinery. Re-adjudicating everything under the newest policy is the clean fix and is always available (G2 is deterministic and re-runnable over artifacts) - the flag tells you when it is worth doing.
close_reason: ci-v4 shipped: per-arm power (normal approx vs registered effect size) + n_for_80pct + UNDERPOWERED qualifier below 50% floor (statuses unchanged); one-sided p-values aligned with the CI rule (parametric t / deterministic bootstrap wrong-side proportion); BH q-values per run family with the mandatory two-number headline + mixed-policy notice; mgr hypotheses power command; registry header policy doc; vdc.3 power-derived-min_seeds note. Live validation: braid-dyck shows 51% power at n=25 (needs ~50) - reproduces the sm47 lesson as an instrument reading

title: Gauge block applies RoPE in wrong tensor layout (B,H,T,D) — forward crashes for n_head != T
description:
FOUND BY: the mgr certify causality check (model_guided_research-5ki.1), first run.

BUG: nanochat/gauge_block_torch.py forward() transposes q/k/v to (B, n_head, T, head_dim) BEFORE calling apply_rotary_emb, but apply_rotary_emb (model_utils.py) expects (B, T, H, D) with time at dim 1 - its cos/sin caches are shaped (1, T, 1, head_dim/2). Consequence: broadcasting fails with a RuntimeError for any configuration where n_head != T (i.e., every realistic configuration), so --attention-type gauge has been un-runnable in nanochat training; in the pathological case n_head == T it would silently compute WRONG rotations instead of crashing.

WHY NO TEST CAUGHT IT: tests/test_demos.py only asserts the gauge block REJECTS kv_cache (NotImplementedError), which raises before the RoPE line; no test ran a plain gauge forward pass.

FIX: apply RoPE in the (B, T, H, D) layout before transposing, matching every other mechanism module (see CausalSelfAttention in gpt.py:231-243 for the canonical order); move the inline apply_rotary_emb import to module top.

VERIFICATION: mgr certify -m gauge (causality + rotation checks) green; full certify suite green; a direct gauge forward smoke is now implicitly covered by the certify causality check which runs a real forward+backward.
close_reason: Fixed: RoPE now applied in (B,T,H,D) layout before transpose, matching the canonical order in gpt.CausalSelfAttention; apply_rotary_emb import moved to module top. Regression test test_certify_gauge_forward_runs added (uses n_head != T, the crashing case). Found by the first-ever mgr certify run.

title: Braid attention: add YBE-satisfying crossing law option
description: The braid doc formalism assumes a crossing map satisfying the set-theoretic Yang–Baxter equation (3-strand coherence) to make R3 sound. The current demo intentionally restricts to σ₁^k and uses a crossing_update that is invertible but not YBE. Add an optional crossing law (and minimal tests) that *does* satisfy YBE, so the demo can legitimately support broader braid words and R3 reductions when enabled. Keep the existing crossing_update as the fast restricted mode.
acceptance_criteria: New crossing law option included with proof-by-test that YBE holds on random samples; demo can run in both modes (restricted vs YBE); docs/report clarify which algebraic guarantees apply to each mode.
close_reason: Added optional YBE-valid crossing law (crossing_update_ybe) with decoder support and unit test proving R3/YBE on random samples; updated braid markdown docs to clarify restricted vs YBE guarantees and how to enable via BRAID_CROSSING_LAW.

title: First preregistered pilot campaign: train/eval/adjudicate the mechanism zoo on the diagnostic battery (CPU scale)
description: Run the receipts loop end-to-end for real, for the first time. SCOPE (CPU-feasible pilot, explicitly partial): arm x task combos covering 8 operationalized predictions - hier x {standard, ultrametric, fractal}, arith x {standard, surreal}, dyck x {standard, braid}, rot x {standard, quaternion}, rel x {standard, simplicial} - at equal --target-flops, 3 TRAINING seeds per arm (eval adds 3 eval seeds per artifact), all runs from a CLEAN committed tree (tainted evidence is refused by the engine). OUT OF SCOPE this round (stay BLOCKED): needle (tropical/ultrametric long-context), bag (gauge/reversible), placebo (needs all 10 arms), octonion (per-query loop too slow at seq 256), ordinal/hoss (train-schema arms). PREREQUISITE: train.py --data-dir flag to point training at a generated task corpus (the one infrastructure gap; small, tested). DELIVERABLES: campaign script + logs, eval artifacts, mgr adjudicate run committed (ledger updates + report), campaign summary documenting budget/scale caveats and any floor effects honestly. The verdicts are whatever the engine says - including refutations at this budget; history is append-only and E1-scale runs supersede.

LEDGER: 5 REFUTED (ultrametric/fractal-hier, surreal-arith, quaternion-rot, simplicial-rel; effect exactly 0, CI below +0.05), 1 INCONCLUSIVE (braid-dyck), 18 BLOCKED correctly. KEY FINDINGS: (1) the campaign's cross-arm sanity check caught a REAL eval bug before adjudication - EOT-separator gluing failed correct answers; fixed + regression-tested + evals re-run on the fixed commit; (2) the degenerate-policy regime: at this budget every mechanism converges to format + majority-answer (verified token-level), so refutations are honest but scoped - E1-scale runs append-supersede via the append-only ledger. Campaign report at artifacts/adjudications/pilot1/campaign.md. Registry: 5 refuted / 1 inconclusive / 9 open / 9 blocked, validate green.

title: Precision-axis mismatch follow-up: register fairer compression axis before evidence
description: tcuy's hyp-padic-truncation-graceful was honestly REFUTED (auc_ratio 1.017 vs registered >=1.1) for a PROTOCOL reason worth its own bead: the float-quantization cliff (b3 ppl 17, b2 ppl 82k on hier standard) lives at memory fractions 0.094/0.0625, BELOW the digit arm's floor of k=1/K=8 = 0.125, so the registered matched-memory common window [0.125, 1.0] cannot see it - within the window, float >=4 bits is also near-lossless. The digit arm IS flat to k=1 (ppl 2.56-2.64 across all 3 hard-digit seeds; the flat-error signature is real). Candidate fairer axes: (a) train hard-digit checkpoints at larger K (e.g. K=16) so k=1 reaches fraction 0.0625; (b) sweep float bits only within {4..32} and digits within the same fraction range with denser k grid; (c) digit-truncate WEIGHTS (per-tensor valuation truncation) to mirror what eval_weight_quant_bits does to the standard arm. Register the new hypothesis + prediction BEFORE any new evidence (see registry governance); do NOT morph hyp-padic-truncation-graceful.
close_reason: Complete: hyp-padic-truncation-graceful-k16 registered PRE-EVIDENCE (cd76796, before any K=16 checkpoint existed) then SUPPORTED by engine ci-v4 (auc_ratio_deepwindow effect 1.433, CI95 [1.371,1.496], p=0.0019, power 1.0, survives FDR q=0.1; 3 clean seed pairings). The fairer axis worked exactly as designed: K=16 digit truncation is flat to k=1 (fraction 1/16, worst +1.9% ppl) where float b2 sits at ppl 82k; deep window [1/16,1/4] via new precision-curve --window-hi. Pool disjointness from the refuted full-axis hypothesis via distinct observable key auc_ratio_deepwindow (engine hardening for the general case landed separately as bhjf by teammate). Taint episode (worktree-spawn untracked dir + concurrent edits) repaired with 8 -r2 re-runs; .claude/ now gitignored.

title: EPIC THEORY-III: Asymptotics of learning - infinitesimals, transseries, and well-founded training programs
description:
THE DISCOVERY: the project's 'infinity frameworks' (nonstandard analysis, surreal/transseries, ordinals) are not architecture components - they are the correct mathematical instruments for the theory of TRAINING itself, and they slot directly into this project's empirical program:

- NONSTANDARD ANALYSIS derives hyperparameter-transfer parameterizations (muP-style) by a clean criterion: take width hyperfinite, demand every activation and update have a finite non-infinitesimal STANDARD PART, and the init/LR exponents fall out as existence conditions. The payoff specific to US: our mechanisms are NOT iid-sum machines. Max-plus layers concentrate at sqrt(2 ln N) (extreme-value/Gumbel class), not sqrt(N) (CLT class); ultrametric digit-matching concentrates like branching processes; normed-algebra (quaternion/octonion/Clifford) layers are isometry-class. Each class has DIFFERENT correct scalings - meaning every width-varying comparison currently run anywhere (including our own planned scaling study) is silently confounded for non-CLT mechanisms. T3.1 derives the per-mechanism scaling table and validates it with coordinate checks; it is simultaneously a novel theory contribution (EVT-class parameterization) and the validity guarantee for EPIC-E.
- TRANSSERIES + RESURGENCE: single power laws cannot represent scaling 'breaks'; transseries (multi-power sectors plus exponentially small corrections) are the complete asymptotic language, and resurgence says the perturbative tail ENCODES the nonperturbative sectors - the smooth part of the curve knows where the break is. T3.2 builds the fitting machinery (the surreal demo's dominance engine is literally the right tool) and tests break-prediction on held-out scale: falsifiable, and valuable to anyone planning compute.
- ORDINALS: the theory program creates genuinely multi-phase training (Maslov beta-annealing, constraint tightening, digit curricula) with retries and restarts. Composing phases naively yields fragile pipelines with no guarantees. Cantor-normal-form ranks over program state give every event a strictly decreasing ordinal => the whole program PROVABLY terminates. T3.3 generalizes the existing ordinal LR scheduler into a program-level orchestration calculus and runs the T1.1 annealing as its first client.

LEAPFROG THESIS: the field tunes hyperparameters empirically, extrapolates power laws blindly, and shell-scripts its curricula. This epic replaces all three with theory that produces numbers: scaling exponents from standard-part criteria, break locations from resurgent structure, termination guarantees from well-foundedness.

CHILDREN: T3.1 NSA-derived parameterization per mechanism; T3.2 resurgent transseries scaling laws; T3.3 ordinal-orchestrated homotopy training.

title: Nonstandard-analysis parameterization: per-mechanism width scaling from the standard-part criterion
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: muP/abc-parameterizations (Yang-Hu Tensor Programs) determine the init-scale and learning-rate exponents that keep training well-behaved as width N grows, by demanding activations and updates stay Theta(1). NONSTANDARD ANALYSIS gives a cleaner route to the same equations and - crucially for us - a route that works for NON-GAUSSIAN-SUM layers: take N hyperfinite (an actual infinite integer in the hyperreal extension), compute the forward and backward pass with infinitesimals, and REQUIRE every activation and every per-step update to have a finite, non-infinitesimal STANDARD PART. The exponent equations fall out as existence conditions for st(.). The transfer principle guarantees the conclusions descend to large finite N.

THE PROJECT-SPECIFIC DISCOVERY: our mechanisms are NOT iid-sum machines, so they do not live in the CLT universality class that muP assumes - each has its own concentration class with DIFFERENT correct scalings:
(a) TROPICAL / MAX-PLUS: a max over N independent Gaussian-scale terms concentrates at sqrt(2 ln N) - EXTREME-VALUE (Gumbel) class, not sqrt(N) CLT class. Correct tropical parameterization carries LOG-WIDTH corrections; a max-plus layer initialized and stepped by CLT-muP rules is silently mis-scaled, and every tropical-vs-standard comparison across widths inherits that confound. Fluctuations around the max are Gumbel with O(1/ln N)-class corrections - derive the full (init exponent, LR exponent, multiplier) triple.
(b) ULTRAMETRIC / LCP: digit-match indicators multiply along depth-K prefixes - branching-process/geometric class; expected LCP depths scale logarithmically in the number of distinguishable keys; derive scalings for the digit projections and the alpha/beta temperature parameters.
(c) QUATERNION / OCTONION / CLIFFORD: normed-algebra products are isometries on the norm - multiplicative/isometry class; benign for forward scale, but rotor PARAMETERS take gradients through the exponential map, so their LR exponent differs from magnitude parameters - derive the split rule (this retroactively justifies or corrects the per-group LR splits already in the optimizer setup).
(d) HOSS: the OU macro-step couples step size delta to curvature scale; NSA frames SGD as a hyperfinite random walk whose standard part IS the SDE (Anderson's construction of Brownian motion) - the shadow-dynamics formalization becomes a section of this bead's theory note, putting the hyperreal demo's framing on rigorous footing.

DELIVERABLE - THE WIDTH-SCALING TABLE: for every mechanism in the repo: concentration class, init exponent, LR exponent, output multiplier, with derivations - then VERIFIED empirically by the standard muP acceptance test (coordinate checks).

WHY LEAPFROG: (i) parameterization theory for non-CLT layer classes does not exist in the literature - EVT-class muP is a novel contribution by itself; (ii) it is the VALIDITY GUARANTEE for this project's entire empirical program: EPIC-E scaling comparisons and every fixed-FLOPs A/B across widths are confounded for mis-parameterized mechanisms - this bead is what makes 'tropical scales better/worse than standard' a meaningful sentence.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the NSA framework section (hyperfinite width, transfer principle, standard-part criterion - self-contained, no NSA background assumed); per-mechanism derivations organized by concentration class; the HOSS shadow-SDE section; the width-scaling table.
2. COORDINATE-CHECK HARNESS: width ladder {64, 128, ..., 4096} on CPU/small GPU; per-mechanism activation-scale and update-scale vs N under (current, derived) parameterizations - flat log-log lines validate the derived exponents; the harness is generic over mechanisms (one config table).
3. WIRE the derived parameterizations into nanochat config as a per-mechanism multiplier table behind a flag (--parameterization {current, nsa}); default unchanged until E1 adopts it.
4. TRANSFER TEST (the muP acceptance standard): LR tuned at width 128 transfers to width 1024 within 2x for each mechanism under the derived parameterization, and FAILS to transfer under the wrong one where the classes differ (tropical is the showcase).
5. NOTES added to E1/E2: record parameterization used per run; sensitivity rerun on the smallest ladder before full sweeps.

TESTS & LOGGING: coordinate-check assertions (|slope| < tolerance under derived parameterization AND the test must FAIL under deliberately-wrong CLT scaling for tropical - assert the separation both directions; a parameterization test that cannot fail proves nothing); Gumbel-input unit test (empirical max of N standard Gaussians matches sqrt(2 ln N) asymptote within CI across N - validates the EVT ingredient independently of any network); per-mechanism harness logs rich scale-vs-width tables and the fitted slopes with CIs; transfer-test harness logs LR-sweep curves at both widths; ALL plots/exports deterministic-seeded.

RELATION: EPIC-E / w94.1 / w94.2 (the confound this kills); muon/adamw per-group LR machinery (the (c) split rule lands there); nonstandard_analysis demo + hoss_opt (the shadow-SDE section); G1 registry (the scaling-table predictions are registrable and falsifiable via coordinate checks).
SCOPE NOTE (plan-space pass): the width-scaling table gains a row for the TROPICAL FFN (8gk.8) - a max over d_ff additive terms is the same EVT/Gumbel concentration class as max-plus attention, so the (init exponent, LR exponent, multiplier) derivation for class (a) covers it with d_ff as the width variable; 8gk.8's init rule cites this row rather than re-deriving it. The coordinate-check harness should include one tropical-FFN configuration so the FFN row is validated empirically alongside the attention rows.
IMPLEMENTATION NOTE FROM 8gk.8 (discovered while building the tropical FFN's EVT init): (1) the Gumbel-location correction is PER-STAGE, not per-network - it applies where the stage input is unit-scale (the normed residual stream); a stage whose input is the concentrated POST-MAX distribution (Gumbel fluctuations ~1/sqrt(2 ln n), not unit scale) needs a much smaller (second-order) correction, and applying the unit-scale offset there overshoots by ~sqrt(2 ln n). The width-scaling table should give the rule per stage position. (2) Finite-n constants matter at practical widths: E[max of 16 N(0,1)] ~ 1.77 vs the asymptotic sqrt(2 ln 16) ~ 2.35 (25% off at n=16) - the table should carry the finite-n Gumbel location (or the standard correction terms), not just the leading asymptote, or smoke-scale validations will appear to refute correct theory.
notes:
POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - SCOPE NARROWING AND EXTENSION (both adopt):
1. NARROW: 'every width-varying comparison is confounded' overclaims. The precise statement: comparisons are confounded for MAX-LIKE mechanisms exactly when the varied quantity changes (i) the number of competitors in any max, or (ii) the score variance/correlation entering the max. CLT-class mechanisms at matched parameterization are fine. State it this way everywhere, including the w94.1/E1 note interpretation.
2. EXTEND - THE N-AXES ENUMERATION (this makes the theory MORE consequential, not less): the extreme-value scaling runs over EVERY max-axis, and tropical attention has several simultaneously: head_dim d (max over feature dimension in scores), CONTEXT LENGTH T (max over keys in aggregation), and head count via score variance under width scaling. The T-axis consequence is new and testable: tropical attention's effective routing temperature drifts with sqrt(2 ln T) as context grows - LENGTH EXTRAPOLATION for max-like mechanisms has an EVT correction that CLT reasoning misses entirely. Register this as its own prediction and cross-link: C5 (length extrapolation) and E3 (length scaling) gain an EVT-correction hypothesis - margin distributions shift with ln T, predictable from the theory before measurement; the C5/E3 reports should test for the sqrt(2 ln T) signature in the gamma (margin) statistics that tropical attention already logs.
3. Positioning: cite Tensor Programs V (Yang et al.) as the CLT-class baseline theory being extended, and the classical EVT/Gumbel literature for the max-asymptotics; the contribution statement is 'abc-parameterization for non-CLT concentration classes', which is currently absent from the literature.

title: Resurgent scaling laws: transseries fits that predict the break before you reach it
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: empirical scaling laws are fit as L(C) = a C^(-b) + c - a single perturbative term. This ansatz CANNOT represent regime changes: breaks, bends, emergent-capability kinks, data-exhaustion plateaus all live outside its function class, which is why power-law extrapolations get ambushed. TRANSSERIES are the mathematically complete asymptotic language: L(C) = sum_i a_i C^(-b_i) + sum_j e^(-S_j C^(theta_j)) C^(-beta_j) (+ log factors) - multiple power sectors PLUS exponentially small 'nonperturbative' sectors that are invisible at small compute and DOMINANT after a threshold: a break is precisely a nonperturbative sector waking up. RESURGENCE (Ecalle): for resurgent functions, the perturbative sector's coefficients grow factorially with a rate set by the nearest Borel-plane singularity - a_n ~ n! / S^n - so THE SMOOTH PART OF THE CURVE ENCODES THE LOCATION S OF THE BREAK. Alien calculus makes the encoding exact for genuinely resurgent systems.

THE CONJECTURE (falsifiable core - state it as a conjecture, test it as one): neural scaling curves are resurgent-like: Borel-Pade analysis of the perturbative fit residuals at SMALL compute predicts the location (and roughly the size) of the first nonperturbative correction at LARGER compute, better than unconstrained model selection among broken-power-law ansatze. If true even coarsely, compute planning changes: you forecast your own breaks from below. If false, the negative result delimits where physics-style asymptotics applies to learning curves - also worth publishing.

WHY THIS BELONGS TO THIS PROJECT: the surreal/transseries demo's dominance machinery (ordered comparison of asymptotic terms, dominant-balance extraction) IS the term-selection engine a transseries regressor needs - framework #10 finally gets a first-class empirical job. And the E1 mechanism ladders provide in-house multi-mechanism scaling data nobody else has: DO different mathematical structures have different sector structure? (Preregister: ultrametric on hierarchical data shows an EARLIER nonperturbative sector than standard attention - capacity-driven; the valuation tree saturates its address space at predictable scale - derive the heuristic estimate from T1.5 Moran capacity and register the number.)

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the transseries ansatz class for L(C); IDENTIFIABILITY ANALYSIS (which sectors are recoverable from k decades of compute at noise level sigma - this determines honest claims; synthetic experiments quantify it); the resurgence-constrained version (tie sector parameters to perturbative residual structure via Borel-Pade); honest scope: ML curves are not proven resurgent - the constraint is a hypothesis the validation ladder tests.
2. FITTING ENGINE: extend the surreal demo's dominance machinery into a transseries regression tool (term selection by dominance order + constrained nonlinear least squares; Borel-Pade module; full numerics documentation - this is delicate numerics, treat it as such).
3. VALIDATION LADDER (in order, each gating the next): (a) PLANTED-BREAK RECOVERY: synthetic curves with known sectors + noise - recover sector parameters blind, quantify the identifiability boundary; (b) PUBLIC DATA: published scaling-study points (Chinchilla-class): hold out the largest-compute points, predict the bend location, compare against single power law and against unconstrained broken-power-law fitting (the incumbent baselines); (c) IN-HOUSE: E1 mechanism ladders - per-mechanism sector tables, the ultrametric-early-sector prediction adjudicated.
4. REGISTRY: the break-prediction protocol (out-of-sample, quantified error metric: log-compute distance between predicted and realized bend) and the per-mechanism sector predictions.

TESTS & LOGGING: planted-break recovery suite as deterministic unit tests (seeded noise; recovery within stated CI across noise levels and sector separations - the METHOD's unit test, run in CI on small instances); Borel-Pade numerical-stability tests (perturbed-coefficient sensitivity bounded; pole-location stability across Pade orders logged); baseline-comparison harness producing rich tables (transseries vs power law vs broken-power-law, with out-of-sample errors and CIs); every fit emits a reproducibility manifest (data hash, ansatz, seeds, convergence diagnostics); failure modes logged loudly (non-convergence, boundary solutions) - a fit that silently returns garbage is the worst outcome for this bead's credibility.

DEPENDS ON: w94.1 / E1 (in-house ladders for phase (c); phases (a)-(b) start immediately - do not block on E1).
RELATION: surreal_numbers demo (the dominance engine); T1.2 (Hahn-series shadow - the same valuation mathematics); T1.5 (Moran capacity feeds the ultrametric sector estimate); E2 (note added: transseries joins its fit families); G1 registry.
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - THE BRUTAL FALSIFICATION STANDARD, adopted verbatim as the bead's acceptance bar: retrospective break-fitting is numerology; only PROSPECTIVE prediction counts. Protocol upgrade beyond held-out: for the in-house E1 ladders we control data collection, so run TRUE prospective tests - (1) fit the perturbative sector on the small rungs only; (2) infer the first nonperturbative sector (location, sign, shape); (3) COMMIT the prediction to the G1 registry (git timestamp = the lock) BEFORE the large rungs are launched; (4) run the large rungs; (5) adjudicate via G2. The public-data phase (b) remains held-out-style (we cannot re-run history) and is therefore explicitly labeled the weaker evidence tier in all reporting; the in-house prospective tier is the headline. Acceptance gains: at least one completed prospective cycle (prediction committed before compute spent, then adjudicated, EITHER verdict) - a REFUTED prospective prediction is full success for the methodology and must be reported with the same prominence as a SUPPORTED one.

title: Ordinal-orchestrated homotopy training: well-founded multi-phase programs with termination proofs
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the theory program creates genuinely MULTI-PHASE training procedures: Maslov beta-annealing (T1.1: smooth semiring -> tropical limit), constraint homotopies (T2.1: dense -> nilpotent generators; T2.5: unconstrained -> gradient-potential coupling), p-adic digit curricula (T1.4: coarse residue -> fine digits) - each with failure-triggered retries, annealing-rate adaptation, and restarts. Composed naively, such pipelines are shell-script science: no termination guarantee, no principled budget accounting, restarts that silently reset global progress. ORDINALS IN CANTOR NORMAL FORM are the correct control structure: assign the program state a rank rho = omega^2 * (phases remaining) + omega * (annealing level within phase) + (patience within level); design every event - advance, anneal, in-phase restart, phase-failure restart with tightened budget - to STRICTLY DECREASE rho. Well-foundedness of the ordinals then PROVES the program terminates, no matter which failure branches fire in which order. The hierarchy encodes budget semantics exactly: an in-phase restart burns omega-level budget without touching the omega^2 phase budget; a phase failure burns phase budget without resetting completed phases. This generalizes the existing nanochat/ordinal_scheduler.py (rho = omega^2 A + omega B + C over LEARNING RATE only) to PROGRAM-LEVEL orchestration - framework #6 graduating from a scheduler to a calculus.

WHY LEAPFROG: curricula, annealing schedules, and multi-stage recipes are ubiquitous and universally ad hoc - every lab has fragile pipeline scripts with hand-tuned retry logic and no guarantees. A well-founded orchestration calculus with a termination THEOREM, exact budget semantics, and auditable descent logs is simultaneously real engineering value and the first substantive payoff of ordinal mathematics in ML beyond LR restarts. It is also load-bearing for THIS project: T1.1's dequantization annealing is its first client, and the entire theory program's experiments become reproducible programs rather than babysat runs.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the schedule calculus - state space, event alphabet, the CNF rank function, the strict-descent requirement per event type, the termination theorem (proof: structural induction on CNF - short, write it fully); EXPRESSIVITY analysis: which adaptive policies are encodable under strict descent (adaptive patience: yes, within a level's budget; unbounded retry: no, by design - that is the point); composition rules (sequencing and nesting of orchestrated programs = ordinal arithmetic: sum for sequencing, products for nesting - make this precise, it is the elegant part).
2. IMPLEMENTATION: extend nanochat/ordinal_scheduler.py IN PLACE into a program orchestrator: phases registered with (advance_predicate, anneal_action, restart_policy) over the D2 metrics stream; the rank rho logged on EVERY event with before/after values; state_dict/load_state_dict for D1 checkpointing (an orchestrated program must survive preemption - rank and phase state resume exactly).
3. TERMINATION VERIFICATION of the implemented automaton: bounded exhaustive state-space check on small budgets (every reachable event sequence terminates) PLUS the structural proof in the note covering the general case; be explicit about which guarantees are machine-checked vs proved on paper.
4. CLIENTS: (a) T1.1 beta-annealing end-to-end on CPU under the orchestrator (the integration test); (b) D4 regime-shift recovery gains the program-level variant (phase = re-adaptation episode); (c) document the pattern for T1.4 digit curricula and T2.1/T2.5 constraint homotopies as orchestration recipes.
5. REGISTRY: orchestrated beta-annealing matches or beats hand-tuned monotone schedules at equal budget while never exceeding its declared budget (the budget-exactness claim is checkable from logs alone).

TESTS & LOGGING: RANK-DECREASE INVARIANT as a property test - randomized event sequences over randomized phase configurations: EVERY event strictly decreases rho (exact ordinal comparison on CNF tuples, no tolerances; a single non-decreasing event is a bug and fails loudly); termination fuzzing (adversarial predicate sequences on small budgets cannot produce nonterminating runs - bounded exhaustive); CNF arithmetic unit tests (ordinal comparison, the sequencing-sum and nesting-product laws on representative cases); resume test (kill mid-phase, restore via D1, assert rank/phase state identical and descent log seamless); EVENT LOG as a first-class artifact: every transition logged with (event type, trigger metric values, rho before -> after in CNF notation) - the descent must be auditable by a human reading the log; integration test: tiny T1.1 anneal under the orchestrator on CPU in CI-lite.

DEPENDS ON: 8gk.1 / T1.1 (first client and concrete test vehicle).
RELATION: ordinal_schedules demo + nanochat/ordinal_scheduler.py (the seed implementation); D1 (checkpoint integration), D2 (metrics predicates), D4 (scheduler bake-off gains the program-level entrant); T1.4 / T2.1 / T2.5 (future clients); G1 registry.
notes: POLISH ROUND 2 - IMPLEMENTATION TRAP TO PREVENT: ordinal addition is non-commutative, and the sequencing law must be stated with the correct order: for program A-then-B, the combined rank is rank(B) + rank(A) (the LATER program's ordinal on the LEFT in standard ordinal-arithmetic convention), so that descent through A's states decreases the right summand while B's budget rides unchanged, and the transition into B drops strictly below rank(B) + 0 = rank(B). Writing rank(A) + rank(B) breaks strict descent at the phase boundary (ordinal addition absorbs on the left: 1 + omega = omega). The CNF arithmetic unit tests must include exactly this regression case: a two-phase program where the wrong addition order would produce a non-decreasing event at the boundary - assert the implementation rejects/avoids it. Same care for the nesting-product law (omega-th iterations: rank(loop body) scaled on the correct side). This is precisely the kind of error a reviewer will hunt for - get it right in the theory note first.

title: EPIC: Frameworks #12-13 - Clifford (geometric algebra) & hyperbolic attention
description:
WHY (background): the project's proven growth pattern is dual implementation: markdown theory doc -> JAX demo with in-process property checks -> nanochat torch attention -> benchmarks. The three existing long-horizon framework epics (model_guided_research-bya category theory, model_guided_research-my1 topos theory, model_guided_research-7dm derived algebra) are deliberately speculative p4 placeholders with no concrete implementation path. This epic adds the two NEAREST-term, highest-confidence framework candidates:

#12 CLIFFORD / GEOMETRIC ALGEBRA: strictly generalizes the quaternion mechanism (Cl(0,2) is isomorphic to the quaternions - giving an exact reduction test) and relates to octonions; graded products (geometric = inner + wedge) give rotation-equivariant features in arbitrary dimension; there is an active literature to position against (Geometric Algebra Transformers, Clifford-group-equivariant networks), which makes the work legible to reviewers; it is the natural algebraic sibling of the gauge block.

#13 HYPERBOLIC ATTENTION: negative-curvature embedding geometry for hierarchies; complements ultrametric (CONTINUOUS hierarchy bias vs p-adic DISCRETE hierarchy bias) and fractal (self-similar addressing), enabling a three-way "which hierarchy bias wins" study on the hierarchical-retrieval diagnostic task (EP-C task 3) that, to our knowledge, no one has run.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): F1/F4 design docs in the house style of markdown_documentation/ (first-principles derivations, complexity analysis, falsifiable predictions sections that feed the EP-G registry); F2/F5 JAX demos with property checks registered in cli.py DEMOS; F3/F6 nanochat attention modules built on the EP-A AttentionCore scaffolding (F3 doubles as the refactor's "is adding a mechanism actually easy now?" acceptance test).

CONVENTIONS: new root demo file + new nanochat/*_attention_torch.py file per framework are justified under AGENTS.md (genuinely new functionality matching the established one-file-per-framework pattern). Predictions must be preregistered (EP-G) BEFORE benchmarking.

title: Clifford/geometric-algebra framework design doc (framework #12)
description:
WHY: see EP-F epic rationale - Clifford algebra is the nearest-term high-confidence framework addition: it strictly generalizes the quaternion mechanism (Cl(0,2) ~ H gives an EXACT reduction test), relates to octonions, has rotation-equivariance payoff in arbitrary dimension, and has an active literature to position against (which makes results legible).

WHAT: write markdown_documentation/clifford_algebra_and_geometric_attention.md in the established house style (the other 11 docs are 24-45KB: first-principles derivation -> connections to literature -> complexity analysis -> falsifiable predictions -> implementation plan):
- MATH: Clifford algebras Cl(p,q); graded structure (scalar/vector/bivector/...); geometric product = inner + wedge; rotors as even-grade elements generalizing quaternions; sandwich product R x R~ for rotations; grade projection operators.
- ALGEBRA CHOICE (analyze both, recommend one): Cl(3,0) - 8-dimensional, fits the head_dim%8 pattern octonion already uses, full 3D geometric semantics; vs Cl(2,0) - 4-dimensional, cheaper, fits head_dim%4. Decision criteria: parameter efficiency, multiplication-table cost, what the diagnostic tasks (C1 task 5) can actually detect.
- MECHANISM DESIGN: multivector-valued features; rotor-gated value aggregation generalizing the quaternion rotor-gate (quaternion_attention_torch.py y = Q*(K_conj*V) is the template). KEY CORRECTNESS ANCHOR: restricted to the even subalgebra of Cl(0,2), the mechanism must reduce EXACTLY to the existing quaternion attention - derive this reduction in an appendix symbolically; it becomes the F2/F3 acceptance test.
- POSITIONING: vs Geometric Algebra Transformer (Brehmer et al.) and Clifford-group-equivariant networks - state precisely what is DIFFERENT here (rotor-gate attention design + certificate-first validation + the scorecard methodology).
- PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS section (feeds C3/G1): e.g. "clifford beats quaternion on rotation-composition-with-translations by >= 2 points at equal FLOPs"; "norm preservation certificate holds to fp32 tolerance"; "matches quaternion on pure-rotation tasks (reduction sanity)".
- IMPLEMENTATION PLAN detailed enough that F2/F3 require zero further design decisions (basis-blade multiplication table generation, normalization strategy, initialization).

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete in house style; the quaternion-reduction identity verified symbolically (sympy scratch work acceptable, conclusion in the appendix); predictions written in registry-compatible form.
close_reason: markdown_documentation/clifford_algebra_and_geometric_attention.md complete in house style, claims-validated-before-written (Appendix A receipts: 512-triple associativity, even closure, the exact H-isomorphism i=e23/j=-e13/k=-e12 verified against the IMPLEMENTED qmul table, rotor normalizer exactness, the scores identity, unit-rotor norm preservation, central pseudoscalar). Cl(3,0) recommended over Cl(2,0) with decision table; mechanism = the production quaternion rotor-gate template generalized (scores stay fused dot-product by the reversion identity); quaternion reduction is the F2/F3 acceptance test with the temperature bookkeeping pinned; predictions registry-ready incl. a non-inferiority form; implementation plan leaves zero decisions (blade-table generation algorithm, fp32 islands, goldens-recapture reminder)

title: Clifford JAX demo with property checks (12th demo)
description:
WHY: house pipeline step 2: every framework gets a self-contained JAX demo with in-process property validation before production implementation. The demo is where the multiplication table and rotor machinery get debugged cheaply.

WHAT: new root-level demo module (new file justified: genuinely new framework, matching the established one-file-per-framework pattern), e.g. clifford_algebra_and_geometric_attention.py, registered as the 12th entry in cli.py DEMOS dict, with demo() entry point and __main__ guard per house pattern:
- Geometric product for the F1-chosen algebra with the basis-blade multiplication table BUILT PROGRAMMATICALLY (signs from blade reordering/contraction rules), not hand-typed - then verified against known identities (e1e2e1 = -e2 in Cl(3,0), etc.).
- PROPERTY CHECKS in-demo (house style - the demo prints a rich property-check table):
  1. quaternion-subalgebra reduction: embed random quaternions into the even subalgebra, geometric product must match qmul EXACTLY (the F1 appendix identity, now numerical);
  2. rotor norm preservation: R x R~ preserves vector norms for unit rotors;
  3. grade-projection orthogonality and completeness (sum of grade projections = identity);
  4. associativity of the geometric product (unlike octonions! - this contrast with the octonion demo is pedagogically valuable, say it in the output);
  5. rotor composition = rotation composition (R2R1 sandwich = composed rotation matrix).
- LEARNING TASK: small rotation-equivariant task - train on rotations near identity, test on large rotations, vs an unstructured MLP baseline; show the equivariance generalization gap (the demo's "why it matters" payoff).
- Rich console output throughout (CLAUDE.md philosophy); runtime < 2 min CPU.

TESTS: add to tests/test_demos.py parametrized module/demo lists; property checks mirrored as assertions in tests/test_mathematical_properties.py (house pattern observed for the other 11).

ACCEPTANCE: `mgr run clifford` clean with all property checks green; `mgr list` shows 12 demos; `mgr info clifford` renders the docstring; tests green.

DEPENDS ON: F1 (design decisions: algebra choice, table construction, normalization).

title: Clifford nanochat attention (production torch implementation)
Hyperbolic-attention framework design doc (framework #13)
description:
WHY: hyperbolic space is the CONTINUOUS hierarchy bias (negative curvature embeds trees with low distortion) where ultrametric is the p-adic DISCRETE one and fractal the self-similar one. Adding it enables the three-way "which hierarchy bias wins" study on hierarchical retrieval (C1 task 3) - the single most interesting comparative question this project can pose, and to our knowledge unrun anywhere.

WHAT: markdown_documentation/hyperbolic_geometry_and_negative_curvature_attention.md in house style:
- MATH: hyperbolic space H^n; model choice analysis - Poincare ball vs Lorentz/hyperboloid. RECOMMEND LORENTZ and justify: distances via Minkowski inner product avoid the Poincare boundary saturation that kills fp32 gradients (cite Nickel & Kiela 2018, "Learning Continuous Hierarchies in the Lorentz Model"); exp/log maps at a basepoint; parallel transport basics.
- MECHANISM DESIGN: q,k mapped to H^n via exp map at the origin; scores = -d_H(q,k)/tau (negative hyperbolic distance); value aggregation via tangent-space approximation of the Frechet mean (log-map values' weights -> weighted average in tangent space -> exp back); curvature c as a LEARNABLE per-head parameter. KEY REDUCTION ANCHOR: as c -> 0 the mechanism must converge to standard (Euclidean) attention - derive the limit; it becomes the F5/F6 acceptance test (the analogue of Clifford's quaternion reduction).
- NUMERICS SECTION (this doc's most important section): fp32 saturation radii; why Lorentz; the constraint manifold <x,x>_L = -1/c maintenance policy (projection after updates); clamping policy with explicit thresholds; bf16 policy (compute Minkowski products in fp32 under autocast).
- POSITIONING: vs existing hyperbolic attention literature (hyperbolic neural networks, HNN++, hyperbolic transformers) - what is different: the certificate-first methodology and the three-way hierarchy-bias comparison.
- PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS (feeds C3/G1): hyperbolic beats Euclidean standard on hierarchical retrieval at depth >= 4; the three-way ordering question stated as an explicit open hypothesis with adjudication criteria.
- IMPLEMENTATION PLAN sufficient for F5/F6 with zero further design decisions.

ACCEPTANCE: doc complete in house style with the numerics section reviewed extra carefully (this mechanism lives or dies on numerics); predictions registry-ready.
notes: THEORY-PROGRAM ADDENDUM (see 8gk.6 - raised this bead to P2): the design doc must incorporate the Gromov-product reformulation, which upgrades hyperbolic attention from 'framework #13' to the continuous completion of the ultrametric mechanism: (1) scores in Gromov-product form 2(q|k) - lambda_q d(q,o) - lambda_k d(k,o) with LEARNABLE radial gates (separating hierarchy-overlap from depth-in-hierarchy - the -d(q,k) form conflates them); (2) learnable per-head curvature c with BOTH degeneration acceptance tests: c -> 0 recovers Euclidean attention (already specified) AND rescaled c -> -infinity recovers ultrametric LCP attention (new - the exact tree identity Gromov product = LCP depth is the anchor; see 8gk.6 for the propositions and test design); (3) the per-head learned-c readout into the metrics stream is a first-class deliverable (the which-geometry-does-language-want observable), not an afterthought. F5/F6 inherit these requirements.
close_reason: Design doc complete at markdown_documentation/hyperbolic_geometry_and_negative_curvature_attention.md (8fb8bf3), numerics-first: all claims validated before writing. Lorentz recommended with fp32 receipts (Poincare saturates at d~20, Lorentz usable to d~80); Gromov-product scores with learnable radial gates (addendum req 1); BOTH degeneration acceptance tests designed and numerically witnessed - c->0 Euclidean (linear-in-c convergence, tol 1e-4 at c=1e-3) and rescaled c->inf ultrametric LCP (delta-hyperbolicity 1/sqrt(c) law + the exact ln(1/sin(theta/2)) ray witness = ln 2 at 60deg) (addendum req 2); per-head learned-c telemetry as first-class deliverable (addendum req 3); numerics section with explicit clamp thresholds and bf16 policy; positioning vs HNN/HNN++/hyperbolic transformers; 3 registry-ready predictions (three-way study as pairwise registrations + sizing probe protocol per dzor); F5/F6 implementation plan with zero further design decisions. 8gk.6, mnn.5, mnn.6 unblocked.

comments.author: PurpleEagle
comments.text: CORRECTION from 8gk.6 (PurpleEagle, numerically validated): mnn.4 section 2.2 claims the naive -d_H score reduces to DOT-PRODUCT (standard) attention as c->0. That is not exact for the score the doc defines. The raw-DISTANCE Gromov product 2(q|k)_o converges to |u|+|w|-|u-w| (a Laplacian/distance-kernel limit), NOT u.w. Only the ENERGY (squared-distance) Gromov product 2*G_c with D_c=-(1/c)(1+c<x,y>_L) converges to u.w exactly. Numerics (c:1->1e-5): distance form locks onto |u|+|w|-|u-w| (err 3.3e-7), energy form locks onto u.w (err 1.4e-6) - different limits. CONSEQUENCE: the section-2.2 acceptance test 'weights match Euclidean attention to 1e-4 at c=1e-3' will FAIL by ~0.5 in the logits if scores use raw distances and the reference is dot-product attention. FIX (composes with my earlier arcosh-free mnn.6 suggestion): score with energies - gets the exact dot-product anchor AND avoids arcosh in the gradient path; recover the distance Gromov product via the stable arsinh readout d=(2/sqrt c)arsinh(sqrt(c D/2)) for the tree-limit anchor + telemetry. Full proof + receipts: markdown_documentation/gromov_product_and_the_ultrametric_hyperbolic_bridge.md section 3.

title: Hyperbolic JAX demo with property checks (13th demo)
description:
WHY: house pipeline step 2 for framework #13 - debug the Lorentz-model machinery cheaply, with the property checks that make or break it (numerics) validated interactively before any torch port.

WHAT: new root demo module (file pattern justified as in F2), e.g. hyperbolic_geometry_and_negative_curvature_attention.py, registered as the 13th cli.py DEMOS entry:
- Lorentz-model primitives per F4: Minkowski inner product, d_H, exp/log maps at basepoint, constraint projection.
- PROPERTY CHECKS (rich table, house style):
  1. exp/log inverse round-trip: log(exp(v)) = v within tol across magnitudes (and document where fp32 breaks - that radius goes in the table as a measured value, echoing the F4 numerics section);
  2. Lorentz constraint maintenance: <x,x>_L = -1/c within tol under a short optimization loop;
  3. c -> 0 Euclidean limit: hyperbolic attention output converges to standard attention output as curvature vanishes (sweep c in {1, 0.1, 0.01, 0.001}, show monotone convergence);
  4. tree-embedding sanity (Sarkar-style): embed a random tree, verify distance distortion beats a Euclidean embedding of equal dimension (the classic hyperbolic win, demonstrated concretely);
  5. d_H satisfies the triangle inequality and exceeds Euclidean distance for far-apart points (curvature signature).
- LEARNING TASK: tree reconstruction from learned embeddings vs Euclidean baseline at equal dimension - the canonical demonstration.
- Runtime < 2 min CPU; rich output throughout.

TESTS: tests/test_demos.py registration; property assertions in tests/test_mathematical_properties.py.

ACCEPTANCE: `mgr run hyperbolic` clean, all checks green, 13 demos listed.

DEPENDS ON: F4 (design + numerics policy).

title: Hyperbolic nanochat attention (production torch implementation)

comments.author: PurpleEagle
comments.text: Implementation suggestion from the mnn.4 race (PurpleEagle): consider an arcosh-FREE forward path. Define chordal/radial energies D_c(x,y) = -(1/c)(1 + c<x,y>_L) = (2/c)sinh^2(sqrt(c) d/2) and R_c(x) = D_c(x,o) - pure bilinear forms, no transcendental in the score path; the energy Gromov product G = (R_q + R_k - D)/2 has the EXACT c->0 dot-product limit (2G -> u.w), and exact distances for telemetry/certs come from the stable arsinh readout d = (2/sqrt(c)) arsinh(sqrt(c D/2)) (no 1+eps cancellation, finite gradient at coincident points). The committed doc's acosh clamp policy (N2) then only applies to telemetry, never to training gradients.

title: Rich training dashboard + live insights
description:
- Design a rich console/HTML dashboard showing: loss/throughput/TFLOP/s, memory, LR, grad norm, NaN flags, feature flags (flex, math modules), and key math diagnostics (e.g., orthogonality error, tropical margin, ultrametric occupancy) updated during runs.
- Include small ASCII sparklines/heatmaps for quick terminal use; optional HTML export for deeper drill-down.
- Wire into telemetry schema and artifacts layout; keep overhead minimal when disabled.

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

PANEL ADDITION - invariant drift: consume the certificate-telemetry fields (bead 5ki.5; certs.* namespace in metrics.jsonl) as a dedicated dashboard panel: sparkline per designated invariant (orthogonality residual, rotor norm deviation, round-trip error, gamma-margin summary, energy/charge values from u55.5 when present) with warn-band highlighting when a value departs its trailing baseline band. This is the dashboard's most differentiating panel - no other training dashboard shows live mathematical-invariant health - and it costs nothing here because 5ki.5 produces the data; this bead only renders it.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard design + prototype running on short training loop; shows loss/throughput/TFLOP/s, memory, LR, grad norm, NaN flags, feature flags, plus ≥2 math diagnostics (e.g., orthogonality error, tropical margin); terminal + HTML mode; toggleable; outputs saved to artifacts/dashboard/.
close_reason: Shipped: TrainingDashboard in nanochat/report.py (the metrics/reporting home) - live rich console dashboard rendering the SAME record dicts MetricsStream writes (no second telemetry path, zero overhead when --dashboard off). Panels: loss/val/grad-norm/throughput sparklines with latching NaN flag, mechanism-aware feature flags, and the differentiating invariant-drift panel: ANY numeric scalar outside the core record keys is auto-adopted as a sparkline with trailing-band (mean+/-3sigma) drift highlighting - route_coverage, braid Q1/Q2, symplectic shadow energy, and future certs.* land with zero coupling. HTML snapshot to artifacts/dashboard/<run_id>.html. Smoke-tested live on a 12-step symplectic-tied training run; 3 unit tests (sparkline/NaN marker, drift band, full render + NaN latch + HTML export). 92m (per-head route heatmaps) unblocked.

title: Audit rmatrix verdict drift before any real adjudicate --all (s5-length-slope degenerate zero-variance flip; solvable-control-specificity refute flip)
description: PurpleEagle's heads-up (mail 16748, 2026-06-12): a dry-run adjudicate shows two recorded-INCONCLUSIVE rmatrix entries (u55.3 family, mine) recomputing differently from grown evidence pools: (1) hyp-rmatrix-s5-length-slope -> SUPPORTED with degenerate effect=-0 ci95=[-0,0] - zero-variance observable, likely constant-zero length-slope metrics flooding the pool from newer artifacts; verify artifact-by-artifact before trusting; (2) hyp-rmatrix-solvable-control-specificity -> REFUTED (effect -2.707). Supersession of the 71wp floor-effect hand-checks must be owner-deliberate. Audit pools, decide supersede vs pool hygiene, document in ledger. Do NOT run mgr adjudicate --all until resolved.
close_reason: Audit complete: NO VERDICT DRIFT EXISTS. The dry-run's 'movers' reproduce the 2026-06-11 mechanical entries EXACTLY (s5: supported effect=-0 ci95=[-0,0]; z60 control: refuted effect=-2.707) - the evidence pools did not materially change (v2/v3 re-evals carry slope values identical to the originals; lineage dedupe). Both entries already carry the registered floor-precondition hand-check downgrades to INCONCLUSIVE (adjudicator human:CyanCreek, 06-11): baseline held-out EM [0.0069-0.0139] sits below the recorded answer prior 0.0972, all rmatrix s5 slopes are exactly 0.0 (flat because dead), z60 ratio is sign-noise of near-zero slopes. The structural issue: slope metric paths have no engine floor gate (_adj_prior_path derives priors only for exact_match), so ANY future mechanical adjudication re-appends a vacuous verdict AFTER the hand-checks, flipping the standing status by recency. Fix filed as qtdq (slope-path floor gating); adjudicate --all remains gated until qtdq lands. Standing verdicts for both entries: the hand-check INCONCLUSIVEs.
