title: EPIC: Training infrastructure hardening
description:
WHY (background): code investigation found several infrastructure gaps that undermine every experiment built on top:
- nanochat/checkpoint_manager.py is fully implemented (save/load with model_/optim_/meta_ files, per-rank optimizer shards) but ORPHANED - train.py never calls it. Any interrupted run is lost; eval-from-checkpoint and scaling sweeps are impossible.
- No structured per-step metrics stream - only console logs plus an end-of-run summary.json. Dashboards, scaling fits, and automatic verdicts all need step-resolved data.
- hoss_opt_torch.py is a stub compared to the JAX hoss_opt.py (which has symmetric Lanczos curvature via HVP and the analytic OU macro-step with Lyapunov integral). The "3 optimizers" story is currently dishonest in torch.
- scheduler-type only offers none|ordinal: the ordinal scheduler has no modern comparator (cosine/WSD), so its claims are tested against a strawman.
- Zero end-to-end generation/inference CLI - a "production transformer" that has never generated text for a user.
- 29 undocumented env-var feature flags across the JAX demos with no registry or typo protection; no environment diagnostics command for onboarding.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): D1 checkpoint/resume with full state capture; D2 per-step metrics JSONL; D3 HOSS torch parity; D4 optimizer x scheduler bake-off with cosine/WSD baselines; D5 `mgr sample` generation CLI; D6 env-var registry; D7 `mgr doctor`.

STRATEGIC VALUE: converts nanochat from "benchmark script" to "dependable training system". D1 and D2 are hard prerequisites for EP-C (eval-tasks), EP-E (scaling sweeps), and EP-G (verdict engine).
notes: POLISH ADDENDUM: this epic gained an 8th child during the plan-polish pass: rz8.8 (end-to-end pipeline test scripts - full research loop on tiny budgets with rich stage-by-stage logging). It is the program's integration safety net: scenario scripts composing doctor -> gen-tasks -> train (kill + resume) -> certify -> eval-tasks -> sample -> regressions, plus a maintenance contract requiring G2/C4/E1 to append their stages as part of their own acceptance. The child-list sentence in the description above predates it.

title: Checkpoint/resume in train.py (adopt the orphaned checkpoint_manager)
description:
WHY: nanochat/checkpoint_manager.py is fully implemented (save_checkpoint/load_checkpoint, model_{step}.pt / optim_{step}_rank{rank}.pt shards / meta_{step}.json) but train.py NEVER CALLS IT - confirmed by code investigation. Consequences: interrupted runs are lost; C2 eval-tasks has nothing to load; E1 scaling rungs (hours each) are un-runnable; C6 distillation has no teachers. This is the single highest-leverage infrastructure gap in the repo. NOTE: existing bead model_guided_research-1ra (activation checkpointing strategy) is about MEMORY (rematerialization), not persistence - different problem, no overlap.

WHAT: wire checkpoint_manager into train.py IN PLACE:
- Flags: --checkpoint-interval N (steps; 0=off), --checkpoint-dir (default under the run's artifact dir), --checkpoint-keep K (retention), --resume-from {path|latest}.
- FULL state capture: model state_dict; ALL optimizers (AdamW + Muon groups; HOSS state when D3 lands - design the dict layout to accommodate it now); scheduler state (OrdinalScheduler needs state_dict()/load_state_dict() added - A/B/C counters, EMA, best_loss); dataloader state (tokenizing_distributed_data_loader_with_state already returns resume_state_dict with pq_idx/rg_idx - actually plumb it through and restore it); RNG states (torch CPU + CUDA, numpy, python random); step counter; FLOPs-budget accounting so a resumed run honors the ORIGINAL --target-flops (budget consumed so far persisted in meta).
- summary.json records resume lineage: parent run_id chain, resume step.

ACCEPTANCE (the interruption test is the bead):
- Run 100 steps saving at 50; kill; resume -> steps 51-100 loss trajectory matches the uninterrupted run. TARGET: bitwise-identical on CPU fp32 (achievable if RNG + data order restored exactly); if bitwise proves impossible, document precisely why and assert statistical equivalence (same final loss to 1e-4) instead - the WHY must be in the close notes.
- Resume restores: optimizer momenta (verify Muon buffers round-trip), scheduler counters, data position (no repeated/skipped batches - assert via batch-hash logging in the test).
- torch.compile interaction: state_dict key normalization (_orig_mod. prefix) handled both directions; test with --compile on CPU.
- DDP: per-rank optimizer shards save/load correctly (single-rank CI test + code-reviewed rank logic).

CONSUMERS: C2 (eval), C6 (teachers), E1 (rungs), D5 (sampling). Their schemas depend on this layout - document the checkpoint directory contract in checkpoint_manager.py docstring.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- SAVE/LOAD LOGGING: every checkpoint save/load logs a rich line: step, path, bytes written/read, duration, components included (model/optim/sched/dataloader/rng) - silent partial saves are the failure mode to design against.
- INTEGRITY OPTION: --checkpoint-verify reloads immediately after save and compares state hashes (model tensors + optimizer tensors), logging the result; cheap corruption tripwire for long runs on flaky storage.
- DIVERGENCE DIAGNOSTICS: the interruption e2e test, on trajectory mismatch, must log the FIRST divergent step with both loss values and the RNG-state comparison at that step - "resume drifted" without a pinpoint is undebuggable.
- RETENTION TEST: --checkpoint-keep K honored (oldest pruned, newest kept, meta files consistent). NOTE: pruning deletes only checkpoint files this feature itself created within the run dir - never user files.
- E2E: this bead's interruption test is promoted to rz8.8 Scenario 2 with randomized kill points (logged seed).
close_reason: Completed with the bitwise TARGET achieved (not the statistical fallback): interruption test checkpoints at step 5, resumes, and steps 6-11 match the uninterrupted CPU fp32 run bitwise - exact data replay reconstructs the loader's token_buffer remainder by fast-forwarding the deterministic stream; approximate (row-group skip) mode kept as --resume-data-mode approximate for very long runs. Full state: model/all-optimizers/scheduler-counters/data-position/all-RNG-streams (weights-only-safe serialization); original FLOPs budget restored from meta; lineage chain in summary.json. Retention (only this run's files), --checkpoint-verify integrity tripwire, rich save/load logging. ORPHAN FIX: ordinal schedulers were never stepped in the loop (constructed-only since landing) - now driven per-step; counters provably advance and round-trip. Directory contract documented for C2/C6/E1/D5. DDP: per-rank shard logic code-reviewed, single-rank CI tested (no multi-GPU in CI). 9 new tests.

title: Structured per-step metrics stream (JSONL artifact)
description:
WHY: train.py currently emits console logs and ONE end-of-run summary.json. Step-resolved data exists only as ephemeral stdout. Everything analytical downstream - scaling-law fits (E2), verdict adjudication (G2), the planned rich dashboard (existing bead model_guided_research-nyp, which should CONSUME this stream as its data source - coordinate, do not duplicate), regression forensics - needs a durable per-step record.

WHAT: emit one JSONL line per log_interval from train.py into the run's artifact dir alongside summary.json (metrics.jsonl):
{schema_version, step, loss, val_loss (when measured), lr, grad_norm, tokens_per_s, tflops, peak_mem_gb, elapsed_s, and mechanism-specific buffer stats when present: tropical_gamma_min/head_mean, MoM routing entropy + per-member utilization (A3), ordinal scheduler state (rank rho = (A,B,C), restarts), HOSS diagnostics (D3: lanczos eigenvalue extremes)}.
- schema_version field from day one; schema documented in docs/ (extend an existing doc or the artifact conventions doc).
- A small reader utility (function in an existing module, e.g. alongside artifact helpers) with schema validation, used by E2/G2/tests.
- bench-fixed-flops subprocess runs inherit it automatically (it is just train.py).

PERFORMANCE: buffered writes, flush at val intervals and exit (no per-line fsync); rank-0 only under DDP; measured overhead < 1% step time on CPU smoke config (record measurement in close notes).

ACCEPTANCE:
- metrics.jsonl present for all training paths incl. subprocess benchmarks; final line flushed on clean exit AND on KeyboardInterrupt (try/finally).
- Reader round-trip test on a real tiny run; malformed-line tolerance (skip with warning, never crash an analysis over one bad line).
- Buffer-stat passthrough tested for tropical (gamma stats present when --tropical-record-margins).
PROVENANCE FIELDS (idea-wizard round 2; receipts need tamper-evidence): metrics.jsonl's header line AND summary.json gain a provenance block: {git_sha, git_dirty (bool - any uncommitted changes in tracked files at launch), config_hash (canonical serialization of the resolved config), data_snapshot_hash (from the wiz bead's helper where available), schema_version}. RATIONALE: the registry/adjudication machinery (G2) treats artifacts as evidence; evidence from a dirty working tree is unattributable to any reviewable code state. RULE: git_dirty=true marks the artifact TAINTED - fine for exploration, but the G2 engine refuses tainted evidence for verdicts (gatekeeping note added to hij.2). Implementation: read SHA/dirty via subprocess at run start (graceful no-git fallback: provenance marked unavailable, treated as tainted); zero additional deps. Tests: provenance block present and correct on a clean tree (fixture repo), dirty flag trips on a touched tracked file, no-git fallback path covered.
close_reason: Completed: metrics.jsonl (schema mgr.metrics.v1) written by every train run - per-log-interval step records (loss, lr+groups, grad_norm via foreach clip trick with pre-clip REUSE on clipped runs, tok/s, TFLOPs, peak mem, elapsed), val records at val cadence, ordinal counters + tropical gamma passthrough; rank-0 only; buffered with val-cadence + exit flush via try/finally; header (provenance: git_sha/git_dirty/config_hash/data_snapshot_hash(null until wiz)/tainted) flushed IMMEDIATELY; same provenance block in summary.json; reader read_metrics_jsonl with malformed-line tolerance; schema documented in artifacts/README.md; subprocess benchmarks inherit automatically. OVERHEAD MEASUREMENT (close-note requirement): stream itself 0.007ms/logged-step (0.01%); grad_norm field 8.9ms on the default CPU smoke config (1028ms step) = 0.87% total < 1% bound; the field amortizes further on real configs and is free on clipped runs. HOSS diagnostics deferred to D3 (optional fields). 4 new tests; full suite 360 passed.

title: HOSS PyTorch parity with the JAX implementation
description:
WHY: the README sells three optimizers, but hoss_opt_torch.py is a minimal stub while the JAX hoss_opt.py implements the real algorithm: symmetric Lanczos curvature estimation via Hessian-vector products, then an analytic Ornstein-Uhlenbeck macro-step over delta with the Lyapunov integral computed by eigendecomposition. Framework #11 (nonstandard analysis / hyperreal training) therefore has NO production-grade torch realization, and every torch optimizer comparison that includes HOSS is currently misleading. D4's bake-off is blocked on this being honest.

WHAT: port the full algorithm to hoss_opt_torch.py IN PLACE:
- HVP via double-backprop (torch.func or autograd.grad twice) - requires a LOSS CLOSURE, so HOSS cannot be a pure .step() optimizer. Design the closure interface deliberately (LBFGS-style closure convention), plumb it through train.py's step path, and DOCUMENT why (this is an API decision others will read).
- k-step symmetric Lanczos (k ~ 10, configurable) building the tridiagonal T; eigendecompose; compute phi_delta and the Lyapunov integral EXACTLY as the JAX version does - mirror the math comments line-for-line so the two implementations stay reviewable side-by-side.
- Shaped noise injection per the SDE solution; all states stored per-param-group for D1 checkpointing (coordinate the state dict layout with D1).
- Keep the existing guard: incompatible with gpt_synaptic (no closure available there).

VALIDATION (the bead's core):
1. Reproduce the stiff-quadratic demo from nonstandard_analysis_and_hyperreal_training.py (run_stiff_quadratic_demo) in torch: matching convergence behavior across a 100x learning-rate sweep - the LR-robustness claim is THE claim; this becomes a B1-style certificate.
2. JAX<->torch numerical cross-check: identical synthetic problem, seed-controlled identical Lanczos starting vector -> extreme eigenvalue estimates agree within 1e-4.
3. HVP correctness vs explicit Hessian on a tiny MLP (fp64).

ACCEPTANCE:
- nanochat trains end-to-end with --optimizer-type hoss on CPU smoke config, no nonfinite grads (--check-numerics clean).
- Honest overhead documented: wall-clock factor vs AdamW at equal steps AND equal wall-clock comparison guidance for D4.
- Unit tests for Lanczos (known-spectrum matrix), Lyapunov integral (closed-form 1D case), and closure plumbing.
notes:
POLISH ADDENDUM (tests & logging):
- DIAGNOSTICS INTO METRICS STREAM: emit per-log_interval HOSS diagnostics into the D2 metrics.jsonl: Lanczos extreme eigenvalue estimates (lambda_min/lambda_max), chosen step scaling, injected noise scale - D2's schema already reserves these fields; a test asserts presence and finiteness during a smoke run.
- STEP LOGGING: optional debug flag logging per-step Lanczos convergence (iterations, residual) - curvature estimation failures must be visible, not silent.
- CROSS-IMPL TEST LOGGING: the JAX<->torch cross-check test logs both eigenvalue sequences side-by-side on failure (not just the max-diff scalar) so numerical divergence is localizable.
- CLOSURE-API TESTS: closure called the expected number of times per step; helpful TypeError when train.py passes no closure with --optimizer-type hoss; gpt_synaptic guard still raises with a clear message.

title: Optimizer x scheduler bake-off with modern baselines (cosine/WSD)
description:
WHY: --scheduler-type currently offers none|ordinal. Beating "none" is a strawman: the ordinal scheduler's transfinite restart story (omega^2*A + omega*B + C) means nothing until it faces cosine and WSD (warmup-stable-decay), the actual modern defaults. Likewise the optimizer story (adamw/muon/hoss) has never been compared under one controlled protocol in this repo.

WHAT:
1. Add cosine and WSD schedulers to train.py IN PLACE (--scheduler-type none|ordinal|cosine|wsd; trivial math, golden-curve unit tests: LR value at known step fractions).
2. Fixed-FLOPs bake-off: {adamw, muon, hoss} x {none, cosine, wsd, ordinal} x >=3 seeds, on standard attention plus two exotic mechanisms (tropical, ultrametric) to detect optimizer-mechanism interactions (plausible: max-plus subgradients may want different momentum handling).
3. ORDINAL'S REAL TEST: the regime-shift stream (C1 task 7): measure steps-to-recover pre-shift loss after each boundary. Restarts are the ordinal scheduler's selling point; stationary FineWeb cannot show it. Report restart counts and post-restart recovery curves from the D2 metrics stream (ordinal state is logged there).
4. LR FAIRNESS: optimizers need different LR scales - run a coarse 3-point LR sweep per (optimizer, scheduler) cell and report the best, rather than sharing one LR; document the protocol in the report (this is the most common bake-off methodology error; do not commit it).

OUTPUT: artifacts per conventions; statistical comparison via the existing Mann-Whitney machinery; explicit verdict lines: "ordinal {beats|matches|loses to} cosine on stationary data" and "... under regime shift"; verdicts feed the G1 registry (the ordinal claims are registry entries via C3).

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-2bx (config templates for fair comparisons) - reuse if landed; existing model_guided_research-u8l (HP search plan) is broader - this bead is the focused scheduler/optimizer question.

DEPENDS ON: D2 (metrics stream for recovery curves), D3 (honest HOSS). C1 task 7 used when available - if C1 has not landed, build the regime-shift stream as a minimal inline generator and note the duplication for C1 to absorb (do not block).

ACCEPTANCE: report committed; scheduler unit tests green; every cell's best-LR protocol recorded in summary.json.

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

OPTIMIZER-MECHANISM CO-DESIGN CELL (added arm 5): the bake-off should explicitly probe the piecewise-linear hypothesis: tropical/max-plus layers have piecewise-constant Jacobians (subgradients, no curvature), which plausibly interact badly with optimizers tuned for smooth losses (second-moment estimates see spurious variance at region boundaries). Add ONE exploratory arm: sign-momentum (signSGD-with-momentum) and/or a Polyak-style step on the tropical mechanism, vs adamw/muon on the same mechanism, same protocol. INTERPRETATION RULE (state in the report): if the subgradient-aware arm wins on tropical but NOT on standard attention, that is the first evidence for mechanism-aware optimizer design and warrants a discovered-from bead ("subgradient-native optimizers for piecewise-linear mechanisms"); if it loses everywhere, the smooth-optimizer default is vindicated for max-plus too - either way the cell earns its compute.

title: mgr sample: end-to-end generation CLI
close_reason: mgr sample landed (3cdc3b8, merged 741c8f0): streaming generation with rich Live, tokens/s + peak memory, --compare side-by-side panels, --json agent mode, separator semantics matching the eval scorer. Verified in anger across 10 mechanisms on real pilot1 checkpoints - the degenerate constant-'lt' policy is directly demonstrable in one command. Determinism/compare/separator/arg-error tests in tests/test_sample.py; README Quick Tour gains the sampling step with expectation-setting.

title: Centralize demo env-var/feature-flag registry with validation
description:
WHY: code investigation counted 29 distinct environment variables steering the JAX demos (GAUGE_* x9, TROP_* x8, REV_* x6, ULTRA_* x5, BRAID_CROSSING_LAW) with no central registry, no validation, and no discoverability. A typo (GAUGE_STRUCTRED=1) is silently ignored - the worst failure mode for research code because the experiment silently runs the WRONG configuration and the result gets believed.

WHAT:
1. Declarative registry in config.py IN PLACE (AGENTS.md: no new file): a table of {name, demo, type (bool/int/float/enum), default, description} covering all 29 flags.
2. Helper get_flag(name) used by the demos - the call-site migration is many simple mechanical changes: per AGENTS.md, parallelize across subagents for the simple swaps; NO regex scripting.
3. `mgr flags [demo]` CLI command: rich table of flags, types, defaults, current values, descriptions (discoverability).
4. Validation at demo start: warn loudly (rich yellow panel) on any set env var that prefix-matches a known demo family (GAUGE_/TROP_/REV_/ULTRA_/BRAID_) but is not in the registry - catches typos; also validate value types (non-integer in an int flag = hard error with the expected format).

BEHAVIOR GUARANTEE: zero behavior change for correctly-spelled flags - this is pure relocation + validation. Defaults must be byte-identical to current behavior (capture current defaults in a test BEFORE migrating).

ACCEPTANCE:
- All 29 flags in the registry with descriptions; `mgr flags` renders the table;
- Typo test: set GAUGE_STRUCTRED=1, run gauge demo, assert warning emitted;
- Type test: ULTRA_PACKED=banana -> hard error with helpful message;
- Demo equivalence: one demo run per family before/after migration with flags set, identical outputs (seeded).

title: mgr doctor: environment diagnostics command
close_reason: Implemented mgr doctor in cli.py: 10 environment checks with actionable fix-it hints per failing row - python>=3.13, uv on PATH, venv active, torch (CUDA/MPS/bf16 detection, CPU-only=warn), JAX backend+devices (reuses utils.get_device_info), Triton importability (A7 prerequisite, optional=warn), training-data parquet count with auto-download hint (warns on 0 or 1 shard since train/val needs >=2), tokenizer load with vocab size, disk space at NANOCHAT_BASE_DIR (warn <5GiB), and an end-to-end tiny-GPT forward smoke on the detected device. Rich table + summary panel; --json mode for agent consumption (schema_version 1, kind=doctor); exit codes 0/1/2 = ok/warnings/failures (scriptable preflight). Runs in ~3s CPU. Test covers: CPU-CI safety (no FAIL on healthy env), JSON schema keys, simulated-missing-data warn with the exact fix flag in the hint, model-smoke green. README Troubleshooting now leads with 'Start with the doctor' per the bead acceptance.

title: End-to-end pipeline test scripts: full research loop on tiny budgets, with rich stage-by-stage logging
description:
WHY: every bead in this program carries its own unit/parity/golden tests, but nothing exercises the FULL research loop as a user would run it. Integration failures (artifact schema drift between commands, checkpoint format mismatches, CLI flag renames, tokenizer/dataloader incompatibilities) only surface where commands COMPOSE - and right now nothing composes them automatically. This bead is the program's integration safety net and the single highest-value test asset for catching cross-bead regressions during the long implementation campaign ahead.

WHAT: e2e scenario scripts under scripts/ (precedent: scripts/verify_flex_correctness.py), runnable locally and as an optional CI job (manual/nightly trigger - too slow for per-PR), each completing on CPU in bounded time:

SCENARIO 1 - FULL LOOP (<15 min CPU): mgr doctor (optional stage - skip with logged warning if not yet implemented) -> mgr gen-tasks (one small diagnostic task) -> train standard + tropical at tiny --target-flops with --checkpoint-interval -> SIGKILL one run mid-training -> resume it and verify completion -> mgr certify (fast subset) -> mgr eval-tasks on both checkpoints -> mgr sample (greedy, fixed seed) -> mgr regressions comparing the two variants' summaries. Every artifact consumed by a later stage was produced by an earlier one - that chain IS the test.
SCENARIO 2 - RESUME ROBUSTNESS: SIGKILL at a randomized step (logged seed), resume, assert trajectory continuity per the D1 acceptance criteria (this is D1's interruption test promoted to a repeatable script with randomized kill points).
SCENARIO 3 - GENERATION DETERMINISM: greedy same-seed decode across every KV-cache-supporting mechanism; byte-identical outputs across two invocations; tokens/s recorded per mechanism.
SCENARIO 4 - BENCHMARK + REGRESSION GATE: bench-fixed-flops with two variants at tiny budget -> mgr regressions --fail-on-regression against a committed golden baseline snapshot; proves the gate trips on a deliberately degraded fixture.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS (the user-facing core of this bead - logs must make any failure diagnosable WITHOUT rerunning):
- every stage wrapped in rich BEGIN/END panels: stage name, key params, elapsed time, artifact paths produced;
- on stage failure: dump the tail (last ~50 lines) of the failing subprocess's output, the env snapshot (mgr doctor --json if available, else a minimal inline probe), and the exact reproduction command - all into the report;
- consolidated e2e_report.md + e2e_report.json artifact per run (stage matrix: pass/fail/skipped, durations, artifact inventory with sizes);
- nonzero exit on any required-stage failure; skipped optional stages reported as SKIPPED, never silently absent.

MAINTENANCE CONTRACT (binding on later beads, noted on each): G2 (adjudicate stage), C4 (scorecard smoke cell), and E1 (smoke ladder) must extend Scenario 1 with their stage as part of their OWN acceptance criteria - this script is append-only infrastructure, never allowed to go stale.

CI INTEGRATION: workflow_dispatch + nightly schedule job in ci.yml (separate from the fast PR jobs); uploads e2e_report artifacts on failure. RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-c6v (CI-lite uv workflow) - the local invocation should be the same entrypoint c6v wraps.

ACCEPTANCE: all four scenarios green on CPU from a fresh clone (after uv sync + data fixture); deliberate-breakage check during development for each scenario (e.g. corrupt a checkpoint file, change an artifact field name) confirms the failure is caught AND the log/report pinpoints the failing stage and shows the repro command; total runtime documented per scenario.

DEPENDS ON: B1/5ki.1 (certify), C2/vdc.2 (eval-tasks; transitively C1 tasks + D1 checkpoints), D2/rz8.2 (metrics assertions), D5/rz8.5 (sample). mgr doctor (rz8.7) deliberately NOT a dependency - used opportunistically.
MAINTENANCE-CONTRACT EXTENSIONS (idea-wizard additions; same binding rule as G2/C4/E1 - each bead extends the scenarios as part of its OWN acceptance): 5ki.5 (Scenario 1 trains with --certify-interval and asserts certs.* fields in metrics.jsonl; Scenario 2 asserts the certs stream survives SIGKILL/resume without gaps or duplicates); vnl.1 (Scenario 1 gains a theorems-validate --deep stage); 77l.1 (Scenario 1 gains an optional profile-data stage on the generated task); 7b0.8 (Scenario 4 gains one composite score/aggregate variant in the bench list). The scenarios remain append-only infrastructure; each addition follows the BEGIN/END rich-panel logging requirements above.
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - SCENARIO 5 (THEORY SMOKES, grows by maintenance contract as theory beads land): an optional-stage group exercising the theory program's training-level machinery end-to-end on CPU-tiny configs, with the same BEGIN/END rich logging, skip-if-not-implemented semantics, and consolidated-report integration as Scenarios 1-4:
- 5a (from 8gk.1): dequantization-annealing mini-run - beta schedule, both certificate-coverage curves logged, endpoint tropical-mode parity asserted.
- 5b (from u55.5): symplectic-tied no-norm mini-run - activation-norm and shadow-energy trajectories logged, bounded-drift assertion vs the additive baseline's drift.
- 5c (from u55.3): word-problem mini-eval - harness smoke producing per-length slope tables (assertion is harness correctness, not model quality at tiny scale).
- 5d (from lab.3, when landed): a two-phase ordinal-orchestrated program with the full descent log - every event's rho before/after in CNF notation - asserted strictly decreasing.
Each sub-scenario is added BY the corresponding theory bead as part of its own acceptance (same contract pattern as G2/C4/E1 in Scenario 1); this note is the registration of record so the e2e script's growth is planned, not ad hoc.
close_reason: e2e pipeline scenarios landed (04d17c5, merged 741c8f0): scripts/e2e_pipeline.py with full-loop (17 stages incl. SIGKILL/resume + adjudicate-refusal integrity, ~75s), resume (randomized kill, splice-aware continuity, ~40s), determinism (10-mechanism byte-identical greedy, ~3.5min), regression-gate (self-pass + vacuity guard + degraded-fixture trip, ~30s). Nightly+dispatch CI job. Caught ll08 and gref on first runs - the bead's reason for existing, demonstrated before it even merged. Deliberate-breakage validation happened organically (torn checkpoint -> pinpointed stage + repro command; vacuous gate pass -> caught and guarded).

title: Shared config parity tests (bio_inspired ↔ MGR)
description:
- Define a small battery of parity tests/configs runnable in both repos (or replicated here) to compare baseline/Flex/feature toggles under matched hyperparameters.
- Capture command lines, seeds, and expected metrics to detect drift over time.
- Store configs/commands in docs/config_parity_suite.md; align with FLOPs harness and baseline tasks.
acceptance_criteria: Config parity suite defined with ≥3 matched runs (baseline, Flex, one math feature) including commands, seeds, expected metric bands; runnable here (and/or mirrored instructions for bio_inspired); documented in docs/config_parity_suite.md.

title: Investigate torch.compile failure for synaptic FlexAttention KV-cache paths
description: On torch 2.9.1+cu128, torch.compile of synaptic FlexAttention fails in KV-cache paths (verify_flex_correctness --model synaptic --compile), with Inductor/SymInt errors (e.g., AttributeError: 'int' object has no attribute 'is_Add' while lowering torch.sym_max) and earlier FlexibleLayout indexing failures. Investigate minimal repro and implement a workaround or document a compatibility matrix.
acceptance_criteria: Repro captured (exact torch/cuda/flags) and either: (a) synaptic FlexAttention compiles for full_forward + KV-cache paths, or (b) clearly documented as unsupported with version constraints + skip logic in scripts/tests.
close_reason: Fix: use torch.compile(dynamic=False) for synaptic FlexAttention checks to avoid Inductor SymInt lowering failure in KV-cache paths; verified verify_flex_correctness --model synaptic --compile now passes.

title: campaign: E1 seed expansion - braid-dyck + surreal-arith to 9 seeds/arm (decides the +-0.13 CIs from rrs5)
close_reason: Seed expansion complete (ef3cd42): 24 runs pooled to n=9/arm in the 1e14 cohort. hyp-surreal-wide-dynamic-range REFUTED (-0.009 [-0.047,+0.029], both arms off-floor, gate stood aside) - the program's first genuine, fully-powered refutation. hyp-braid-dyck inconclusive but CI now excludes zero (+0.101 [+0.012,+0.190]): braid genuinely beats standard on held-out Dyck depths; the registered +0.05 size is unresolved - next evidence from a bigger rung (baseline bimodality at floor drives the variance), not more seeds. CI shrink tracked 1/sqrt(3): ci-v2 clustering converged honestly.

title: EPIC: Reach & versatility - standalone API and documentation site
description:
WHY (background): the work only compounds if others can USE it. Two adoption funnels are currently closed:
1. The attention mechanisms cannot be cleanly imported into someone else's PyTorch model - they are coupled to GPTConfig and live behind training-oriented modules. "pip install, three lines, tropical attention in YOUR transformer" is the cheapest possible adoption funnel and turns the repo from "a benchmark you read about" into "a library you try". (Depends on EP-A's A1: the refactored scaffolding defines the stable public surface worth documenting.)
2. The 338KB of graduate-level mathematics in markdown_documentation/ (24-45KB per framework, with derivations and proofs) is the project's most undervalued asset - and it is effectively unreadable as raw GitHub markdown blobs (no LaTeX rendering, no navigation). A static docs site with per-framework pages linking theory -> JAX demo -> torch implementation -> certificates -> scorecard verdicts makes the full pipeline visible per framework; that page structure IS the project pitch.

WHAT (scope, see child beads): H1 standalone public API + wheel-install CI test; H2 static docs site (MkDocs Material or equivalent, decided in-bead) with math rendering and GitHub Pages deploy.

RELATED EXISTING BEADS: model_guided_research-9f1 (pedagogical walkthrough mode) is the interactive in-terminal pedagogy angle; H2 is the web angle - coordinate content structure, do not duplicate. The release.yml PyPI workflow already exists; H1 adds the proof that the published wheel actually works standalone.
CHILD ADDITION (idea-wizard round 2): H3 = swh.3 mgr quickstart - the five-minute showcase command (train two mechanisms, certify their invariants live, sample side by side, render a mini comparison) that turns the distributed pitch into one command; the adoption multiplier for H1's library story and H2's docs site (whose quickstart page mirrors it).

title: Standalone public API for attention mechanisms (+ wheel-install CI proof)
description:
WHY: "pip install and drop TropicalAttention into YOUR model" is the cheapest adoption funnel this project can build, and adoption is what converts the work from a private benchmark into a cited library. Today the mechanisms are entangled with GPTConfig and training-module imports; the release.yml workflow publishes a wheel nobody has verified works standalone.

WHAT:
1. PUBLIC SURFACE: define __all__ exports in nanochat/__init__.py for every mechanism class; ensure mechanism modules import cleanly in a torch-only environment (no JAX leakage through transitive imports - audit and fix import graphs IN PLACE).
2. CONSTRUCTION ERGONOMICS: a from_dims(n_embd, n_head, **mechanism_kwargs) classmethod on the A1 scaffold (so external users never touch GPTConfig), with per-mechanism kwargs documented in docstrings. The A1 protocol should already minimize GPTConfig coupling - this bead finishes the job. Do NOT fork modules to decouple (AGENTS.md); fix imports in place.
3. CONTRACT DOCS: input/output shapes, mask semantics, KV-cache optionality, dtype expectations - documented in the README new section "Using the mechanisms in your own model" with a runnable example (tropical attention inside a vanilla PyTorch transformer block, ~20 lines).
4. SMOKE TESTS: build each mechanism standalone via from_dims without GPTConfig; forward + backward on random input; run in CI.
5. WHEEL PROOF: CI job (extend ci.yml): uv build -> install the wheel into a FRESH venv -> python -c import + tiny forward for 3 representative mechanisms. This catches packaging rot (missing modules, data files, version pins) that repo-checkout testing never sees.

ACCEPTANCE: README section live; standalone smoke tests green; wheel-install CI job green on main; zero new code files (docs + tests + in-place edits only).

DEPENDS ON: A1 (the refactored scaffold IS the stable public surface; documenting the pre-refactor one would be wasted work).

title: Static documentation site (theory docs + pipeline pages + artifact showcases)
description:
WHY: markdown_documentation/ holds 338KB of graduate-level mathematics (11 docs, 24-45KB each, with derivations and proofs) - the project's most undervalued asset, currently unreadable in practice (raw GitHub markdown blobs: no LaTeX rendering, no navigation, no cross-linking). A docs site also gives the scorecard/scaling results a public home.

WHAT:
1. TOOLING DECISION (make in-implementation, document the rationale in the site config): MkDocs Material is the default recommendation (lowest friction for math-heavy markdown, KaTeX/MathJax plugin, search built in); alternatives considered: Sphinx (heavier), custom Nextra (overkill without a webapp need).
2. CONTENT - render, do not rewrite:
   - the 11 (13 after EP-F) theory docs AS-IS, fixing only render-breaking syntax (math delimiters, etc.);
   - a quickstart distilled from the README;
   - PER-FRAMEWORK PIPELINE PAGES - the site's centerpiece and the project's pitch made visible: for each framework one page linking theory doc -> JAX demo (how to run, what the property checks verify) -> torch implementation (file, flags, constraints) -> certificates (B1 results when available) -> scorecard verdicts (C4/G2 ledger rows when available). Pages degrade gracefully when later-stage artifacts do not exist yet ("not yet adjudicated" rather than broken links).
   - artifact showcases: the scaling overlay plot (E2), the scorecard (C4), the hypothesis ledger (G2) - embedded/linked as they materialize.
3. DEPLOY: GitHub Pages via a new workflow file (.github/workflows/ - infrastructure, not code proliferation); build on push to main; site URL added to README and the repo's About field.
4. MATH RENDERING ACCEPTANCE: spot-check the heaviest pages - the BCH formula sections in matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.md and the p-adic notation in ultrametric_worlds - render correctly.

RELATED: existing bead model_guided_research-9f1 (pedagogical walkthrough mode) is the in-terminal pedagogy angle - share the per-framework content structure (theory/demo/production/verdict) so the two stay consistent; do not duplicate prose.

ACCEPTANCE: site builds in CI and deploys; math renders on the spot-checked pages; per-framework pipeline pages live for all current frameworks; README links the site.

NO HARD DEPENDENCIES (renders whatever exists; richer as other epics land).

=== EXPANSION (idea-wizard revision) ===

MECHANISM MATH CARDS (added content item): auto-generated one-page-per-mechanism summaries - the "model card" pattern applied to mathematics: mechanism name + one-line thesis; defining invariants with LATEST certificate values (B1 artifacts); preregistered predictions + current verdicts (G1/G2 ledger rows); theorem-registry entries (vnl.1) with proof status (conjecture / proved-on-paper / lean-checked); complexity + constraints (head_dim divisibility, KV-cache support, known gaps with bead ids); flags and config surface. GENERATED, never hand-maintained (staleness is the failure mode of every hand-written status table - the README's Expected Performance table being the in-house example): a small build step renders cards from the registries + artifact tree at site build time. Cards embed in the per-framework pipeline pages and replace the README mechanism table with a pointer (same move hij.1 makes for the claims table).
TESTS FOR MATH CARDS (plan-space pass 2): card generation is deterministic from fixture registries + a fixture artifact tree (byte-stable output, snapshot-tested); graceful degradation tested (missing cert artifact -> "not yet certified" rendered, never a broken card); site CI includes a link-checker pass over generated cards so a renamed artifact path fails the build loudly instead of 404ing silently.

title: mgr quickstart: the five-minute showcase - train, certify, and compare two mathematical transformers in one command
Float-quantization quality-vs-bits baseline harness + AUC aggregate (unblocks hyp-padic-truncation-graceful)
close_reason: Complete: float-quant knob (eval_weight_quant_bits) + precision-curve AUC artifact (mgr.bench.precision_curves.v1, common-window protocol) + hyp-padic-truncation-graceful promoted PRE-EVIDENCE then adjudicated REFUTED by engine ci-v3 (auc_ratio 1.017, CI95 [0.990,1.045] vs >=1.1; 3 clean seed pairings). Follow-up insight filed separately.

title: EPIC THEORY-II: Lie-theoretic composition - depth as flow, integrability as protected memory
description:
THE DISCOVERY: the project's composition-side frameworks (gauge, reversible, braid, quaternion/octonion/Clifford) share one picture: THE RESIDUAL STREAM IS A CONNECTION on a depth x position lattice. Layers are exponentials of generators; composition through depth is governed by commutators (BCH/Magnus); the algebraic class of the generators determines everything interesting:

- NILPOTENT generators => the BCH series TERMINATES: an L-layer composite is exactly a single exponential of a polynomial in the layer generators. Depth becomes a training-time convenience, not an inference cost (T2.1: algebraic depth compression - distillation with theorems).
- CURVATURE of the connection (log-holonomy of plaquettes) is the gauge-INVARIANT observable of the stream - unlike neurons and features, which are basis-dependent and notoriously fragile across seeds. Conjecture: curvature concentrates exactly where the model does order-sensitive work - binding, variable assignment, induction (T2.2: invariant interpretability).
- YANG-BAXTER mixing => commuting transfer matrices => a tower of EXACTLY conserved charges through depth: protected memory with an algebraic mechanism rather than an empirical regularity, plus a principled spectral-parameter theory of relative position (T2.3 - includes the group-word-problem leapfrog test against the known TC0 state-tracking barrier of standard transformers).
- TRIALITY: attention is already a trilinear story (Q,K,V); Spin(8) triality is the symmetry that ties three 8-dimensional representations together - the octonion mechanism redesigned around the invariant trilinear form t(x,y,z) = Re((xy)z-bar) instead of the naive product (T2.4).
- SYMPLECTIC: the reversible block's coupling is one constraint away (F, G as gradients of scalar potentials) from being an EXACT symplectic integrator, whose shadow-Hamiltonian conservation makes activation drift impossible - normalization layers exist to fight a disease that conservation-law architectures simply do not have (T2.5: the no-norm transformer).

LEAPFROG THESIS: conventional architectures manage composition with normalization, careful init, and hope. Lie theory replaces hope with structure: compressible depth (nilpotency), invariant observables (curvature), provably protected memory (integrability), symmetry-protected parameter tying (triality), and stability theorems (symplecticity + Noether). Each child carries a falsifiable, scorecard-ready prediction.

CHILDREN: T2.1 BCH/Magnus depth compression; T2.2 curvature as gauge-invariant interpretability; T2.3 integrable R-matrix attention + word-problem benchmark; T2.4 triality-tied trilinear QKV; T2.5 symplectic no-norm transformer with Noether channels.

title: BCH/Magnus depth compression: train deep, deploy shallow, with algebra instead of distillation
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: treat DEPTH AS TIME. A gauge-block stack applies x -> exp(A_L) ... exp(A_2) exp(A_1) x. Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff gives the composite generator: exp(A)exp(B) = exp(A + B + [A,B]/2 + [A,[A,B]]/12 - [B,[A,B]]/12 + ...). Two regimes matter:
(1) NILPOTENT GENERATORS: if all A_l lie in a nilpotent Lie algebra of step k (e.g., strictly upper-triangular in a learned shared basis, or graded operators mapping grade i -> grades > i), the BCH series TERMINATES at nested-commutator weight k: the L-layer composite is EXACTLY a single exponential of a polynomial in the layer generators. Depth collapses algebraically - L layers of representation cost become 1-2 layers of inference cost, with NO approximation.
(2) GENERAL CASE - MAGNUS EXPANSION: state-dependent generators make the stack a nonautonomous flow X-prime = A(t) X; the Magnus expansion gives the single-exponential solution exp(Omega) with Omega = int A + (1/2) int int [A,A] + ..., convergent while int ||A|| < pi, with computable truncation bounds. Honest scope: state-dependence means per-input compression UNLESS generators are constrained to depend only on transported gauge-INVARIANT slow features - design exactly that constraint (generators read invariants that are themselves parallel-transported), making the composite input-independent to first order, and DERIVE the correction term so the approximation is bounded, not vibes.
EFFECTIVE-DEPTH THEORY (free byproduct): the commutator tower's norms ||[A_i, A_j]||, ||[A_i,[A_j,A_k]]||, ... measure how much genuinely NON-COMMUTATIVE work depth performs. Fast-decaying towers = effectively shallow networks (compressible); slow decay = depth is real. This is measurable TODAY on the existing gauge demo and gives an a-priori compressibility predictor.

WHY LEAPFROG: distillation compresses depth empirically, lossily, and with a student-training bill. BCH/Magnus compresses ALGEBRAICALLY: exact in the nilpotent case, error-bounded in general, and the compressibility is PREDICTABLE from commutator decay before you attempt it. Nobody deploys this because nobody trains in Lie coordinates - this project already does (gauge blocks).

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: both regimes with proofs/citations; the gauge-invariant-generator constraint and its correction-term derivation; the commutator-decay compressibility criterion.
2. IMPLEMENT nilpotent generator option in gauge_block_torch.py IN PLACE (flag: banded/strictly-triangular generator parameterization in a learned basis; verify A^k = 0 exactly).
3. COMPRESSION UTILITY (mgr or script per repo conventions): collapse L trained gauge blocks into 1-2 via exact BCH (nilpotent) or truncated Magnus (general); evaluate parity.
4. EXPERIMENTS (preregister): train 8-block nilpotent-gauge nanochat configs; compress to <= 2 blocks; predict <= 2% loss degradation at >= 3x inference-FLOP reduction; BASELINE: logit-distillation to a 2-block student at matched compute (must beat it or the story dies - say so); commutator-decay spectra logged for trained models and CORRELATED with achieved compression quality (predict correlation - the a-priori predictor claim).
5. METRICS: commutator-tower norms by weight into the D2 stream during training (cheap to sample stochastically).

TESTS & LOGGING: BCH identity tests up to weight 4 against brute-force matrix log/exp in fp64 on controlled-spectrum matrices (the implementation of the composite formula is itself testable algebra); nilpotency enforcement test (A^k exactly zero for the triangular parameterization - integer-exact structural property of the mask); Magnus truncation error <= theoretical bound on random nonautonomous flows (bound-respect assertion: violation falsifies the implementation); compression harness logs before/after losses, per-layer commutator spectra, wall-clock, and a rich parity table; e2e: compressed model passes the same eval-tasks (C2) battery as its parent within stated tolerance.

RELATION: gauge_block_torch.py + matrix_exponential demo (curvature/BCH machinery exists at demo level); T2.2 (same commutator objects, interpretive use); D2 (metrics); C2 (parity evaluation); registry (G1).

title: Curvature of the residual connection: gauge-invariant interpretability
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the residual stream with per-layer, per-position gauge fields defines a CONNECTION on the depth x position lattice; its CURVATURE is the log-holonomy around elementary plaquettes: F_(l,t) = log( U_(l,t) * U_(l+1,t)^(-1) * U_(l,t+1) * ... ) for the elementary square at layer l, position t (discrete field strength; for gauge blocks the U are the per-token rotations the mechanism already computes; for standard blocks use the polar part of layer Jacobians as a proxy - define both levels precisely in the note, with the bound relating them). THE POINT: curvature is GAUGE-COVARIANT - its norm is invariant under position/layer-dependent basis changes. Features and neurons are NOT: mechanistic interpretability's reproducibility problems across seeds and training runs are precisely basis-dependence. Curvature is the observable that survives.

CONJECTURES (each independently falsifiable):
C1 (FLATNESS): trained networks are nearly flat (curvature ~ 0) in most regions - composition is parallel-transportable where features merely move; flat regions are where the network 'just routes'.
C2 (CURVATURE = BINDING): curvature concentrates at order-sensitive computation - variable binding, coreference resolution, induction-head copy events. Non-commutativity is literally what [A,B] != 0 measures; binding is the canonically order-sensitive operation. Quantify: AUROC of plaquette-curvature-norm as an UNSUPERVISED detector of annotated binding sites on diagnostic sequences (C1 battery items provide token-level annotations: where the needle is retrieved, where the antecedent resolves).
C3 (TRANSFER): curvature maps computed on the same input across different seeds/runs agree far better than feature-basis observables (CKA-style comparison of curvature maps vs activation maps across seeds).

WHY LEAPFROG: interpretability currently lacks invariant observables - everything is post-hoc basis fishing (probes, SAE features) with known fragility. Importing field strength from gauge theory gives an observable with a THEOREM for its invariance and a hypothesis for its meaning. If C2 holds even partially, curvature heatmaps localize where reasoning happens with ZERO supervision and ZERO probe training.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: connection/curvature definitions at both rigor levels (exact for gauge blocks; polar-Jacobian proxy for standard attention, with the relating bound); invariance proofs; the three conjectures with their quantitative protocols.
2. INSTRUMENTATION: plaquette-curvature computation for (a) the matrix_exponential demo (it already has curvature-proxy diagnostics - upgrade to the lattice definition IN PLACE) and (b) nanochat gauge models; depth x position heatmap artifact + per-layer curvature-norm summary into the D2 metrics stream (sampled; cheap).
3. EXPERIMENTS: C1 - curvature distribution statistics on trained vs random-init models (flatness should EMERGE with training; random nets are not flat - this contrast is the cleanest first result); C2 - AUROC on annotated diagnostic tasks vs baselines (attention-entropy peaks, gradient-norm peaks - the obvious rivals curvature must beat); C3 - cross-seed agreement scores.
4. REGISTRY: preregister AUROC > 0.75 vs both baselines on needle-retrieval binding sites; trained-vs-random flatness gap; transfer-agreement gap.

TESTS & LOGGING: GAUGE-INVARIANCE TEST (the load-bearing one): apply random position-dependent gauge transformations to a model, assert curvature-norm maps invariant to fp tolerance while raw activation maps change maximally - both directions asserted; flat-limit sanity (commuting/abelian toy networks must give exactly zero curvature - constructible, exact); plaquette computation validated against brute-force holonomy on tiny lattices in fp64; heatmap export with deterministic rendering; AUROC harness with seeds, CIs, and per-task rich tables; all instrumentation overhead measured and logged (<2% step time or sampled less frequently).

RELATION: matrix_exponential_gauge_learning.py (existing diagnostics); gauge_block_torch.py; existing beads hi3 / 7ow / nyp (visualization and dashboards - curvature heatmaps are a natural panel; coordinate, do not duplicate); C1 battery (annotated diagnostics); T2.1 (same commutator machinery).

title: Integrable attention: Yang-Baxter R-matrices, conserved charges, and the group word-problem barrier
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the braid mechanism currently uses heuristic crossing updates with an optional YBE check. Go all in on integrability. Parameterize token mixing by R-MATRICES with SPECTRAL PARAMETER satisfying the Yang-Baxter equation - concretely the trigonometric R-matrix of U_q(sl_2) acting on pairs of 2-dimensional token sub-blocks, with per-head learned deformation parameter q = e^eta and per-position learned rapidities u_i; crossings between positions i and j use R(u_i - u_j). THE STRUCTURE THIS BUYS:
(1) COMMUTING TRANSFER MATRICES: row products T(u) satisfy [T(u), T(v)] = 0 for all spectral parameters - the defining miracle of integrable systems.
(2) CONSERVED CHARGES: log-derivative expansions of T(u) at distinguished points yield a TOWER of commuting charges Q_1, Q_2, ... that the mixing transports EXACTLY through depth: information channels protected by ALGEBRA, not by empirical regularity. This is the precise mechanism behind the braid framework's hand-wavy 'topologically protected information' - now with the actual theorem and measurable charges.
(3) PRINCIPLED RELATIVE POSITION: the difference property R(u_i - u_j) IS translation invariance; learned rapidity profiles are learned position warps - a relative-position theory derived from integrability rather than bolted on (RoPE is a special ansatz; rapidities are the integrable generalization).
(4) CANONICAL INVARIANT READOUTS: the Temperley-Lieb quotient and its Markov trace give sequence-level invariant observables (Jones-polynomial-adjacent) - principled invariant pooling for sequence representations.

THE LEAPFROG TEST - GROUP WORD PROBLEMS: composing permutations in S_5 (non-solvable) is NC1-complete; the Merrill-Sabharwal line of results places fixed-depth softmax transformers in TC0-like classes, and empirically LLMs fail exactly this kind of state tracking. R-matrix mixing implements (q-deformed) representation composition NATIVELY in its weights. HONEST FRAMING (write it exactly this way): the mixing is still linear algebra, so the right scientific question is whether structured mixing changes EFFECTIVE expressivity at fixed depth or 'only' the learnability and length-generalization slope - characterize the circuit class precisely in the theory note, make claims only where proofs or experiments back them. PREREGISTERED PREDICTIONS: on S_5 word problems, R-matrix/braid attention shows a length-generalization slope at least 2x flatter than standard attention at equal FLOPs; on SOLVABLE-group controls (Z_60, S_3) the gap shrinks substantially (mechanism-specificity - the win must track non-solvability, else the story is wrong); conserved-charge drift through depth is ~0 for R-matrix mode and measurably nonzero for the existing heuristic crossing modes (the diagnostic separating integrable from heuristic mixing).

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the R-matrix construction self-contained (weights, YBE verification for the trigonometric family); transfer-matrix commutativity proof for our exact configuration (finite chain, our boundary conventions - do the proof, do not wave at the literature); charge extraction Q_1 (the 'momentum-like' charge) and Q_2 explicitly; TL/Markov-trace readout design; the circuit-class discussion with precisely scoped claims.
2. IMPLEMENTATION: braid_crossing_law gains mode 'rmatrix' in braid_attention_torch.py IN PLACE; learned rapidities as per-position parameters; the existing braid_verify machinery extends to the trigonometric YBE residual check; charge instrumentation Q_1/Q_2 measured at every layer boundary into the D2 metrics stream.
3. BENCHMARK: the group word-problem task family enters the C1 battery (note added to vdc.1): sequences of generators, predict the composed element / its action on a tagged item; S_5 and A_5 vs Z_60 and S_3 controls; held-out lengths 2x-8x training; exact-match metric.
4. REGISTRY: the three preregistered predictions above with exact protocols.

TESTS & LOGGING: YBE residual == 0 (fp tolerance) for the trigonometric family - parameterized property test over random (u, v, eta) including degenerate corners (u=v, eta->0 rational limit); [T(u), T(v)] commutator-norm test on small chains via exact dense construction in fp64 (assert < 1e-10, and assert it FAILS for the heuristic crossing law - the separation proves the test has teeth); charge-conservation drift test through N-layer stacks (rmatrix: < 1e-5; soft/restricted modes: measurably nonzero, both asserted); word-problem eval harness with per-length accuracy curves, slope fits with CIs, and rich tables; KV-cache parity per house pattern; certify (B1) gains YBE + charge-conservation certificate entries; all runs log eta, rapidity profiles, and charge drift per layer.

RELATION: braid_attention_torch.py + knot_theoretic demo (crossing machinery, YBE check, Dyck experiments); C1 (new task family); B1 certify; D2 metrics; existing literature positioning in the note (quantum-group RNNs, unitary RNNs - what is different: spectral parameters + charges + the word-problem protocol).

PLEASE commit WIP or move to a worktree (convention: git worktree add /data/tmp/wt-u553 -b feat/u553 main). Also: braid_attention_torch.py is golden-gated (bitwise fixtures); cli.py is hot (campaign adjudication endgames pending). title: Triality-tied attention: Spin(8) and the trilinear structure of (Q, K, V)
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the octonion mechanism currently uses the Cayley-Dickson product as a feature mixer - exploiting perhaps a tenth of what is mathematically special about octonions. The deep structure is TRIALITY: Spin(8) is the unique simple Lie group whose outer automorphism group is S_3, permuting its three inequivalent 8-dimensional irreducible representations (vector V8, spinor S8+, spinor S8-). The octonion algebra realizes this concretely: the normed trilinear form t(x, y, z) = Re((x y) z-bar) is invariant under triality triples (g, g-prime, g-double-prime) in Spin(8) acting on the three slots through the three different representations. THE OBSERVATION: attention is ALREADY a trilinear story - (Q, K, V) - but conventional attention factors it as a bilinear score plus a linear mix, with three UNRELATED learned projections. Triality says: there is a canonical symmetry tying three 8-dimensional slots together, and it is exactly the symmetry type attention's shape calls for.

TWO DESIGNS (implement both behind one flag):
(a) CONSERVATIVE - TRIALITY-TIED PROJECTIONS: q, k, v projections per 8-dim chunk are NOT independent: one learned Spin(8) element per head determines all three via the triality automorphisms. Prediction: matches the naive octonion mechanism's quality at ~3x fewer projection parameters in those blocks - parameter tying justified by a symmetry theorem rather than an ad-hoc weight-sharing heuristic.
(b) RADICAL - GENUINELY TRILINEAR LOGITS: attention scores s_ij = t(q_i, k_j, v_j): VALUE-AWARE ROUTING - attend to positions whose value payload algebraically interacts with your query, not merely whose key matches. Value-aware scoring is normally dismissed as unprincipled (it breaks the retrieval metaphor); the triality form gives it a symmetry-protected normal form with provable invariance properties (exactly which transformations leave routing unchanged is a theorem, stated in the note). CROSS-FRAMEWORK MEETING POINT: trilinear logits are 2-simplex interactions derived from ALGEBRA - the simplicial mechanism derives them from COMBINATORICS. The scorecard can now ask a sharp question: on multi-entity relation tasks (C1 task 4), does algebraic trilinearity or combinatorial trilinearity win, and does either beat factored bilinear attention at equal FLOPs?

WHY LEAPFROG: parameter-efficiency claims from symmetry are usually retrofitted; here the symmetry (triality) is EXACTLY the symmetry of the object (three-slot attention over 8-dim chunks) - the rare case where exceptional mathematics fits an existing ML structure without forcing. And value-aware routing, if it works under the protected form, opens a design axis (content-interactive attention) the field has avoided for lack of a principled version.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE (self-contained - triality is esoteric even for mathematicians): construction of the triality automorphisms via octonions; invariance theorem for t; the tied-projection parameterization and a proof it loses no expressivity WITHIN the invariant class; the trilinear design's exact invariance group; complexity accounting (trilinear logits cost an extra multiply per pair - the einsum is still O(T^2 d), constant-factor honest).
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --octonion-mode {naive, triality-tied, trilinear} in octonion_attention_torch.py IN PLACE on the A1 scaffold; Spin(8) elements parameterized via the even Clifford construction (coordinate with F1/F3 Clifford work - shared rotor machinery, do not duplicate).
3. EXPERIMENTS (preregister): (a) triality-tied vs naive octonion at equal FLOPs - predict parity within noise at 3x fewer projection params in the attention blocks; (b) trilinear vs bilinear octonion AND vs simplicial on C1 task 4 (2-hop relations) - predict trilinear > bilinear by >= 3 points; the trilinear-vs-simplicial direction is genuinely open (that is the point of running it - register it as an open question with adjudication criteria, not a directional bet).
4. METRICS: rotor-norm deviations and per-mode parameter counts logged; routing-entropy comparison between bilinear and trilinear modes (value-awareness should change routing statistics measurably - log and report).

TESTS & LOGGING: triality-invariance numerical test (random Spin(8) triples generated via the construction; t invariant to fp tolerance across all three slots - the implementation of triality IS the thing under test); representation-tying correctness verified exactly on basis elements (the three projection maps related by the claimed automorphisms - exact structural test); parameter-count accounting test (the 3x reduction is real, asserted in code); norm-preservation certificate entries in B1; KV parity per house pattern; multiplication-table regression vs the existing naive omul (mode 'naive' must remain bitwise-stable through the refactor).

DEPENDS ON: 7b0.1 / A1 (scaffold - same integration pattern as F3/F6).
RELATION: octonion_attention_torch.py; F1/F3 Clifford (shared Spin machinery); simplicial mechanism (the comparison partner); C1 task 4; B1 certify; G1 registry.
notes: POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - the referee's bar, adopted as the acceptance criterion: 'Q,K,V having three slots is not automatically Spin(8) triality - the model must INSTANTIATE the structure, not gesture at it.' Concretely, beyond the form-invariance tests already specified, add the LAYER-LEVEL EQUIVARIANCE TEST: for random triality triples (g, g-prime, g-double-prime) in Spin(8), applying the three representations to the layer's three input streams must transform the layer's output covariantly per the derived equivariance law (state the law explicitly in the theory note: which representation the output lives in, for design (a) and design (b) separately - they differ). This test is what distinguishes 'true Spin(8)-equivariant attention' from decorated weight-tying; it must be in B2/certify and pass to fp tolerance before any benchmark claims. Reference for the construction: Baez, 'The Octonions' (the canonical exposition of the triality trilinear form and Spin(8) outer automorphisms) - cite and follow its conventions to keep the implementation auditable against the literature.

title: Symplectic transformer: gradient-potential coupling, shadow energy, and normalization-free training
description:
THE MATHEMATICAL IDEA: the reversible block's additive coupling y1 = x1 + F(x2); y2 = x2 + G(y1) preserves volume for ANY F, G - but not energy, because arbitrary F, G are not flows of anything. ONE CONSTRAINT CHANGES EVERYTHING: take F = grad(phi_F) and G = grad(phi_G) for scalar potential networks phi (gradients computed exactly by autograd). Then the block is EXACTLY the kick-kick (Stormer-Verlet style) symplectic integrator for the separable Hamiltonian H(x1, x2) = phi_F(x2) + phi_G(x1) - a 5-line proof (each half-step is a shear generated by a function of half the variables; shears generated by gradients are exact symplectic maps; compositions of symplectic maps are symplectic). The codebase is ONE parameterization choice away from this: the reversible demo even has a generating-function symplectic mode (REV_GENERATING) at demo level; nanochat's reversible_block_torch.py does not yet constrain F, G.
WHAT SYMPLECTICITY BUYS - BACKWARD ERROR ANALYSIS: a symplectic integrator exactly conserves a SHADOW HAMILTONIAN H-tilde = H + O(h^2) for exponentially long times. Translation to depth: a quantity epsilon-close to the learned energy is conserved across HUNDREDS of layers - activation magnitudes cannot drift, they oscillate boundedly around the shadow level set. CONSEQUENCE 1 - THE NO-NORM TRANSFORMER: LayerNorm/RMSNorm exist to fight activation drift; conservation removes the cause, not the symptom. Predict: normalization-free symplectic GPTs train stably at depths where unnormalized standard GPTs diverge - and freeing the stream from per-token normalization restores MAGNITUDE as an information channel (the surreal framework's core claim: scale carries content; norms destroy it - two frameworks converge here).
CONSEQUENCE 2 - NOETHER CHANNELS: every continuous symmetry of the potentials yields an EXACTLY conserved charge. Engineer task-aligned invariants: make phi invariant under permutations of tokens within designated segments -> the corresponding charge is a conserved MULTISET MEMORY (what is in the bag cannot be forgotten by depth, by theorem). 'Noether regularization': penalize symmetry violation of learned potentials to create SOFT conservation laws aligned with task structure - a new regularization family with a physics pedigree.
HONEST SCOPE (derive in the note, do not hide): phi takes all tokens through attention, so the Hamiltonian is token-coupled - symplecticity holds on the full token phase space (gradient of a scalar over all tokens is still a gradient; fine), but CAUSAL MASKING makes the effective phi time-dependent during incremental decoding - state precisely what is conserved during teacher-forced forward (everything claimed) vs autoregressive generation (shadow conservation per prefix; derive the correction).

WHY LEAPFROG: the field manages training stability with normalization, init lore, and warmup rituals - all empirics. A conservation-law architecture makes stability a THEOREM, removes normalization's representational tax, and opens a genuinely new design dimension (engineered conserved quantities). If the no-norm prediction holds even at moderate depth, it rewrites a default of every modern architecture.

CONCRETE PROGRAM:
1. THEORY NOTE: the kick-kick symplecticity proof; shadow-Hamiltonian statement adapted to discrete depth (cite Hairer-Lubich-Wanner precisely; state what 'exponentially long' becomes for L layers); the Noether correspondence for this architecture with the multiset-charge construction worked out; the causal-masking scope analysis.
2. IMPLEMENTATION: --reversible-mode {additive, symplectic} in reversible_block_torch.py IN PLACE: phi as scalar-output networks over the half-streams (attention inside phi_F, MLP inside phi_G, both reduced to scalars by a learned functional - design the reduction carefully and document; gradients via autograd.grad with create_graph for training); O(1)-memory inverse still holds (the inverse of a kick is the negative kick - EXACT, even nicer than additive coupling's fixed-point-free inverse).
3. EXPERIMENTS (preregister): depth ladder {8, 16, 32} x {symplectic no-norm, additive with-norm, standard with-norm, standard no-norm (the expected failure)} at equal params: trainability (loss curves), activation-norm trajectories (predict: bounded oscillation vs drift/divergence), LR sensitivity width. HEADLINE REGISTRY PREDICTION: symplectic no-norm trains to within 5% of the normed baseline at depth 16 while standard no-norm diverges or degrades > 20%. NOETHER PROTOTYPE: segment-permutation-invariant phi on a multiset-tracking diagnostic (C1 gains a small task variant - note added): verify the charge is conserved to integrator tolerance and that multiset recall beats the unconstrained baseline.
4. METRICS: shadow-energy estimate, activation-norm stats, charge values per layer into the D2 stream.

TESTS & LOGGING: SYMPLECTICITY CHECK (the load-bearing test): Jacobian J of one block satisfies J^T Omega J = Omega to fp64 tolerance on random small configs - extend the existing symplectic-check machinery (matrix-gauge demo / rev demo cycle tests) and add to B1 certify; ENERGY-DRIFT SEPARATION test: long composition (256 layers, tiny width) - symplectic mode shows bounded oscillation, additive mode shows drift; ASSERT THE SEPARATION quantitatively, both directions; Noether charge conservation exact-ish test (constructed symmetric phi -> charge drift < integrator tolerance through depth); inverse round-trip test for the kick form (exact, tighter than the additive case); gradcheck on the autograd-of-autograd path; all training runs log energy/charge panels with rich live display (the dashboard bead nyp gains its most physics-flavored panel).

RELATION: reversible_block_torch.py + reversible demo (REV_GENERATING precedent); surreal framework (magnitude-as-information convergence); B1 certify; C1 (multiset task variant); D2; nyp dashboard; G1 registry.
notes:
POLISH ROUND 2 - MATHEMATICAL PRECISION FIXES (apply before implementation):
1. SHADOW-HAMILTONIAN SCOPE: classical backward-error analysis (Hairer-Lubich-Wanner) gives exponentially-long conservation for an AUTONOMOUS integrator - the SAME map iterated. Per-layer-varying potentials phi_l make the composition nonautonomous, where the strong statement does not directly apply. The honest claim hierarchy, which the theory note must state explicitly: (i) EXACT symplecticity and volume preservation per layer - holds unconditionally, any phi_l; (ii) STRONG shadow conservation - holds under LAYER-TIED potentials (phi shared across layers, universal-transformer style - also a parameter-efficiency win) and approximately under slowly-varying phi_l (adiabatic regime; derive the drift bound in terms of ||phi_(l+1) - phi_l||); (iii) untied independent phi_l - volume preservation plus per-layer symplecticity only; norm-drift behavior becomes an EMPIRICAL question. CONSEQUENCE FOR THE EXPERIMENT MATRIX: add the weight-tied variant as a first-class arm: {symplectic-tied no-norm, symplectic-untied no-norm, additive with-norm, standard with-norm, standard no-norm} x depth ladder. The headline no-norm registry prediction should be registered separately for tied (theory-backed) and untied (empirical bet) - if untied drifts but tied does not, that is a CONFIRMATION of the theory, not a failure of the bead.
2. INVERSE-EXACTNESS CORRECTION: the description's claim that the kick inverse is 'even nicer than additive coupling's fixed-point-free inverse' is wrong - standard additive coupling already has an exact fixed-point-free inverse (x2 = y2 - G(y1); x1 = y1 - F(x2)). The kick form's advantage is SYMPLECTICITY, not invertibility. Correct the framing in the theory note; the O(1)-memory property is inherited unchanged.
3. BOOTSTRAP RULE for the Noether prototype: if C1 task 10 (multiset tracking) has not landed, build a minimal inline generator (D4 precedent) and note the duplication for C1 to absorb - do not block on vdc.1.
E2E CONTRACT: a symplectic-tied no-norm mini-run (depth 8, tiny width, CPU) joins rz8.8 Scenario 5 as part of this bead's acceptance, logging activation-norm and shadow-energy trajectories with drift assertions.

POLISH ROUND 3 (external referee pass) - COERCIVITY, a missing DESIGN requirement (not just wording): conserved energy bounds activations ONLY IF the Hamiltonian is coercive (level sets bounded: H -> infinity as ||x|| -> infinity). Unconstrained potential networks phi need not be coercive - energy could be conserved along an unbounded level set, and then 'norm drift impossible' is simply false as stated. FIX: (1) add an explicit confinement term to the potentials: phi(x) = phi_net(x) + (lambda/2)||x||^2 with lambda learnable but floored at lambda_min > 0 - this makes H coercive by construction and the bound activation-norm <= f(shadow energy, lambda_min) explicit; derive f in the theory note; (2) soften every 'drift impossible' phrasing to the theorem-shaped version: 'drift controlled by a conserved (tied case) or nearly conserved (adiabatic case) shadow Hamiltonian, under coercivity'; (3) the experiment matrix gains a lambda ablation (lambda_min in {0, small, moderate}): the lambda_min = 0 arm is the falsification control - if it also never drifts, coercivity was not the binding constraint and the theory note's account needs revisiting; if it drifts, the theory is doing real work. (4) Positioning: cite the symplectic-networks literature (SympNets and symplectically-integrated architectures for Hamiltonian systems) and state the delta: we are not learning Hamiltonian dynamics from data - we are imposing symplectic structure on a LANGUAGE-MODEL residual stream to obtain stability theorems and Noether channels; different goal, same machinery.
EOF
